- lock_id: 1c85a54d-f00a-4413-962e-52de2e7f3b6d
- step_id: 05-011
- step_title: parse-sprite-namespace
- summary: Added `src/assets/parse-sprite-namespace.ts`, a self-contained audit ledger plus runtime parser for the vanilla DOOM 1.9 sprite namespace — the contiguous block of patch-format sprite frame lumps between the `S_START` and `S_END` markers in a WAD directory that `R_InitSpriteLumps` walks to populate the `spritewidth` / `spriteoffset` / `spritetopoffset` parallel `fixed_t` arrays the renderer indexes through `lump - firstspritelump` at draw time. The 15-axis audit ledger pins every textual semantic from `r_data.c` and `r_things.c`: the `firstspritelump = W_GetNumForName("S_START") + 1` skip-marker arithmetic, the `lastspritelump = W_GetNumForName("S_END") - 1` skip-marker arithmetic, the `numspritelumps = lastspritelump - firstspritelump + 1` inclusive count, the three `Z_Malloc(numspritelumps*sizeof(*spritewidth/spriteoffset/spritetopoffset), PU_STATIC, 0)` parallel-array allocations (no trailing +1 guard slot, unlike `flattranslation` which is `numflats+1`), the three `<<FRACBITS` shifts that convert int16-LE patch header fields to vanilla `fixed_t` values, the `R_InitSpriteLumps()` placement as the third step of `R_InitData()` between `R_InitFlats` and `R_InitColormaps`, the `frame = lumpinfo[l]->name[4] - 'A'` frame letter decoding at offset 4, the `rotation = lumpinfo[l]->name[5] - '0'` rotation digit decoding at offset 5, the optional second-view encoding via `if (lumpinfo[l]->name[6]) { frame = lumpinfo[l]->name[6] - 'A'; rotation = lumpinfo[l]->name[7] - '0'; ... }` for sprites like `SARGB4B6` that ship two mirrored views in one lump, the `sprtemp[frame].lump[r] = lump - firstspritelump` zero-based sprite number formula in `R_InstallSpriteLump`, and the shareware `DOOM1.WAD` axis pinning S_START at directory index 552, S_END at directory index 1036, and exactly 483 sprite lumps in the namespace (matching `lumpCategories.sprite: 483` in `reference/manifests/wad-map-summary.json`). The 18-entry derived-invariants ledger and `crossCheckSpriteNamespaceRuntime(snapshot)` helper convert each axis into an observable runtime fact: numSpriteLumps formula equality, firstSpriteIndex/lastSpriteIndex bounds, spriteTableSlotCount equals numSpriteLumps (no trailing +1), SPRITE_FIXED_SHIFT equals FRACBITS=16, parser returns frozen namespace+entries, sprite numbers sequential 0..numSpriteLumps-1, directory indices match firstSpriteIndex+spriteNumber, parser rejects missing S_START / missing S_END / S_END before S_START, spriteNumForLumpIndex returns directoryIndex-firstSpriteIndex and throws on out-of-range, decodeSpriteFrameLetter / decodeSpriteRotationDigit map name[4]/name[5] correctly, hasSpriteSecondView requires length>=8 and name[6]!=0, and the EVERY_AUDIT_AXIS_IS_DECLARED ledger-completeness gate. Exposed runtime constants `SPRITE_FIXED_SHIFT=16`, `SPRITE_NAME_FRAME_LETTER_OFFSET=4`, `SPRITE_NAME_ROTATION_DIGIT_OFFSET=5`, `SPRITE_NAME_SECOND_VIEW_FRAME_OFFSET=6`, `SPRITE_NAME_SECOND_VIEW_ROTATION_OFFSET=7`, `SPRITE_NAME_FRAME_LETTER_BASE_CHARCODE=0x41`, `SPRITE_NAME_ROTATION_DIGIT_BASE_CHARCODE=0x30` plus the parser entry points `parseSpriteNamespace(directory)`, `spriteNumForLumpIndex(namespace, directoryIndex)`, `decodeSpriteFrameLetter(name)`, `decodeSpriteRotationDigit(name)`, `decodeSpriteSecondViewFrameLetter(name)`, `decodeSpriteSecondViewRotationDigit(name)`, `hasSpriteSecondView(name)`, `spritePatchHeaderToFixed(raw)` (which mirrors the upstream `<<FRACBITS` shift on width/leftoffset/topoffset and preserves int16 sign on negative offsets like CHGGA0's -104/-117). The `SHAREWARE_DOOM1_WAD_SPRITE_NAMESPACE_ORACLE` pins six named sprites covering both frame-form variants and full namespace coverage: CHGGA0 (sprite 0, the very first content sprite, exercises the negative leftoffset/topoffset = -104/-117 fixed-point shift with sign preservation), SARGB4B6 (sprite 99, the second-view encoding example with frame B rotation 4 / frame B rotation 6 mirrored), TROOA1 (sprite 149, the Imp/Trooper rotation 1 single view), BOSSH4 (sprite 249, mid-namespace stride coverage), PLAYA1 (sprite 272, the player sprite), and TREDD0 (sprite 482, the very last content sprite — TREDD0 = "tree death" frame D rotation 0). Each pinned sprite captures fileOffset, size, sha256 fingerprint, and the int16-LE patch header fields (width, leftoffset, topoffset). The `crossCheckShareWareDoom1WadSpriteNamespaceSample` helper enforces every oracle scalar field plus per-sprite directory placement, fileOffset, size, sha256, and patch header values. Added focused test `test/vanilla_parity/wad/parse-sprite-namespace.test.ts` (79 tests, 2222 expects) that re-derives the live oracle sample from `doom/DOOM1.WAD` every run, exercises the parser on synthetic adjacent-marker zero-sprite / minimal-three-sprite / duplicate-marker last-marker-wins / missing-S_START / missing-S_END / S_END-before-S_START directories, exercises the spriteNumForLumpIndex helper across the live namespace and the six pinned named sprites, exercises the frame/rotation decoders on the 6-character form (TROOA1) and the 8-character second-view form (SARGB4B6) plus the second-view-detection contract on length-7 vs length-8 names, exercises the spritePatchHeaderToFixed helper on the negative-offset CHGGA0 case to prove sign preservation through the `<<FRACBITS` shift, asserts every pinned sprite's sha256 matches the live IWAD bytes byte-for-byte, asserts the `lumpCategories.sprite: 483` manifest field matches `SHAREWARE_DOOM1_WAD_SPRITE_NAMESPACE_ORACLE.numSpriteLumps`, runs the cross-check helpers against the live runtime snapshot and the live oracle sample (both report zero failures), and runs the cross-check helpers against deliberately tampered snapshots/samples to prove every audit axis and every per-field oracle scalar is enforced (16 failure-mode tests covering numSpriteLumps drift, firstSpriteIndex drift, lastSpriteIndex drift, trailing +1 slot tamper, SPRITE_FIXED_SHIFT drift to 8, parser-not-frozen tamper, sprite-numbers-not-sequential tamper, directory-index-not-firstSpriteIndex+i tamper, missing-S_START accepted tamper, missing-S_END accepted tamper, end-before-start accepted tamper, spriteNumForLumpIndex returns wrong number tamper, spriteNumForLumpIndex fails to throw on out-of-range tamper, decodeSpriteFrameLetter mis-decodes offset-4 tamper, decodeSpriteRotationDigit mis-decodes offset-5 tamper, hasSpriteSecondView misclassifies offset-6 tamper, plus per-oracle-field tampers on numSpriteLumps / startMarkerIndex / missing pinned sprite / wrong patchLeftoffset / wrong sha256). Marked 05-011 complete in `plan_vanilla_parity/MASTER_CHECKLIST.md`. Did not flip the `05-011` step file heading from `# [ ]` to `# [x]` per the established wad-lane practice (the prior 01-010/03-008/03-009/05-007 step-file heading flips are pre-existing plan-validator failures outside this lane).

- open_risks: The audit module is a static ledger plus self-contained parser — it does NOT change the runtime sprite-frame cache (which lands in step 05-024 build-sprite-frame-cache). The runtime parallel arrays `spritewidth` / `spriteoffset` / `spritetopoffset` are documented at the audit-ledger level (axis IDs `sprite-namespace-spritewidth-allocation-numspritelumps`, etc.) and cross-checked via the `spriteTableSlotCount === numSpriteLumps` invariant, but this audit does NOT instantiate the three arrays from live patch headers — that is the responsibility of the future step 05-024 which must respect this audit's `<<FRACBITS` shift and the `lump - firstspritelump` lookup formula. The `spritePatchHeaderToFixed` helper exposed by this module is the canonical conversion that future step should use (it preserves int16 sign on negative offsets via JavaScript's signed left shift on values that fit within 16 bits — confirmed by the CHGGA0 -104/-117 test case). The sprite name format in vanilla DOOM 1.9 follows the convention 4-char prefix + 1-char frame letter + 1-char rotation digit + optional 4th-char + 5th-char for second view (e.g. SARGB4B6 = SARG frame B rotation 4 / frame B rotation 6 mirrored); shareware DOOM1.WAD has 61 unique 4-character prefixes across the 483 lumps. Inner sub-markers like `S1_START` / `S1_END` do NOT exist in vanilla DOOM 1.9's sprite namespace (unlike `F1_START` / `F1_END` in the flat namespace), so the parser does not flag any entry as `isInnerMarker` — every directory entry between S_START and S_END is a content sprite lump. A future step that supports a registered DOOM IWAD with custom sprite extensions or a PWAD that overrides sprites via the `start = firstspritelump-1; end = lastspritelump+1;` scan range in `R_InitSprites` must NOT alter the `parseSpriteNamespace` namespace bounds (vanilla scans the directory range, not the marker-bounded range, when matching against `info[i].sprite` names — but this is a downstream concern handled by the sprite-frame cache step). The cross-checker is exact-equality on every probe field; future audits that extend the probe set must add new probes without mutating prior expectations (the audit ledger is intentionally append-only). The recurring parallel-lane working-tree race documented in prior 01-008..01-016 / 03-008..03-014 / 04-005..04-009 / 05-005..05-010 open_risks is still active: the fifteen `bun test` failures are caused by parallel-lane committed state, parallel-lane untracked state (including this step's net-zero contribution to inventory drift since the inventory snapshot at `plan_vanilla_parity/current-state/inventory-wad-and-asset-modules.json` predates 05-005..05-011), and prior step-file heading flips. The mitigation pattern remains: (a) document the failures in `tests_run` as expected and outside this lane's write lock, (b) commit only this lane's own write-locked files plus plan-control updates, (c) await the inventory lane's snapshot regeneration as 01-005/01-006 are revisited and a future governance step un-flips the 01-010/03-008/03-009/05-007 step-file headings. Lane lock `1c85a54d-f00a-4413-962e-52de2e7f3b6d` is held by the launcher and will be released on normal exit. No proprietary assets were modified or staged; `doom/`, `iwad/`, and `reference/` were strictly read-only.

- summary: Pinned the vanilla DOOM 1.9 byte-level lock contract for the internal 320x200 8-bit indexed framebuffer at `src/bootstrap/lock-internal-320-by-200-indexed-framebuffer.ts`. Where step 03-012 audited the broad framebuffer contract (dimensions, palette, aspect-correction, lifecycle, Chocolate-only-omissions), step 03-014 narrows to the runtime-locked byte-level layout that any vanilla-parity Win32 framebuffer must hold against drift. The audit module declares thirty-two lock clauses against `linuxdoom-1.10/i_video.c` (lines 693-732) and `linuxdoom-1.10/v_video.c` (lines 358-368, 153-169) covering: the 320 column count and 200 row count locked at SCREENWIDTH/SCREENHEIGHT (verbatim macros from doomdef.h), the 1-byte-per-pixel / 8-bit element type locked by the verbatim `(unsigned char *)` cast and the `XShmCreateImage(X_display, X_visual, 8, ZPixmap, ...)` / `XCreateImage(X_display, X_visual, 8, ZPixmap, ...)` depth=8 arguments, the row stride = SCREENWIDTH locked by the verbatim `dest += SCREENWIDTH;` row-walk in V_DrawPatch, the 64000-byte total per slot pinned by `malloc (SCREENWIDTH * SCREENHEIGHT)` and `screens[i] = base + i*SCREENWIDTH*SCREENHEIGHT`, the V_Init contiguous 256000-byte allocation `I_AllocLow (SCREENWIDTH*SCREENHEIGHT*4)` sliced into four screens via `for (i=0 ; i<4 ; i++) screens[i] = base + i*SCREENWIDTH*SCREENHEIGHT;`, the four-slot count, the visible framebuffer at index 0 with top-left pixel at offset 0 and bottom-right pixel at offset 63999, the row-major scanline byte ordering with `y * SCREENWIDTH + x` formula and top-down (NOT bottom-up BMP-style) storage, the no-row-padding / no-row-alignment guarantee with `bitmap_pad = 8` (one byte) and `bytes_per_line = X_width = SCREENWIDTH` for the non-SHM XCreateImage path, the `(unsigned char *)` element type and `byte` typedef from doomtype.h with no signed-char drift, the direct alias `screens[0] = (unsigned char *) (image->data)` on multiply==1 with NO per-frame memcpy, the absence of any framebuffer header / metadata prefix / alpha channel, and the absence of 16-bit (e.g. 5-6-5 packed RGB) or 32-bit (e.g. ARGB intermediate) element types. Twelve derived invariants are witnessed by thirty-seven pinned probes that exercise thirty query kinds (framebuffer-width / -height / -bytes-per-pixel / -bits-per-pixel / -row-stride / -total-bytes-per-slot, V_Init contiguous-allocation / screens-slot-count / visible-framebuffer-index, top-left/bottom-right pixel byte offset, pixel-byte-offset-for-coordinate, screens-slot-base-byte-offset, X11 image-depth / format-symbol / non-shm-bytes-per-line / non-shm-bitmap-pad, framebuffer-c-element-type / byte-typedef, V_Init alloc symbol, I_InitGraphics alias C symbol, plus nine vanilla-* boolean negative-fact queries). ## 2026-04-28 - 04-009 implement-game-tic-counter-ownership

- status: completed
- agent: Claude Code
- model: claude-opus-4-7
- effort: max
- lane: core
- lock_id: d9d4409e-4dc5-48da-b3f1-656b3b85e6fe
- step_id: 04-009
- step_title: implement-game-tic-counter-ownership
- summary: Pinned the vanilla DOOM 1.9 game tic counter ownership contract — the precise semantics of `gametic`, the file-scope `int` counter declared in `linuxdoom-1.10/g_game.c` that drives every per-tic decision in the engine (monster `(gametic & 3)` scheduling, menu mouseWait/joyWait deadlines, `levelstarttic = gametic;` capture in G_DoLoadLevel, demo timing, consistency-check buf index `(gametic/ticdup)%BACKUPTICS`) — as a self-contained audit ledger at `src/core/implement-game-tic-counter-ownership.ts`. The audit module declares twelve C-source facts (three c-header file-scope declarations: the verbatim `int             gametic;` declaration in g_game.c with no explicit initialiser, the C-default zero-initialisation contract for static-storage objects, the verbatim `int             levelstarttic;          // gametic at level start` declaration immediately following gametic with the comment that documents the absolute-clock-feeds-level-start direction; nine c-body facts: the `M_Ticker (); G_Ticker (); gametic++; maketic++;` statement form in d_main.c D_DoomLoop singletics branch, the `M_Ticker (); G_Ticker (); gametic++;` form in d_net.c TryRunTics inner `for (i=0 ; i<ticdup ; i++)` loop, the canonical `gametic++;` post-increment statement form NOT `++gametic;` and NOT `gametic = gametic + 1;`, the never-reset invariant pinning that no `gametic = N;` statement appears anywhere in the linuxdoom-1.10 source tree, the G_InitNew non-mutation invariant pinning that the new-game init function calls M_ClearRandom but does NOT touch gametic, the G_DoLoadGame and G_DoSaveGame non-mutation invariants pinning that save/load operates on leveltime not gametic, the verbatim `levelstarttic = gametic;        // for time calculation` capture statement in G_DoLoadLevel, the canonical four-step `advancedemo / M_Ticker / G_Ticker / gametic++` ordering at both increment sites, and the unique-pair-call-site invariant pinning exactly two physical `gametic++` locations across the entire linuxdoom-1.10 tree). The invariant ledger pins fifteen operational invariants (zero-initialisation at construction, +1 increment delta per tic, monotonic non-decreasing across the lifetime, post-Ticker post-increment so the value observed during the K-th tic ticker is K-1, after-N-tics-equals-N closed sweep across N in {0,1,2,5,35,70,256,1000,2100}, no internal reset across repeated ticking, zero-indexed ticker-observation sequence [0,1,..,N-1], integer-not-fractional increments, no premature wrap up to 35*60*60=126000 tics one game hour, exact 35-tic one-second probe, exact 70-tic two-second probe, no byte-boundary wrap at 256 tics, two-instance independence, K-1 ticker-observation across K in {1,2,3,36,100,1000}, ticker-callback parameter agrees with instance gametic getter inside the callback). Sixteen curated probes target gametic post-state from canonical pre-states (fresh instance to 0, one tic to 1, two tics to 2, five tics to 5, thirty-five tics to 35, seventy tics to 70, hundred tics to 100, two-hundred-fifty-six tics to 256, thousand tics to 1000, twenty-one-hundred tics to 2100, one-game-minute alias, one-game-hour to 126000) plus during-ticker observation probes (first-tic to 0, second-tic to 1, thirty-sixth-tic to 35, thousandth-tic to 999). Exposed top-level constants `AUDITED_GAMETIC_INITIAL_VALUE=0`, `AUDITED_GAMETIC_INCREMENT_DELTA=1`, `AUDITED_GAMETIC_RESET_STATEMENT_COUNT_LINUXDOOM=0`, `AUDITED_GAMETIC_INCREMENT_SITE_COUNT_LINUXDOOM=2`, `AUDITED_GAMETIC_DURING_FIRST_TICKER_VALUE=0`, `AUDITED_GAMETIC_AFTER_FIRST_TICK_VALUE=1`, `AUDITED_GAMETIC_AFTER_ONE_SECOND_VALUE=35`, `AUDITED_GAMETIC_AFTER_256_TICS_VALUE=256`, `AUDITED_GAMETIC_DECLARATION_FILE='linuxdoom-1.10/g_game.c'`, `AUDITED_GAMETIC_INCREMENT_STATEMENT='gametic++;'`, plus the `crossCheckDoomGameTicCounter(candidate)` helper that returns the failure-id list (empty list = parity-safe) against any candidate exposing a `gametic` getter and a `runOneTic(tickerCallback)` operation. Authority order is per `plan_vanilla_parity/SOURCE_CATALOG.md` but the gametic contract is pinned against authority 3 (id Software `linuxdoom-1.10/g_game.c` is the verbatim source for the file-scope declaration, `linuxdoom-1.10/d_main.c` is the verbatim source for the singletics increment site, `linuxdoom-1.10/d_net.c` is the verbatim source for the TryRunTics increment site — all three are textual constants the binary cannot disagree with). The audit module deliberately does NOT import from `src/bootstrap/tryRunTics.ts` (or any runtime tic-runner module), keeping the audit isolated so a corruption of either side surfaces in the focused test instead of silently calibrating to a wrong reference. - open_risks: The audit module is a static ledger plus reference handler — it does NOT change the runtime `src/bootstrap/tryRunTics.ts` and does NOT add a new gametic accessor. The existing TicRunner runtime correctly maintains a per-instance `#gametic = 0` initial value, increments with `this.#gametic++;` after `callbacks.ticker();`, and never resets gametic — all consistent with the canonical linuxdoom-1.10 contract. A future step that reads `src/bootstrap/tryRunTics.ts` under its read-only path can wire the runtime cross-check by importing TicRunner and adapting it to the `DoomGameTicCandidate` interface. A future oracle-capture follow-up step targeting `doom/DOOMD.EXE` under DOSBox should pin the exact gametic value reported by the engine after a controlled tic count so that downstream demo-sync tests (04-024..04-026) can verify the runtime stays bit-exact with the binary at the canonical tic count boundary. The cross-checker is exact-equality on every probe field and every invariant; future audits that extend the probe set must add new probes without mutating prior expectations (the audit ledger is intentionally append-only). The audit module pins the ticdup=1 single-player path semantics; the multi-player ticdup>1 path where the inner `for (i=0; i<ticdup; i++)` loop advances gametic by ticdup-per-outer-iteration is documented in the C_BODY_GAMETIC_INCREMENT_IN_TRYRUNTICS_TICDUP_LOOP fact's description but is NOT pinned as an operational invariant because the current TicRunner runtime hardcodes single-player ticdup=1; a future multiplayer step would extend the candidate interface with a `runOneOuterTic(ticdup, tickerCallback)` operation. The recurring parallel-lane working-tree race documented in prior 01-008..01-015 / 03-008..03-013 / 04-005..04-008 / 05-005..05-008 open_risks is still active: the fifteen `bun test` failures are caused by parallel-lane committed state, parallel-lane untracked state, and prior step-file heading flips. The mitigation pattern remains: (a) document the failures in `tests_run` as expected and outside this lane's write lock, (b) commit only this lane's own write-locked files plus plan-control updates, (c) await the inventory lane's snapshot regeneration and a future governance step un-flips the 01-010/03-008/03-009/05-007 step-file headings. Lane lock `d9d4409e-4dc5-48da-b3f1-656b3b85e6fe` is held by the launcher and will be released on normal exit. No proprietary assets were modified or staged; `doom/`, `iwad/`, and `reference/` were strictly read-only.

- summary: Pinned the vanilla DOOM 1.9 window-title-and-icon policy as a self-contained audit ledger plus reference handler at `src/bootstrap/apply-window-title-and-icon-policy.ts`. The audit module declares the canonical five-channel landscape (`title`, `icon-pixmap`, `icon-name`, `wm-class`, `wm-hints`) covering every window-attribute slot vanilla 1.9 leaves at the X11 window-manager default. For each channel the ledger pins one of two source strategies (`wm-default-no-explicit-set` for the X11 port, `dos-no-window-concept` for the DOS port), the X11 API symbol vanilla would have to call but does NOT (`XStoreName` / `XSetWMHints` / `XSetWMIconName` / `XSetClassHint` / `XSetWMHints`), and the corresponding Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 SDL counterpart (`SDL_WM_SetCaption` / `SDL_WM_SetIcon` for the title and icon-pixmap channels; null for the three remaining channels Chocolate also leaves at SDL defaults). The contract pins twenty-two clauses (VANILLA_X11_PORT_HAS_NO_XSTORENAME_CALL / NO_XSETWMNAME_CALL / NO_XSETWMICONNAME_CALL / NO_XSETCLASSHINT_CALL / NO_XSETWMHINTS_CALL / NO_ICON_PIXMAP / USES_BARE_XCREATEWINDOW_ATTRIBMASK with the verbatim `CWEventMask | CWColormap | CWBorderPixel` three-flag mask, the four `*_SOURCE_IS_WM_DEFAULT` clauses for title/icon/WM_CLASS, the DOS_PORT_HAS_NO_WINDOW_CONCEPT clause for VGA mode 13h fullscreen, the HAS_NO_EMBEDDED_ICON_DATA_ARRAY clause distinguishing vanilla from Chocolate's `icon.c`, the HAS_NO_TITLE_SETTER_FUNCTION clause distinguishing vanilla from Chocolate's `static char *window_title=""` storage and `I_SetWindowTitle` setter, the five OMITS clauses for `I_SetWindowTitle`/`I_InitWindowTitle`/`I_InitWindowIcon`/`SDL_WM_SetCaption`/`SDL_WM_SetIcon` Chocolate-only API calls, the OMITS_PACKAGE_STRING_SUFFIX clause distinguishing vanilla from Chocolate's autotools-generated `<title> - PACKAGE_STRING` format, the OMITS_M_STRINGJOIN_TITLE_FORMAT clause citing `m_misc.h`, the HAS_NO_TITLE_BEFORE_ICON_INIT_ORDERING_REQUIREMENT clause citing the verbatim Chocolate source comment `Windows cares about the ordering`, and the HAS_NO_RUNTIME_TITLE_OR_ICON_MUTATION clause covering per-map title updates such as `DOOM - E1M1` and per-state updates such as `DOOM (Paused)`), the canonical `[CWEventMask, CWColormap, CWBorderPixel]` three-token attribmask list, and five absent Chocolate-only API call symbols. Twenty derived invariants are witnessed by thirty pinned probes that exercise fourteen query kinds (window-title-api-call-count, window-icon-api-call-count, window-wm-class-api-call-count, channel-x11-source-strategy, channel-dos-source-strategy, channel-x11-omitted-api-symbol, landscape-channel-count, dos-port-has-window-concept, x11-port-uses-bare-xcreatewindow, x11-port-attribmask-token-count, x11-port-attribmask-includes-token, title-and-icon-policy-includes-chocolate-api-call, runtime-title-or-icon-mutation-allowed, title-before-icon-ordering-required). Exposed reference handler `REFERENCE_VANILLA_WINDOW_TITLE_AND_ICON_POLICY_HANDLER`, cross-checker `crossCheckVanillaWindowTitleAndIconPolicy`, and convenience derivation helper `deriveExpectedVanillaWindowTitleAndIconPolicyResult`. Authority order is per `plan_vanilla_parity/SOURCE_CATALOG.md` (DOS binary > IWAD > Windows oracle > id Software linuxdoom-1.10 source > Chocolate Doom 2.2.1) but the per-channel landscape is pinned against authority 4 because `linuxdoom-1.10/i_video.c` `I_InitGraphics` is the verbatim source — the function body has no preprocessor branching that could hide a conditional title or icon call (the only `XCreatePixmap` call is the 1x1 cursormask used by `createnullcursor` to hide the X cursor, and the `XCreateWindow` attribmask is statically assigned `CWEventMask | CWColormap | CWBorderPixel` with no flag accumulation pattern). The audit module deliberately avoids importing from `src/launcher/win32.ts` (which already wires a developer-convenience `LauncherWindowOptions.title` for the runtime Win32 STATIC class) so a corruption of either side cannot silently calibrate the audit's own probe table. Added focused test `test/vanilla_parity/launch/apply-window-title-and-icon-policy.test.ts` (78 tests, 587 expects) covering the contract-clause ledger shape (22 clauses, every id unique, every clause cites a real linuxdoom-1.10 source file from `i_video.c`/`d_main.c` and a canonical C symbol from `I_InitGraphics`/`I_ShutdownGraphics`/`D_DoomMain`/`D_DoomLoop`, the NO_XSTORENAME_CALL clause names XStoreName/XCreateWindow/XMapWindow, the NO_XSETWMNAME_CALL clause cites locale awareness, the NO_XSETWMICONNAME_CALL clause cites minimised state, the NO_XSETCLASSHINT_CALL clause cites WM_CLASS and argv[0], the NO_XSETWMHINTS_CALL clause cites WM_HINTS, the NO_ICON_PIXMAP clause distinguishes the cursormask from a real icon, the BARE_XCREATEWINDOW_ATTRIBMASK clause names all three flags plus KeyPressMask, the DOS_PORT_HAS_NO_WINDOW_CONCEPT clause cites VGA mode 13h and 320x200 fullscreen, the NO_EMBEDDED_ICON_DATA_ARRAY clause cites Chocolate's icon.c, the NO_TITLE_SETTER_FUNCTION clause - open_risks: The audit module is a static ledger plus reference handler — it does NOT change the runtime `src/launcher/win32.ts` developer-convenience title (`DOOM Codex - <mapName>` via `LauncherWindowOptions.title`). A future implementation step (or an oracle follow-up that observes `doom/DOOMD.EXE` directly under DOSBox or `doom/DOOM.EXE` under Windows) must decide how the runtime maps the vanilla "no explicit title or icon" contract onto a Win32 host that REQUIRES a window-class name to call `CreateWindowExW`. The current developer-convenience title is NOT vanilla parity but is also not user-facing in the eventual `bun run doom.ts` fullscreen mode — a downstream step (likely 03-031 implement-clean-launch-to-title-loop or 03-035 implement-launch-smoke-test-against-doom-ts) will need to either suppress the title in fullscreen or reconcile its presence with the vanilla 1.9 contract. The DOS binary semantics (whether DOSBox / DOSBox-X surfaces a window title from the `doom/DOOMD.EXE` PE/MZ header, whether `DOOM.EXE` for Windows sets a title via `RegisterClass`/`CreateWindowEx` directly) are NOT pinned by this audit because they are not visible in the linuxdoom-1.10 source we treat as authority 4 — a future oracle-capture step targeting `doom/DOOM.EXE` under Windows should pin whether the Windows port (which is a different binary lineage from linuxdoom-1.10) sets a window title or icon. The cross-checker is exact-equality on every probe field; future audits that extend the probe set must add new probes without mutating prior expectations (the audit ledger is intentionally append-only). The recurring parallel-lane working-tree race documented in prior 01-008 / 01-009 / 01-010 / 01-011 / 01-012 / 01-013 / 01-014 / 03-008 / 03-009 / 03-010 / 03-011 / 03-012 / 04-005 / 04-006 / 04-007 / 04-008 / 05-005 / 05-006 / 05-007 / 03-006 / 03-007 open_risks is still active: the fifteen `bun test` failures (3 WAD-asset inventory + 1 src-playable inventory + 1 core-math inventory + 4 step-file-heading/plan-validation + 4 isolation probe + 2 cataloged-file failures) are caused by parallel-lane committed state, parallel-lane untracked state, and prior step-file heading flips that the prior inventory snapshots and validator pre-date. The mitigation pattern remains: (a) document the failures in `tests_run` as expected and outside this lane's write lock, (b) commit only this lane's own write-locked files plus plan-control updates, (c) await the inventory lane's snapshot regeneration as 01-005/01-006 are revisited and a future governance step un-flips the 01-010/03-008/03-009/05-007 step-file headings. Lane lock `4732d988-0cd1-463b-964b-611af0b37b73` is held by the launcher and will be released on normal exit. No proprietary assets were modified or staged; `doom/`, `iwad/`, and `reference/` were strictly read-only.

## 2026-04-28 - 04-008 implement-deterministic-reset-seed

- status: completed
- agent: Claude Code

- step_title: implement-deterministic-reset-seed
- summary: Pinned the vanilla DOOM 1.9 deterministic reset-seed contract — the precise semantics of `M_ClearRandom()` (`rndindex = prndindex = 0;` chained-assignment body, `void` return type, no time seed in vanilla 1.9) and the canonical call site (one and only — inside `G_InitNew()` in `linuxdoom-1.10/g_game.c`, after the map-bounds validation `if (map < 1) map = 1;` and `if ((map > 9) && (gamemode != commercial)) map = 9;` and BEFORE the `if (skill == sk_nightmare || respawnparm)` skill/respawn branch) — as a self-contained audit ledger at `src/core/implement-deterministic-reset-seed.ts`. The audit module declares ten C-source facts (three c-header file-scope declarations: `void M_ClearRandom (void);` prototype in m_random.h, `int rndindex = 0;` initialiser matching the reset post-state, `int prndindex = 0;` initialiser matching the reset post-state; seven c-body facts: the chained-assignment reset body that brings BOTH indices to zero in a single observable transition, the no-time-seed-in-vanilla constraint that explicitly excludes the post-vanilla Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 `prndindex = 0; rndindex = time(NULL) & 0xff;` deviation, the `void M_ClearRandom (void)` return-type signature, the `M_ClearRandom ();` call inside `G_InitNew()` after map validation, the unique-call-site invariant pinning exactly one call site across the entire id Software linuxdoom-1.10 source tree (with `d_main.c` calling `G_InitNew` conditionally on `autostart || netgame`, but no second `M_ClearRandom` invocation in `d_main.c`/`f_finale.c`/`wi_stuff.c`/`p_setup.c`), the table-preservation invariant that `M_ClearRandom` reads and writes only the two index counters and never the rndtable bytes, and the idempotence invariant that calling `M_ClearRandom` N times in succession is observationally indistinguishable from one call). The invariant ledger pins sixteen operational invariants (post-reset prndindex equals 0, post-reset rndindex equals 0 vanilla 1.9 lineage NOT Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 time-seeded, both indices reset together so the sum is 0 iff both are 0, first pRandom after reset returns rndtable[1] = 8, first mRandom after reset returns rndtable[1] = 8, first pSubRandom after reset returns -101 = 8 - 109, idempotence checked at the index post-state AND the next-roll values for both streams, fresh-instance-equivalence pinning that a freshly-constructed instance is bit-equivalent to one that has just had clearRandom called on it, partial-P-cycle restoration across K in {1, 5, 17, 100, 200, 255}, partial-M-cycle restoration across K in {1, 3, 42, 100, 200, 255}, full-256-call-cycle restoration that confirms the reset is unconditional regardless of wrap state, mixed-PM-use restoration after 50 P + 70 M + 30 P, prior-history-independence pinning that the post-reset prndindex is exactly 0 across K in {0, 1, 2, 3, 17, 100, 200, 255}, table-preservation that the rndtable bytes are byte-identical before and after any sequence of clearRandom calls, time-seed-rejection where two rapid back-to-back clearRandom calls produce identical mRandom values, and the void-return-type invariant). Sixteen curated probes target reset post-states from canonical pre-states (fresh instance to P/M, single-P-then-reset, single-M-then-reset, cross-stream defence with single-P-then-mRandom and single-M-then-pRandom, full-P-cycle reset, full-M-cycle reset, mixed-P-then-M reset, reset-then-pSubRandom returns -101, double-reset idempotence, direct prndindex/rndindex post-state probes after 100 P/M calls, mid-cycle double-reset, post-pSubRandom reset that confirms pSubRandom-driven advances unwind exactly like P_Random advances, and a both-indices-sum-zero probe). Exposed top-level constants `AUDITED_RESET_PRNDINDEX_VALUE=0`, `AUDITED_RESET_RNDINDEX_VALUE=0`, `AUDITED_RESET_FIRST_P_RANDOM_VALUE=8`, `AUDITED_RESET_FIRST_M_RANDOM_VALUE=8`, `AUDITED_RESET_FIRST_P_SUB_RANDOM_VALUE=-101`, `AUDITED_RESET_CALL_SITE_FUNCTION='G_InitNew'`, `AUDITED_RESET_CALL_SITE_FILE='linuxdoom-1.10/g_game.c'`, `AUDITED_RESET_CALL_SITE_COUNT=1`, plus the `crossCheckDoomResetSeed(candidate)` helper that returns the failure-id list (empty list = parity-safe). Authority order is per `plan_vanilla_parity/SOURCE_CATALOG.md` but the reset-seed contract is pinned against authority 3 (id Software `linuxdoom-1.10/m_random.c` is the verbatim source for the function body and `linuxdoom-1.10/g_game.c` is the verbatim source for the call site — both are textual constants the binary cannot disagree with). The audit module deliberately does NOT import from `src/core/rng.ts` so a corruption of either side surfaces in the focused test instead of silently calibrating to a wrong reference. Added focused test `test/vanilla_parity/core/implement-deterministic-reset-seed.test.ts` (66 tests, 457 expects) covering audited canonical constants (post-reset values, first-roll values, call-site function/file/count), fact ledger shape (10 facts, 3 c-header + 7 c-body, every id unique, every fact references either m_random.c or g_game.c, exactly two facts reference g_game.c, non-empty descriptions and cReferences), fact ledger values (each of the ten facts is verified by string match on its cReference and key description fragments), invariant ledger shape (16 invariants, every id unique, full coverage of the canonical set, non-empty descriptions), probe ledger shape (16 probes, every id unique, every probe target represented), runtime invariants — DoomRandom.clearRandom() post-state (prndindex/rndindex zero across multiple K values, simultaneous reset, first pRandom/mRandom/pSubRandom post-reset values, void return), runtime invariants — idempotence and fresh-equivalence (two-reset and five-reset post-state matches one-reset, fresh instance equals cleared-immediately instance, undefined return), runtime invariants — full-cycle and table preservation (256-call P/M cycle reset, RNG_TABLE bytes unchanged across multi-reset stress, two rapid resets produce identical mRandom values), runtime probe values (each of the sixteen probes matches the live DoomRandom output), `crossCheckDoomResetSeed` reports zero failures on the live runtime, fourteen failure-mode tests covering tampered candidates (clearRandom only resets prndindex — Chocolate-Doom-style time-seeded variant signature, clearRandom seeds rndindex from a fixed non-zero value 0x42 simulating time(NULL) & 0xff, clearRandom only resets rndindex — gameplay-stream desync, clearRandom is a no-op, clearRandom resets to non-zero constant 1, clearRandom mutates the rndtable on every call — table-preservation tamper, clearRandom is non-idempotent counter-driven, clearRandom is conditioned on prior wrap state breaking fresh-equivalence, clearRandom returns 42 — signature deviation, pSubRandom returns +101 — subtraction-order swap, clearRandom is non-deterministic across rapid calls — alternating phase tamper, clearRandom post-state depends on prior history via parity, clearRandom does not undo pSubRandom-driven advances, clearRandom only restores fresh state for fresh inputs — mid-cycle desync), and the step file declarations (lane, write lock, research sources, prerequisite). Marked 04-008 complete in `plan_vanilla_parity/MASTER_CHECKLIST.md`. Did not flip the `04-008` step file heading from `# [ ]` to `# [x]` because the plan validator at `plan_vanilla_parity/validate-plan.ts:380-384` requires `# [ ] STEP <id>: <title>` (the prior 01-010/03-008/03-009/05-007 step-file heading flips are pre-existing plan-validator failures outside this lane).

- recovery_edit: One investigation-only WebFetch sequence used to verify the upstream sources before authoring the audit. Three WebFetch calls against id Software DOOM raw GitHub URLs (linuxdoom-1.10/m_random.c, linuxdoom-1.10/g_game.c, linuxdoom-1.10/d_main.c) confirmed the M_ClearRandom body is `rndindex = prndindex = 0;`, the one and only call site is inside G_InitNew in g_game.c after map validation and before the skill/respawnparm branch, and d_main.c does NOT call M_ClearRandom directly — it conditionally calls G_InitNew on autostart || netgame. Two additional WebFetch calls confirmed (a) tables.c and m_fixed.c contain no RNG references (the listed research sources are boilerplate from the step generator and don't actually contribute to this audit), (b) Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 src/doom/m_random.c body is `prndindex = 0; rndindex = time(NULL) & 0xff;` confirming the post-vanilla deviation that this audit explicitly excludes. Two minor in-place edits to the source module and test file after the initial focused-test run revealed two failures: (1) the cross-checker's idempotence invariant was strengthened to compare BOTH the index post-state AND the next pRandom/mRandom values across one-vs-two resets so it detects a counter-driven non-idempotent clearRandom that only mutates rndindex; (2) the test-side shared-state candidate wrapper was modified so the wrapped clearRandom propagates the impl's return value, which was previously discarded by the IIFE wrapper, allowing the void-return-type invariant test to detect a candidate that returns 42. No probe scripts were created or deleted; no tracked file outside the lane's write lock was modified during recovery.

- summary: Captured a current-state inventory of source-only metadata for the 31 committed JSON oracle fixtures under `test/oracles/fixtures/` that Phase 02 oracle-foundation steps own. The artefact `plan_vanilla_parity/current-state/inventory-oracle-fixtures-with-pending-status.json` is source-only metadata: per-fixture filename, embedded step identifier (28 fixtures use `stepId` and 3 use the older `stepIdentifier`), step title, optional `oracleId` or `oracleIdentifier` (8 expose an OR-FPS-* identifier and 23 do not), schema version (every fixture pins `schemaVersion=1`), on-disk size in bytes, line count, SHA-256 fingerprint, derived `is_pending` boolean, the closed-enum set of pending evidence signals (`top-level-pending-key`, `status-field-pending-value`, `hash-status-pending-value`, `null-live-hash-field`, `string-token-contains-pending`), the kebab-case pending status codes used (the 18 distinct codes `contract-only`, `contract-only-not-executed-by-this-step`, `pending-clean-launch-music-event-hash`, `pending-e1m1-start-music-event-hash`, `pending-implementation`, `pending-live-capture`, `pending-live-framebuffer-capture`, `pending-live-reference-capture`, `pending-live-reference-run`, `pending-menu-navigation-music-event-hash`, `pending-reference-capture`, `pending-reference-capture-surface`, `pending-reference-execution`, `pending-reference-oracle-replay-capture-surface`, `pending-sfx-audio-hash`, `pending-unimplemented-side-by-side-surface`, `pending-unimplemented-surface`, `static-contract-pending-live-reference-capture`), and the top-level pending or deferred keys (`deferredLiveHashes`, `pendingCaptureReason`, `pendingLiveCapture`, `pendingLiveHashes`, `pendingReferenceArtifacts`). 28 of 31 fixtures are pending (waiting for live reference-binary capture, replay surface, or implementation surface) and 3 are non-pending static contracts that document current visible launch and startup state (`capture-implementation-clean-launch-expectations.json` step 02-001, `capture-reference-clean-launch.json` step 02-002, `capture-startup-sequence.json` step 02-003). The `pending_status_summary` block aggregates pending vs non-pending filename lists, step-id field distribution, oracle-id field distribution, observed pending signals, observed pending status codes, observed pending top-level keys, and the union of fixture step IDs (every fixture's stepId/stepIdentifier value uses the prior plan_fps numeric step numbering and does not map cleanly to plan_vanilla_parity Phase 02 step IDs because plan_vanilla_parity renumbered Phase 02 with a different ordering). The `fixture_step_id_origin` block pins the current owning step file `plan_vanilla_parity/steps/02-034-replace-pending-oracle-fixtures-with-live-evidence.md` and the gate `plan_vanilla_parity/steps/02-035-gate-oracle-foundation-without-deferred-status.md` for the live-evidence path. The `boundary_status` block records the writable workspace contract (no proprietary id Software bytes embedded — only filenames, role descriptors, and SHA-256 fingerprints). Seven implications are recorded covering pending-fixture replacement ownership, fixture step-id origin and cross-mapping, dual-field-name (`stepId`/`stepIdentifier`, `oracleId`/`oracleIdentifier`) variants, the closed-enum pending vocabulary that downstream Phase 02 capture steps must drain, the five distinct top-level pending or deferred keys, the three non-pending static-contract fixtures pinning launch-surface SHA-256 fingerprints, and the no-proprietary-bytes boundary anchored by `reference/policy.ts` and `reference/manifests/file-hashes.json`. The `follow_up_steps` array references twenty-two downstream Phase 02 steps (02-005, 02-006, 02-016..02-035). Added focused test `test/vanilla_parity/current-state/inventory-oracle-fixtures-with-pending-status.test.ts` (20 tests, 757 expects) covering inventory artefact existence, canonical id/title/lane fields, the nine canonical required top-level fields in canonical order, summary/evidence-method content (referencing `test/oracles/fixtures/` and Phase 02 step files), captured_at_utc UTC ISO 8601 format, repository_root absolute path, fixtures_directory enumeration with on-disk total recomputation (size_bytes, line_count, fixture_count), ASCII-sorted fixture ordering across all 31 fixtures, full per-fixture fingerprint regeneration sweep (size_bytes, line_count, sha256, schema_version, embedded stepId/stepIdentifier and oracleId/oracleIdentifier extraction), per-fixture pending classification recomputation (signals, status codes, top-level pending/deferred keys, is_pending boolean, closed-enum allowlist enforcement), pending_status_summary aggregation match (counts, names, observed signals union, status codes union, top-level keys union, step-id and oracle-id field distributions, fixture-step-id sets), fixture_step_id_origin pin of the current plan replace-pending step (02-034) and gate step (02-035) with on-disk file existence checks and Phase 02 step file count, boundary_status writable-workspace and source-only contract, implications cross-references (fixtures directory, owning step IDs, dual-field naming, schemaVersion=1), follow_up_steps pointer-to-real-step-file enforcement (including 02-034 and 02-035), step file write-lock declaration, and four failure-mode tests (a tampered fixture flipping non-pending to pending, a fabricated zero-sha256, a tampered pending status summary dropping a pending filename, and a fabricated pending signal not in the closed-enum set). ## 2026-04-28 - 03-011 implement-fatal-error-shutdown-ordering

- status: completed

- summary: Pinned the vanilla DOOM 1.9 fatal-error shutdown-ordering landscape as a self-contained audit ledger plus reference handler at `src/bootstrap/implement-fatal-error-shutdown-ordering.ts`. The audit module declares the canonical thirteen-phase init-teardown landscape covering the twelve `D_DoomMain` X_Init phases (V_Init → M_LoadDefaults → Z_Init → W_Init → M_Init → R_Init → P_Init → I_Init → D_CheckNetGame → S_Init → HU_Init → ST_Init) plus the thirteenth `I_InitGraphics` phase initialised by `D_DoomLoop`. For each phase the ledger pins one of seven teardown strategies (`explicit-shutdown-call`, `os-reclaims-heap`, `os-reclaims-file-handles`, `os-reclaims-interrupt-vector-and-sound-device`, `os-reclaims-audio-handle`, `static-memory-implicit-reclaim`, `defaults-not-saved-on-error-path`), the verbatim explicit shutdown C symbol (only `D_QuitNetGame` for `D_CheckNetGame` and `I_ShutdownGraphics` for `I_InitGraphics`), and the corresponding Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 shutdown call (e.g. `S_Shutdown` for `S_Init`, `I_ShutdownTimer` for `I_Init`) that vanilla 1.9 omits. The contract pins twenty-eight clauses (FATAL_PATH_HAS_THREE_EXPLICIT_SHUTDOWN_CALLS, the demorecording-gated demo cleanup, the unconditional `D_QuitNetGame`/`I_ShutdownGraphics`, the verbatim demo-then-network-then-graphics order, the absence of phase-aware destructor unwinding / reverse-init unwinding / partial-init rollback / setjmp-longjmp / I_AtExit registration stack, the per-phase OS-reclamation rationale for the eleven non-explicit phases, and the five Chocolate-only-omission clauses for `S_Shutdown`/`I_ShutdownTimer`/`I_ShutdownMusic`/`OPL_Shutdown`/`NET_Shutdown*`), the canonical three-call shutdown sequence `[G_CheckDemoStatus-gated, D_QuitNetGame, I_ShutdownGraphics]`, and seven absent Chocolate-only shutdown calls. Twenty derived invariants are witnessed by thirty-four pinned probes that exercise fourteen query kinds (fatal-explicit-shutdown-at-index, fatal-explicit-shutdown-count, phase-teardown-strategy, phase-explicit-shutdown-symbol, phase-presence-in-explicit-shutdown, landscape-total-phase-count, landscape-explicit-shutdown-phase-count, landscape-os-reclaimed-phase-count, fatal-path-ordering-varies-by-phase-of-origin, fatal-path-performs-reverse-init-unwinding, fatal-path-performs-partial-init-rollback, fatal-path-uses-setjmp-longjmp, fatal-path-has-registration-stack, fatal-path-includes-chocolate-shutdown). Exposed reference handler `REFERENCE_VANILLA_FATAL_ERROR_SHUTDOWN_ORDERING_HANDLER`, cross-checker `crossCheckVanillaFatalErrorShutdownOrdering`, and convenience derivation helper `deriveExpectedVanillaFatalErrorShutdownOrderingResult`. Authority order is per `plan_vanilla_parity/SOURCE_CATALOG.md` (DOS binary > IWAD > Windows oracle > id Software linuxdoom-1.10 source > Chocolate Doom 2.2.1) but the per-subsystem teardown landscape is pinned against authority 4 because the contract is a textual property of `i_system.c` `I_Error` and `d_main.c` `D_DoomMain`/`D_DoomLoop` (the verbatim three-call cleanup `if (demorecording) G_CheckDemoStatus(); D_QuitNetGame(); I_ShutdownGraphics(); exit(-1);` has no preprocessor branching that could hide alternate shutdown calls). The audit module deliberately avoids importing from any runtime quit/shutdown module so a corrupted runtime cannot silently calibrate the audit's own probe table. Added focused test `test/vanilla_parity/launch/implement-fatal-error-shutdown-ordering.test.ts` (72 tests, 518 expects) covering the contract-clause ledger shape (28 clauses, every id unique, every clause cites a real linuxdoom-1.10 source file and a canonical C symbol from `I_Error`/`D_DoomMain`/`D_DoomLoop`, the FATAL_PATH_HAS_THREE_EXPLICIT_SHUTDOWN_CALLS clause names every shutdown in canonical order with `exit(-1)`, the demo-cleanup clause cites `demorecording` and `M_WriteFile`, the phase-of-origin invariance clause cites both endpoint phases `V_Init`/`ST_Init` and `phase-aware`, the reverse-unwinding clause cites `03-008`/`RAII`/`reverse`, the setjmp/longjmp clause cites both C library symbols, the I_AtExit clause cites `I_AtExit`/`LIFO`/`Chocolate Doom 2.2.1`, the M_LoadDefaults clause distinguishes `I_Quit`/`I_Error`/`inconsistent`, the I_Init clause cites `INT 8`/`I_ShutdownTimer`/`DMA`, the D_CheckNetGame clause cites `D_QuitNetGame`/`NETPACKET_QUIT`, the I_InitGraphics clause cites `D_DoomLoop`/`VGA mode 13h`, every Chocolate-only-omission clause names its specific Chocolate addition, every D_DoomMain-pinned clause references d_main.c, the D_DoomLoop-pinned clause references d_main.c), the canonical thirteen-phase teardown landscape (verbatim ordering, exactly two phases with `explicit-shutdown-call` strategy named `D_CheckNetGame` and `I_InitGraphics`, exactly eleven phases without explicit shutdown, every phase name unique, M_LoadDefaults uses the distinct `defaults-not-saved-on-error-path` strategy, S_Init has Chocolate-only `S_Shutdown` counterpart, I_Init has Chocolate-only `I_ShutdownTimer` counterpart, D_CheckNetGame and I_InitGraphics share their explicit shutdown symbol with Chocolate), the canonical three-shutdown order matches `['G_CheckDemoStatus-gated', 'D_QuitNetGame', 'I_ShutdownGraphics']`, the seven Chocolate-only absent shutdown calls list matches `['S_Shutdown', 'I_ShutdownTimer', 'I_ShutdownSound', - open_risks: The audit module is a static ledger plus reference handler — it does NOT drive the runtime `bun run doom.ts` entry point. A future implementation step (or an oracle follow-up that observes `doom/DOOMD.EXE` directly) must wire the canonical three-call `I_Error` cleanup skeleton into the runtime fatal-error machinery. The current `src/bootstrap/quitFlow.ts` models the Chocolate-shaped `QuitFlow` (with `I_AtExit` registration stack, 8-handler LIFO traversal, and `runOnError` filter) and is NOT vanilla-1.9-faithful — it must be replaced or rewired by a downstream step before `bun run doom.ts` can claim parity with vanilla 1.9 fatal-error behaviour. The DOS binary fatal-error shutdown semantics (when an interrupt vector restores happens relative to `exit(-1)`, what DOS reclaims via `_exit` vs `exit`, whether the sound-device DMA channel is released before or after the formatted error message reaches the text-mode console) are NOT pinned by this audit because they are not visible in the linuxdoom-1.10 source we treat as authority 4 — a future oracle-capture step targeting `doom/DOOMD.EXE` under DOSBox should pin the exact ordering of OS-reclamation events relative to the three explicit shutdown calls. The cross-checker is exact-equality on every probe field; future audits that extend the probe set must add new probes without mutating prior expectations (the audit ledger is intentionally append-only). The recurring parallel-lane working-tree race documented in prior 01-008 / 01-009 / 01-010 / 01-011 / 01-012 / 01-013 / 01-014 / 03-008 / 03-009 / 03-010 / 03-011 / 04-005 / 04-006 / 04-007 / 05-005 / 05-006 / 05-007 / 03-006 / 03-007 open_risks is still active: the fifteen `bun test` failures (4 WAD-asset inventory + 1 src-playable inventory + 1 core-math inventory + 4 step-file-heading/plan-validation + 4 isolation probe + 2 cataloged-file failures) plus the two new typecheck errors from untracked parallel-lane WIP files are caused by parallel-lane committed state, parallel-lane untracked state, and prior step-file heading flips that the prior inventory snapshots and validator pre-date. The mitigation pattern remains: (a) document the failures in `tests_run` as expected and outside this lane's write lock, (b) commit only this lane's own write-locked files plus plan-control updates, (c) await the inventory lane's snapshot regeneration as 01-005/01-006 are revisited and a future governance step un-flips the 01-010/03-008/03-009/05-007 step-file headings. ## 2026-04-28 - 04-007 audit-doom-random-table-and-indices

- status: completed
- agent: Claude Code
- model: claude-opus-4-7
- effort: max
- lane: core
- lock_id: d9d4409e-4dc5-48da-b3f1-656b3b85e6fe
- step_id: 04-007
- step_title: audit-doom-random-table-and-indices
- summary: Pinned the vanilla DOOM 1.9 deterministic random number lookup table and the two index counters (`prndindex`, `rndindex`) that index it as a self-contained audit ledger at `src/core/audit-doom-random-table-and-indices.ts`. The audit module declares ten C-source facts (three c-header file-scope declarations: `unsigned char rndtable[256] = { ... };`, `int rndindex = 0;`, `int prndindex = 0;`; seven c-body facts: the array-literal opening cell `rndtable[0]=0`, the second cell `rndtable[1]=8` that is the first roll after `M_ClearRandom()` thanks to the pre-increment, the closing cell `rndtable[255]=249`, the canonical two-statement bodies of `P_Random()` and `M_Random()` with their `(idx+1)&0xff` pre-increment + table lookup, the `M_ClearRandom()` body `rndindex = prndindex = 0;` that resets BOTH indices simultaneously, and the `int P_Random (void)` integer-promotion return contract). The audit explicitly pins the id Software linuxdoom-1.10 `M_ClearRandom()` semantics (both indices reset to zero), NOT the post-vanilla Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 enhancement that seeds `rndindex` from `time(NULL) & 0xff` for non-deterministic menu animation — the Chocolate enhancement is documented in the ledger as out-of-scope. The invariant ledger pins sixteen operational invariants (length 256, byte range [0,255], first cell 0, last cell 249, the unique 255 cell at index 158, the two zero cells at indices 0 and 83, sum 32986, distinct value count 166 (the table is NOT a permutation of 0..255), `P_Random()` and `M_Random()` first call after clear emits 8, full-cycle wrap returns the index to 0, stream independence (`P_Random` cannot advance `rndindex` and vice versa), `clearRandom()` resets both indices to zero, `pSubRandom()` first call after clear returns -101 (= 8 - 109), `pSubRandom()` advances `prndindex` by exactly 2, and a sha256 fingerprint of the 256-byte table buffer matches the audited `908b529108dcbcd3fe82907cd646e08b12404a893eda2165fe58dadd709a413f` hash). Sixteen curated probes target nine canonical `rndtable[]` cells (indices 0, 1, 2, 83, 100, 128, 158, 200, 255 — the first/second entries that anchor the pre-increment first-roll, both zero positions, the unique 255 position, three mid-table sentinels, and the last entry), six stream-sequence probes (first three `P_Random()` calls returning 8/109/220, first `M_Random()` call returning 8 (proves stream independence on its own), first `pSubRandom()` returning -101, the post-256-call wrap re-emitting 8, and the partial-cycle-then-clear case re-emitting 8). Exposed top-level constants `AUDITED_RNDTABLE_LENGTH=256`, `AUDITED_RNDTABLE_FIRST_ENTRY=0`, `AUDITED_RNDTABLE_SECOND_ENTRY=8`, `AUDITED_RNDTABLE_LAST_ENTRY=249`, `AUDITED_RNDTABLE_UNIQUE_255_INDEX=158`, `AUDITED_RNDTABLE_ZERO_INDICES=[0,83]`, `AUDITED_RNDTABLE_SUM=32986`, `AUDITED_RNDTABLE_DISTINCT_VALUE_COUNT=166`, `AUDITED_RNG_INDEX_INITIAL_VALUE=0`, `AUDITED_RNG_INDEX_WRAP_MASK=0xff`, `AUDITED_RNDTABLE_SHA256`, plus the `crossCheckDoomRandom(candidate)` helper that returns the failure-id list (empty list = parity-safe). Authority order is per `plan_vanilla_parity/SOURCE_CATALOG.md` but the table-and-indices contract is pinned against authority 3 (id Software `linuxdoom-1.10/m_random.c` is the verbatim source — the table literal, the two-statement function bodies, and the both-zero `M_ClearRandom` reset). The audit module deliberately does NOT import from `src/core/rng.ts` so a corruption of either side surfaces in the focused test instead of silently calibrating to a wrong reference. Added focused test `test/vanilla_parity/core/audit-doom-random-table-and-indices.test.ts` (81 tests, 1477 expects) covering audited canonical constants (length, first/second/last entries, unique 255 index, two zero indices, sum, distinct value count, initial index, wrap mask, sha256 fingerprint format and exact value), fact ledger shape (10 facts, 3 c-header + 7 c-body, every id unique, every fact references `linuxdoom-1.10/m_random.c`, non-empty descriptions and cReferences), fact ledger values (each of the ten facts is verified by string match on its cReference), invariant ledger shape plan_vanilla_parity/MASTER_CHECKLIST.md (core lane eligibility and completion state); plan_vanilla_parity/steps/04-008-implement-deterministic-reset-seed.md (immediately downstream step that implements the canonical deterministic reset seed in the runtime RNG, depending on this audit's pinned `M_ClearRandom` semantics); - open_risks: The audit module is a static ledger plus reference handler — it does NOT change the runtime `src/core/rng.ts`. The existing `clearRandom()` runtime correctly resets both indices to zero matching the id Software 1.10 canonical lineage, so no runtime change is needed for the table-and-indices audit. A future oracle-capture follow-up step targeting `doom/DOOMD.EXE` under DOSBox should pin the exact entropy of `prndindex` at canonical demo-replay anchor moments (e.g. the start of a TIC after `G_InitNew`, the index after the first hitscan attack on E1M1) so that downstream demo-sync tests (04-024..04-026) can verify the runtime stays bit-exact with the binary across long demos. The audit pins `RNDTABLE_DISTINCT_VALUE_COUNT_IS_166` even though that fact does not appear in any C source — it is a derived structural property of the canonical 256-cell literal that defends against a "fix" tamper that converts the table to a true permutation of 0..255 (a common temptation when porting). The cross-checker is exact-equality on every probe field; future audits that extend the probe set must add new probes without mutating prior expectations (the audit ledger is intentionally append-only). The recurring parallel-lane working-tree race documented in prior 01-008 / 01-009 / 01-010 / 01-011 / 01-012 / 01-013 / 01-014 / 03-008 / 03-009 / 03-010 / 04-005 / 04-006 / 05-005 / 05-006 / 05-007 / 03-006 / 03-007 open_risks is still active: the fifteen `bun test` failures (3 WAD-asset inventory + 1 src-playable inventory + 1 core-math inventory + 4 step-file-heading/plan-validation + 4 isolation probe + 2 cataloged-file failures) are caused by parallel-lane committed state, parallel-lane untracked state, and prior step-file heading flips that the prior inventory snapshots and validator pre-date. The mitigation pattern remains: (a) document the failures in `tests_run` as expected and outside this lane's write lock, (b) commit only this lane's own write-locked files plus plan-control updates, (c) await the inventory lane's snapshot regeneration as 01-005/01-006 are revisited and a future governance step un-flips the 01-010/03-008/03-009/05-007 step-file headings. Lane lock `d9d4409e-4dc5-48da-b3f1-656b3b85e6fe` is held by the launcher and will be released on normal exit. No proprietary assets were modified or staged; `doom/`, `iwad/`, and `reference/` were strictly read-only.

The artefact `plan_vanilla_parity/current-state/inventory-demo-and-replay-modules.json` is source-only metadata: per-module SHA-256 fingerprint, on-disk size and line count, exported interfaces/functions/constants/classes/types/const-enums, external imports (with duplicates preserved per the test's `externalImports` walker), src/test importer counts, role description, and a Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 `vanilla_concept_modeled` anchor. Module groups partition the surface into four role buckets: demo_lump (demoFile.ts) for the legacy standalone Doom 1.9 single-format lump parser with DEMO_VERSION_19=109/DEMO_HEADER_SIZE=13/DEMO_TIC_SIZE=4/DEMO_END_MARKER=0x80/DEMO_MAX_PLAYERS=4/DEMO_TIC_RATE=35 constants, demo_parse (demoParse.ts) for the full-spectrum vanilla/longtics/old G_DoPlayDemo header dispatch with DEMO_VANILLA_VERSION_19=109 vanilla magic, DEMO_LONG_TICS_VERSION=111 longtics magic, DEMO_OLD_FORMAT_HEADER_SIZE=7/DEMO_OLD_FORMAT_MAX_SKILL=4 pre-v1.4 branch and the DemoFormat 'longtics'|'old'|'vanilla' discriminator, demo_playback (demoPlayback.ts) for the stateful G_DoPlayDemo+G_ReadDemoTiccmd+G_CheckDemoStatus DemoPlayback streaming reader with the netdemo derivation from playersInGame[1] || netDemo || soloNet and the marker-on-next-read finish ordering, and demo_record (demoRecord.ts) for the stateful G_BeginRecording+G_WriteDemoTiccmd+G_CheckDemoStatus DemoRecorder with DEMO_RECORD_DEFAULT_MAXIMUM_SIZE=0x20000 (131072) MAXDEMOSIZE buffer cap and DEMO_RECORD_WRITE_HEADROOM=16 vanilla precheck. The `reference_manifest_status` block cross-checks `reference/manifests/vanilla-limit-summary.json` (MAXDEMOSIZE=131072, MAXPLAYERS=4, TICRATE=35), `reference/manifests/demo-lump-summary.json` (the three bundled DOOM1.WAD DEMO1/DEMO2/DEMO3 lumps), and `reference/manifests/source-catalog.json` (Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 g_game.c canonical demo source file plus the DoomWiki Demo doc id S-041). Six implications are recorded: demoParse.ts is the central parser surface (2 src importers, 4 test importers); demoPlayback.ts owns the DemoPlayback class composed by 11 src/playable/demo-replay/ importers; demoRecord.ts DEMO_RECORD_DEFAULT_MAXIMUM_SIZE=0x20000 mirrors vanilla MAXDEMOSIZE; demoFile.ts is a narrower legacy parser; demo modules accept Buffer/options/ticcmd records via injected parameters; src/demo/ shares a strict no-proprietary-bytes boundary tied to Phase 02 oracle-capture follow-ups. The `follow_up_steps` array references fifteen downstream Phase 02/04/05/10 steps (02-009, 02-028/029/030, 04-019..04-026, 04-030, 05-014, 10-002). The artefact does not embed raw DEMO1/DEMO2/DEMO3 lump bytes or any proprietary configuration data. Added focused test `test/vanilla_parity/current-state/inventory-demo-and-replay-modules.test.ts` (22 tests, 234 expects). Marked 01-014 complete in `plan_vanilla_parity/MASTER_CHECKLIST.md`. Did not flip the `01-014` step file heading from `# [ ]` to `# [x]` per the established inventory-lane practice.

The artefact `plan_vanilla_parity/current-state/inventory-save-and-config-modules.json` is source-only metadata: per-module SHA-256 fingerprint, on-disk size and line count, exported interfaces/functions/constants/classes/types/const-enums, external imports (with duplicates preserved per the test's `externalImports` walker), src/test importer counts, role description, and a Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 `vanilla_concept_modeled` anchor. Module groups partition the surface into four role buckets: save_layout (coreSerialization.ts) for the canonical SAVEGAME_*_SIZE byte-span constants (SAVEGAME_MOBJ_SIZE=154, SAVEGAME_PLAYER_SIZE=280, SAVEGAME_PSPRITE_SIZE=16, SAVEGAME_SECTOR_SIZE=14, SAVEGAME_SIDE_SIZE=10, SAVEGAME_LINE_BASE_SIZE=6, SAVEGAME_MAPTHING_SIZE=10, SAVEGAME_TICCMD_SIZE=8, SAVEGAME_ALIGNMENT=4, SAVEGAME_PLAYER_COUNT=4) and the readArchived/writeArchived players/mobjs/world primitives that walk into typed records and back, save_specials (specialSerialization.ts) for the tc_ceiling=0/tc_door=1/tc_floor=2/tc_plat=3/tc_flash=4/tc_strobe=5/tc_glow=6/tc_endspecials=7 thinker-class enumeration with SAVEGAME_SPECIAL_DOOR_SIZE=40/FLOOR_SIZE=42/FLASH_SIZE=36/STROBE_SIZE=36/GLOW_SIZE=28/REFERENCE_SIZE=4 byte spans plus the SAVEGAME_SPECIAL_ACTIVE_PLAT_COUNT=MAXPLATS=30/ACTIVE_CEILING_COUNT=MAXCEILINGS=30/BUTTON_COUNT=MAXBUTTONS=16 active-thinker caps, save_envelope (saveHeader.ts, loadgame.ts, vanillaLimits.ts) for the savegame header writer/reader (SAVEGAME_DESCRIPTION_SIZE=24, SAVEGAME_VERSION_SIZE=16, SAVEGAME_VERSION_CODE=109 'version 109' magic, SAVEGAME_LEVELTIME_SIZE=3, SAVEGAME_HEADER_SIZE=50), the G_DoLoadGame top-level header→players→world→mobjs→specials→SAVEGAME_EOF=0x1d chain, and the VANILLA_SAVEGAME_LIMIT_SIZE=0x2c000 (180224 bytes) cap with the canonical Doom section assembly, and config_loader (src/bootstrap/config.ts) for the M_LoadDefaults disjoint default.cfg+chocolate-doom.cfg precedence loader with VANILLA_CONFIG_FILENAME='default.cfg' / EXTENDED_CONFIG_FILENAME='chocolate-doom.cfg' filename constants, the ConfigValueType (float|integer|string) discriminator mirroring DEFAULT_INT/DEFAULT_STRING/DEFAULT_FLOAT, the parseConfigValue per-type parser (decimal or 0x-hex integer, double-quote-stripped string, parseFloat), the parseConfigFileContent silent-unknown-variable line parser, and the resolveVanillaConfigPath/resolveExtendedConfigPath -config and -extraconfig CommandLine override resolvers. The `reference_manifest_status` block cross-checks `reference/manifests/vanilla-limit-summary.json` (SAVEGAMESIZE=180224, SAVESTRINGSIZE=24, MAXPLAYERS=4, MAXCEILINGS=30, MAXPLATS=30, MAXBUTTONS=16, TICRATE=35) and `reference/manifests/source-catalog.json` (Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 d_main.c, doomdef.h, g_game.c, p_saveg.c canonical save+config source files plus the DoomWiki Savegame doc id S-043). Six implications are recorded: `saveHeader.ts` is the broadest export surface in the save lane (12 src/ + 15 test/ importers) — reshaping SaveGameHeader/SAVEGAME_DESCRIPTION_SIZE=24/SAVEGAME_VERSION_CODE=109/SaveGamePlayerPresence is a cross-lane breaking change; `loadgame.ts` and `vanillaLimits.ts` enforce the canonical Doom section order header→players→world→mobjs→specials→EOF on both read and write sides, and reordering breaks roundtrip parity against vanilla saves; SAVEGAME_*_SIZE byte-span constants and VANILLA_SAVEGAME_LIMIT_SIZE=0x2c000 plus VANILLA_SAVEGAME_OVERRUN_ERROR='Savegame buffer overrun' are bit-exact mirrors of vanilla p_saveg.c/g_game.c/doomdef.h on-disk layout; SAVEGAME_SPECIAL_THINKER_CLASS_* enumeration and the active-thinker caps mirror vanilla p_saveg.c P_ArchiveSpecials/P_UnArchiveSpecials class-byte-prefixed serialization; `config.ts` implements the M_LoadDefaults two-file disjoint-namespace precedence with -config/-extraconfig overrides — reshaping ConfigValueType/silent-unknown-ignore behavior is a cross-lane breaking change for Phase 03 launch and Phase 12 save lanes; src/save/ and src/bootstrap/config.ts share a strict no-proprietary-bytes boundary tied to Phase 02 oracle-capture follow-ups (02-007 capture-default-cfg-baseline, 02-008 capture-chocolate-doom-cfg-baseline, 02-015 define-save-byte-capture-format, 02-031 capture-save-load-roundtrip-oracle). The `follow_up_steps` array references thirty-two downstream Phase 02/12 steps (02-007/02-008/02-015/02-031 oracle capture, 12-001..12-028 full save/load/config implementation phase). The artefact does not embed proprietary WAD bytes, raw configuration file contents, or raw save bytes — only source metadata, vanilla constants, and Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 source-catalog anchors. - open_risks: The inventory artefact pins exact SHA-256 fingerprints, exact on-disk byte counts, exact line counts, and exact src/test importer counts for all five `src/save/` modules and `src/bootstrap/config.ts`. Any future change to a save or config module's bytes (formatter pass, semantic refactor, new export) will surface as a fingerprint mismatch in the focused test, requiring an inventory regeneration step. The `external_imports` field preserves duplicates (e.g. `loadgame.ts` lists `src/save/coreSerialization.ts`, `src/save/saveHeader.ts`, `src/save/specialSerialization.ts` twice each because there are two distinct `import` statements from each module — one for type and one for runtime values), matching the inventory test's `externalImports` walker semantics; collapsing duplicates would silently desync this inventory from the test's expectation. The `source_directories` block lists only `src/save/` (one entry) because `src/bootstrap/` already contains many modules outside this inventory's scope — listing `src/bootstrap/` would force the directory-totals test to fail since it would not match the on-disk filename list for that directory; the config module is enumerated only inside the `modules` array. The `reference_manifest_status` block cross-checks SAVEGAMESIZE, SAVESTRINGSIZE, MAXPLAYERS, MAXCEILINGS, MAXPLATS, and MAXBUTTONS from `vanilla-limit-summary.json` because those are the limits the save+config modules directly mirror; m_config.c is not in the source catalog so the config module's vanilla anchor is recorded only in the per-module `vanilla_concept_modeled` text and the `reference_manifest_status.vanilla_save_and_config_source_files` array points at d_main.c (the d_main.c entry covers the command-line plumbing that hands -config/-extraconfig override paths to M_LoadDefaults). The recurring parallel-lane working-tree race documented in prior 01-008 / 01-009 / 01-010 / 01-011 / 01-012 / 03-008 / 03-009 / 04-005 / 04-006 / 05-005 / 05-006 / 05-007 / 03-006 / 03-007 open_risks is still active: the fourteen `bun test` failures (3 WAD-asset inventory + 1 core-math inventory + 4 step-file-heading/plan-validation + 4 isolation probe + 2 cataloged-file failures) are caused by parallel-lane committed state, parallel-lane untracked state, and prior step-file heading flips that the prior inventory snapshots and validator pre-date. The mitigation pattern remains: (a) document the failures in `tests_run` as expected and outside this lane's write lock, (b) commit only this lane's own write-locked files plus plan-control updates, (c) await the inventory lane's snapshot regeneration as 01-005/01-006 are revisited and a future governance step un-flips the 01-010/03-008/03-009/05-007 step-file headings. The save_and_config_boundary_status block records that save modules accept SaveGameWorldLayout/SaveGameHeader/SaveGameMobj[]/SaveGamePlayerArchive/SaveGameSpecialArchive/VanillaSaveGameSections records and starting-byte offsets via injected parameters and the config module accepts a CommandLine instance, ConfigDefinition arrays, and a configDirectory via injected parameters; a future implementation step will need to wire G_DoSaveGame/G_DoLoadGame into the runtime `bun run doom.ts` entry point because the save modules currently exist only as serialization primitives rather than the live runtime save system. Lane lock `49f80485-207a-47cf-8dbe-efa38bf6b262` is held by the launcher and will be released on normal exit. No proprietary assets were modified or staged; `doom/`, `iwad/`, and `reference/` were strictly read-only.

## 2026-04-27 - 03-010 implement-clean-quit-and-endoom-policy

- status: completed

- step_title: implement-clean-quit-and-endoom-policy
- summary: Pinned the vanilla DOOM 1.9 clean-quit and ENDOOM policy as a self-contained audit ledger plus reference handler at `src/bootstrap/implement-clean-quit-and-endoom-policy.ts`. The audit module declares the canonical 5-step `I_Quit` sequence (`D_QuitNetGame → G_CheckDemoStatus → M_SaveDefaults → I_ShutdownGraphics → exit(0)`, all unconditional, exit code 0), the canonical 4-step `I_Error` cleanup sequence (`if (demorecording) G_CheckDemoStatus(); → D_QuitNetGame() → I_ShutdownGraphics() → exit(-1)`, with only the first cleanup gated on `demorecording`, exit code -1), the canonical 4-step `I_Error` stderr prologue (`fprintf("Error: ") → vfprintf(error,argptr) → fprintf("\n") → fflush(stderr)`), the verbatim source-literal fingerprints (stderr prefix `"Error: "`, trailing newline `"\n"`, ENDOOM lump name `"ENDOOM"`, lump size 4000 bytes, 80 columns × 25 rows, 2 bytes per cell), the structural absence of every Chocolate Doom 2.2.1-only quit-stage feature from linuxdoom-1.10 (eight items: `I_AtExit`, `lifoTraversal`, `runOnErrorFilter`, `D_Endoom`, `I_Endoom`, `S_Shutdown`, `I_ShutdownTimer`, `recursiveCallGuard`), and the explicit absence of `M_SaveDefaults` from the `I_Error` cleanup path (vanilla intentionally skips the config save on the error path because the defaults table may be in an inconsistent state when an error fires). The audit ledger pins thirty-one contract clauses (eight `I_Quit` clauses, twelve `I_Error` clauses, three linuxdoom-source-absence clauses, four ENDOOM-lump structural clauses, four Chocolate-only-omission clauses) and the derived-invariant ledger pins twenty-four invariants, witnessed by forty pinned probes that exercise every query kind (quit-op-at-index, error-cleanup-op-at-index, error-prologue-op-at-index, op-condition with quit/error-cleanup/error-prologue phase discriminators, op-presence-in-quit, op-presence-in-error-cleanup, quit-total-step-count, error-cleanup-op-count, quit-exit-code, error-exit-code, error-stderr-prefix, error-trailing-newline, endoom-lump-name, endoom-lump-size-bytes, endoom-grid-columns, endoom-grid-rows, endoom-bytes-per-cell, linuxdoom-has-feature, error-has-recursive-guard, quit-op-precedes-quit-op). Exposed reference handler `REFERENCE_VANILLA_CLEAN_QUIT_AND_ENDOOM_POLICY_HANDLER`, cross-checker `crossCheckVanillaCleanQuitAndEndoomPolicy`, and convenience derivation helper `deriveExpectedVanillaCleanQuitAndEndoomPolicyResult`. Authority order is per `plan_vanilla_parity/SOURCE_CATALOG.md` (DOS binary > IWAD > Windows oracle > linuxdoom-1.10 source > Chocolate Doom 2.2.1) but the cleanup contract is pinned against authority 4 (id Software `linuxdoom-1.10/i_system.c` `I_Quit()` and `I_Error()` are the verbatim source of the 5-step / 4-step cleanup sequences, the demorecording gate around `G_CheckDemoStatus` in `I_Error`, the `"Error: "` prefix and `"\n"` trailing newline literals, the `exit(0)` and `exit(-1)` exit codes, and the structural absence of `D_Endoom`/`I_Endoom`/`I_AtExit`); ENDOOM lump structural facts are pinned against authority 2 (the IWAD `DOOM1.WAD`) and cross-validated against the existing `src/ui/endoom.ts` parser constants without importing from that runtime file (the audit module deliberately avoids importing from any runtime quit/ENDOOM module so a corrupted runtime cannot silently calibrate the audit's own probe table). Added focused test `test/vanilla_parity/launch/implement-clean-quit-and-endoom-policy.test.ts` (109 tests, 553 expects) covering ledger shape (31 contract clauses, 24 derived invariants, 40 probes — every id unique, every clause cites a real linuxdoom-1.10 source file or the IWAD, every IWAD clause is pinned against `DOOM1.WAD`, every Chocolate-omission clause names its specific Chocolate addition), the canonical 5-step `I_Quit` order (verbatim sequence, 0-based indices, four cleanup steps + one termination step, every cleanup step unconditional), the canonical 4-step `I_Error` cleanup order (only `G_CheckDemoStatus` gated on `demorecording`, `M_SaveDefaults` absent from the cleanup, three cleanup steps + one termination step), the canonical 4-step `I_Error` prologue order (every prologue step unconditional with `error-prologue` phase), the eight Chocolate-only quit-stage features absence list, top-level constants pin `I_Quit` exit code at 0, `I_Error` exit code at -1, stderr prefix `"Error: "` (length 7), trailing newline `"\n"` (length 1), ENDOOM lump name `"ENDOOM"`, lump size 4000 bytes, grid 80×25, bytes-per-cell 2, total cell count 2000 (cross-validates the byte-product invariant `cells × bytes-per-cell == lump size`), every probe declares an expected answer field consistent with its query kind, every witness invariant id refers to a declared derived invariant, the reference handler passes every probe with zero failures, the reference handler answers every query kind correctly, twenty failure-mode tests confirming the cross-checker detects (a) `M_SaveDefaults` placed before `D_QuitNetGame` in `I_Quit` (Chocolate-style early save), (b) `M_SaveDefaults` gated on a flag (Chocolate-style), (c) `S_Shutdown` included in the quit sequence, (d) `I_ShutdownTimer` included in the quit sequence, (e) `D_Endoom` reported as present in linuxdoom-1.10, (f) `I_AtExit` stack reported as present in linuxdoom-1.10, (g) recursive-call guard inside `I_Error`, (h) `G_CheckDemoStatus` fired unconditionally in `I_Error`, (i) `M_SaveDefaults` included in the `I_Error` cleanup, (j) wrong `I_Quit` exit code (1 instead of 0), (k) wrong `I_Error` exit code (1 instead of -1), (l) different stderr prefix (lowercase "error: "), (m) wrong ENDOOM lump size (4096 instead of 4000), (n) wrong ENDOOM grid (40×25 CGA instead of 80×25 VGA), (o) `D_QuitNetGame` reported as following `I_ShutdownGraphics` in `I_Quit` (reversed precedence), (p) wrong ENDOOM lump name (`ENDOOM2`), (q) dropped trailing newline, (r) wrong cleanup op count (4 instead of 3), (s) wrong total quit step count (8 instead of 5, Chocolate-style); the deriveExpected helper returns the canonical answer for every pinned probe and answers null for out-of-range index/unrecognised-op queries, false for absent-op precedence queries, and true for a non-Chocolate-only `linuxdoom-has-feature` query (e.g., `D_QuitNetGame`); verifies the canonical `I_Quit` order strictly orders `D_QuitNetGame` before `I_ShutdownGraphics`, `M_SaveDefaults` before `I_ShutdownGraphics`, `I_ShutdownGraphics` before `exit-zero`, and the canonical `I_Error` cleanup order strictly orders `I_ShutdownGraphics` before `exit-minus-one`; verifies the eight Chocolate-only features are absent from the canonical mechanisms; preserves the ENDOOM byte-product invariant across the dimensional constants; verifies the step file declares the launch lane and write lock, lists d_main.c/g_game.c/i_timer.c/i_video.c/m_menu.c as research sources, and declares the prerequisite gate - open_risks: The audit module is a static ledger plus reference handler — it does NOT drive the runtime `bun run doom.ts` entry point. A future implementation step (or an oracle follow-up that observes `doom/DOOMD.EXE` directly) must wire the canonical 5-step `I_Quit` and 4-step `I_Error` skeletons into the runtime quit machinery. The current `src/bootstrap/quitFlow.ts` models the Chocolate-shaped `QuitFlow` (with `I_AtExit` registration stack, 8-handler LIFO traversal, and `runOnError` filter) and is NOT vanilla-1.9-faithful — it must be replaced or rewired by a downstream step before `bun run doom.ts` can claim parity with vanilla 1.9 quit/error behaviour. The DOS binary ENDOOM display semantics (when ENDOOM is read, how it's painted to the text-mode buffer at 0xB8000, whether DOS DOOM waits for keypress before exit) are NOT pinned by this audit because they are not visible in the linuxdoom-1.10 source we treat as authority 4 — a future oracle-capture step targeting `doom/DOOMD.EXE` under DOSBox should pin the exact ENDOOM display position relative to `I_ShutdownGraphics` and the keypress-wait semantics. The cross-checker is exact-equality on every probe field; future audits that extend the probe set must add new probes without mutating prior expectations (the audit ledger is intentionally append-only). The recurring parallel-lane working-tree race documented in prior 01-008 / 01-009 / 01-010 / 01-011 / 01-012 / 03-008 / 03-009 / 04-005 / 04-006 / 05-005 / 05-006 / 05-007 / 03-006 / 03-007 open_risks is still active: the fourteen `bun test` failures (3 WAD-asset inventory + 1 core-math inventory + 4 step-file-heading/plan-validation + 4 isolation probe + 2 cataloged-file failures) are caused by parallel-lane committed state, parallel-lane untracked state, and prior step-file heading flips that the prior inventory snapshots and validator pre-date. The mitigation pattern remains: (a) document the failures in `tests_run` as expected and outside this lane's write lock, (b) commit only this lane's own write-locked files plus plan-control updates, (c) await the inventory lane's snapshot regeneration and a future governance step un-flipping the 01-010/03-008/03-009/05-007 step-file headings. Lane lock `99274bec-21d6-47c6-9f44-30c71db90cb0` is held by the launcher and will be released on normal exit. No proprietary assets were modified or staged; `doom/`, `iwad/`, and `reference/` were strictly read-only.
