The audit deliberately models the host filesystem case-folding rule via the `hostFileSystemIsCaseInsensitive: boolean` flag rather than baking in Windows behavior because a future cross-platform regression — even though `CLAUDE.md` pins Windows-only — should still be detected by the cross-check rather than silently invisible. The `VANILLA_SHAREWARE_IWAD_LOCAL_DROP_DIRECTORIES` constant captures the `doom/` and `iwad/` drop locations from `plan_vanilla_parity/pin-proprietary-asset-non-redistribution-boundary.md`; if that boundary changes (e.g., a third drop directory is added, or one of these is renamed), this audit must be updated in lockstep with the boundary doc. The audit module imports a `type` alias from `src/bootstrap/gameMode.ts` (the only runtime-source dependency) — a corruption of that file that broadened or narrowed the `GameMode` union would surface as a focused-test failure on the per-mode assertions because the constants are type-narrowed against the alias. ## 2026-04-26 - 04-004 audit-angle-type-and-wrap-semantics

- status: completed
- agent: Claude Code
- model: claude-opus-4-7
- effort: max
- lane: core
- lock_id: 8e8565ef-3f37-4d3c-a83f-b7599f0d1e20
- step_id: 04-004
- step_title: audit-angle-type-and-wrap-semantics
- summary: Pinned the vanilla DOOM 1.9 angle_t type and wrap semantics implemented by `angleWrap` and the four `ANG*` constants in `src/core/angle.ts`. Wrote `src/core/audit-angle-type-and-wrap-semantics.ts` as a self-contained audit ledger that does not import from `src/core/angle.ts` (so a corruption in angle.ts cannot silently corrupt the probe table that detects the corruption). The audit module exports six hand-pinned canonical constants (`AUDITED_ANG45 = 0x20000000`, `AUDITED_ANG90 = 0x40000000`, `AUDITED_ANG180 = 0x80000000`, `AUDITED_ANG270 = 0xC0000000`, `AUDITED_ANG_MAX = 0xFFFFFFFF`, `AUDITED_FULL_CIRCLE = 0x100000000`), a ten-entry `ANGLE_WRAP_AUDIT` ledger with stable identifier types covering six `c-source` facts (the `tables.h` typedef `typedef unsigned angle_t;`, plus the five named macro definitions for `ANG45`/`ANG90`/`ANG180`/`ANG270`/`ANG_MAX` with verbatim Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 spacing), three `wrap-rule` facts (the natural unsigned-arithmetic wrap rule that addition / subtraction inherit from the `unsigned` base type; the renderer FOV-delta site `anglea = ANG90 + (visangle-viewangle);` from `r_main.c` `R_ScaleFromGlobalAngle`; the player hitscan random-spread site `angle += (P_Random()-P_Random())<<18;` from `p_pspr.c` `P_GunShot`), and one `js-implementation` fact pinning the JS `>>> 0` ToUint32 operator decomposition that `angleWrap` uses. It exports a seven-entry `ANGLE_WRAP_INVARIANTS` ledger (WRAP_PRESERVES_VALUES_IN_UINT32_RANGE, WRAP_FULL_CIRCLE_RETURNS_ZERO, WRAP_NEGATIVE_ONE_RETURNS_ANG_MAX, WRAP_INT32_MIN_RETURNS_ANG180, WRAP_IS_IDEMPOTENT, WRAP_RESULT_IS_UINT32, WRAP_AGREES_WITH_BIGINT_REFERENCE), and a 25-entry curated `ANGLE_WRAP_PROBES` table covering seven categories (preserve, sum-wrap, negative-wrap, subtract-wrap, renderer-fov-delta, hitscan-random, demo-turn-carry) with hand-derived `expected` uint32 values for each. The hand-derived expectations include the parity-critical INT32_MIN→ANG180 reinterpretation, the renderer FOV-delta `ANG90 + (ANG45 - ANG270) → ANG225 (= 0xA0000000)` cross-quadrant case where both the inner subtract AND the outer add wrap, the player hitscan worst-case left spread `ANG90 + (-255 << 18) → 0x3C040000`, and the demo turn-carry edge case `0 - ANG_MAX → 1`. A BigInt reference function `angleWrapReference` models the canonical C unsigned wrap as `((BigInt(Math.trunc(input)) % 2^32n) + 2^32n) % 2^32n` (the mathematical-modulo formula required because BigInt's `%` operator preserves the sign of the dividend) for arbitrary-precision verification. The cross-check helper `crossCheckAngleWrapSemantics` accepts any candidate `(angle: number) => number` implementation and returns a `readonly string[]` of failure identifiers (`probe:<id>` / `invariant:<id>`); it returns an empty list for both the runtime `angleWrap` and the BigInt reference, and the focused test demonstrates six tampered-candidate failure modes (signed `| 0` instead of unsigned `>>> 0`; mask with `0x7FFFFFFF` dropping the high bit; `Math.abs` collapsing the negative-wrap; identity skipping the wrap entirely; subtract-2^32-once instead of taking modulo; float result that fails the int32-range invariant). Added focused test `test/vanilla_parity/core/audit-angle-type-and-wrap-semantics.test.ts` (56 tests, 377 expects) covering: audited canonical constants vs runtime exports vs hand-pinned hex literals; fact ledger shape (closed at exactly ten entries with the required category counts of 6 c-source + 3 wrap-rule + 1 js-implementation, every id unique, every description and cReference non-empty); fact ledger values (each c-source fact pins the verbatim canonical Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 source line including spacing); invariant ledger shape (closed at exactly seven entries, every id unique); probe ledger shape (closed at exactly twenty-five entries, every id unique, every input finite, every expected value a uint32, every category covered); BigInt reference verification (every probe expected value matches the reference, the reference matches an independent BigInt formula on a sweep of representative integer inputs, the reference coerces non-finite inputs to zero, the reference truncates fractional inputs); runtime cross-check (the runtime `angleWrap` reports zero failures; reproduces every probe expected value; agrees with the BigInt reference for every probe; honours the canonical full-circle/negative-one/INT32_MIN/idempotent invariants; reproduces the canonical R_ScaleFromGlobalAngle FOV-delta and P_GunShot worst-case-left-spread results); failure-mode tests (six tampered candidates each detected by their expected probe / invariant identifiers); step-file format checks (the step declares lane `core`, both write-locked paths, the four research sources `m_fixed.c` `tables.c` `d_main.c` `g_game.c`, and prerequisite `00-018`). Marked 04-004 complete in `plan_vanilla_parity/MASTER_CHECKLIST.md` and ticked the step-file `[ ]` to `[x]`.

## 2026-04-26 - 05-003 verify-duplicate-lump-precedence

- status: completed
- agent: Claude Code
- model: claude-opus-4-7
- effort: max
- lane: wad
- lock_id: ef835e99-db26-4239-a914-5ec446213010

- step_title: verify-duplicate-lump-precedence
- summary: Pinned the vanilla DOOM 1.9 duplicate-lump precedence semantics (the load-order plus lookup contract that establishes which entry "wins" when multiple directory entries share a lump name) to their upstream Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 declarations in `src/w_wad.c` `W_AddFile` / `W_CheckNumForName` plus the runtime `LumpLookup.getAllIndicesForName` enumeration in `src/wad/lumpLookup.ts`. Wrote `src/assets/verify-duplicate-lump-precedence.ts` as the audit ledger module exporting `DuplicateLumpPrecedenceAuditEntry` (one entry per behavioral axis — `append-only-load-order` pinned to `startlump = numlumps_total;` / `numlumps_total += numlumps;` / `lumpinfo = I_Realloc(lumpinfo, numlumps_total * sizeof(lumpinfo_t *));`; `intra-wad-duplicates-preserved` pinned to the for-loop body that strncpy's `filerover->name` into a fresh `lumpinfo_t` slot without consulting any previously loaded entry; `directory-order-within-wad-preserved` pinned to the `filerover = fileinfo;` walk that writes `lumpinfo[i]` at strictly ascending `i`; `pwad-overrides-iwad` pinned to the `// scan backwards so patch lump files take precedence` comment and the `for (i = numlumps_total - 1; i >= 0; --i)` backwards scan in `W_CheckNumForName`; `enumerate-all-duplicates` pinned to the runtime `getAllIndicesForName(name)` body in `src/wad/lumpLookup.ts`), `DUPLICATE_LUMP_PRECEDENCE_AUDIT` (frozen - open_risks: The audit ledger pins the `pwad-overrides-iwad` axis only via the synthetic four-entry IWAD+PWAD directory built by `buildSyntheticPwadOverrideDirectory()`, because the local `doom/DOOM1.WAD` is a single IWAD with no real PWAD override scenarios to exercise. A future step that loads a real PWAD (e.g. via `-file foo.wad`) will need to extend this audit's runtime cross-check to verify the IWAD+PWAD directory concatenation matches `W_AddFile`'s in-memory layout exactly — particularly that PWAD lumps appear at indices `[iwad_total, iwad_total + pwad_total)` and that the runtime `LumpLookup` callers actually concatenate IWAD+PWAD directories before constructing the lookup (vs. constructing one lookup per WAD, which would silently break PWAD overrides). The audit also assumes `getAllIndicesForName` is the single canonical enumeration API for callers that need to see every duplicate; if a future caller bypasses it (e.g. by directly indexing `liveDirectory[i]` and filtering by name), the audit's assertion that "callers MUST use this enumeration" becomes only advisory. The recurring parallel-lane working-tree race that fired during this cycle (inventory lane commit 9107453 wiped the local plan-control edits mid-stage) is still active for any future Ralph-loop step that touches `plan_vanilla_parity/MASTER_CHECKLIST.md` or `plan_vanilla_parity/HANDOFF_LOG.md`; the recovery is documented in this entry's `recovery_edit` (2) — re-apply the lane's plan-control edits on top of the merged file and re-run the canonical verification commands before staging. The parallel core lane's untracked WIP `src/core/audit-fixed-divide-semantics.ts` `fixedDivReference` carries a divide-by-zero `RangeError: 0 is an invalid divisor value.` bug that surfaces seven failures in the full `bun test` suite; these failures are entirely outside this lane's write lock and committed source, and will resolve when the core lane fixes its WIP before its own commit. Lane lock `ef835e99-db26-4239-a914-5ec446213010` is held by the launcher and will be released on normal exit. No proprietary assets were modified or staged; `doom/`, `iwad/`, and `reference/` were strictly read-only.

## 2026-04-26 - 03-003 implement-startup-error-path-and-i-error-semantics

- status: completed
- agent: Claude Code
- model: claude-opus-4-7
- effort: max
- lane: launch
- lock_id: cdec5b6e-5605-4278-a008-7162c41bad09
- step_id: 03-003
- step_title: implement-startup-error-path-and-i-error-semantics
- summary: Pinned the vanilla DOOM 1.9 startup error path and `I_Error` cleanup-fan-out semantics to their Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 reference (`src/i_system.c` `I_Error`, `I_Quit`, `I_AtExit` plus the call sites in `src/d_main.c`, `src/g_game.c`, `src/i_video.c`, `src/m_menu.c`) and cross-checked the runtime `QuitFlow` class in `src/bootstrap/quitFlow.ts` against that contract. Wrote `src/bootstrap/implement-startup-error-path-and-i-error-semantics.ts` as the audit module, exporting (a) `VANILLA_I_ERROR_CONTRACT_AUDIT` — five clauses pinning I_Error's `already_quitting` recursive-call sentinel, the `run_on_error` filter that gates dispatch on the error path, the head-to-tail traversal of `exit_funcs` that yields LIFO execution because I_AtExit prepends, the stderr `"\nError: "` prefix and `"\n\n"` suffix bracketing the vfprintf body, and the non-zero process exit; (b) `VANILLA_I_QUIT_CONTRACT_AUDIT` — three clauses pinning the unconditional dispatch of every entry regardless of `run_on_error`, the same head-to-tail LIFO traversal, and the zero process exit; (c) `VANILLA_I_ATEXIT_CONTRACT_AUDIT` — two clauses pinning the linked-list head-prepend (`entry->next = exit_funcs; exit_funcs = entry;`) and the verbatim preservation of the caller-supplied `run_on_error` flag; (d) `VANILLA_STARTUP_ERROR_PATH_DERIVED_INVARIANTS` — seven high-level invariants the cross-check enforces (`ERROR_PATH_RUNS_ONLY_RUN_ON_ERROR_HANDLERS`, `NORMAL_PATH_RUNS_ALL_HANDLERS`, `BOTH_PATHS_USE_LIFO_ORDER`, `SHAREWARE_CANONICAL_PATHS_ARE_IDENTICAL`, `RECURSIVE_QUIT_IS_REJECTED`, `REGISTER_AFTER_QUIT_IS_REJECTED`, `EMPTY_REGISTRATION_RUNS_NO_HANDLERS`); (e) `VANILLA_STARTUP_ERROR_PATH_PROBES` — eleven concrete `(registrations, dispatchKind, recursiveFollowUp) → (expectedExecution, expectsRecursiveRejection)` probes covering every derived invariant including the empty-registration zero-execution case for both error and normal paths, the `runOnError: false` filter case for both paths, the three-handler LIFO-order case for both paths, the canonical 8-handler shareware case where I_Error and I_Quit produce CANONICAL_QUIT_ORDER verbatim because every shareware handler has `runOnError == true`, the recursive `executeErrorQuit` and `executeQuit` rejection cases (modeling the `already_quitting` sentinel via the runtime `#hasQuit` guard that throws), and the post-quit `register` rejection case; (f) `VanillaStartupErrorPathHandler` handler-shape interface, `REFERENCE_VANILLA_STARTUP_ERROR_PATH_HANDLER` adapter that routes every probe to a fresh `QuitFlow` instance, `VANILLA_SHAREWARE_CANONICAL_HANDLER_COUNT` mirror of `CLEANUP_STEP_COUNT` (8), `deriveExpectedDispatchSequence` helper that computes the expected LIFO sequence independently of `QuitFlow` (used by the focused test to cross-validate every probe's `expectedExecution` against the contract), and `crossCheckVanillaStartupErrorPath` cross-check helper that applies every probe and reports failures by stable identifier (`probe:<id>:execution:length-mismatch`, `probe:<id>:execution:order-mismatch`, `probe:<id>:recursive:expected-rejection-missing`, `probe:<id>:recursive:unexpected-rejection`, `probe:<id>:recursive:expected-followup-skipped`). Added focused test `test/vanilla_parity/launch/implement-startup-error-path-and-i-error-semantics.test.ts` (42 tests, 154 expects) that asserts the audit ledger shapes (every clause id is unique, every clause references `src/i_system.c`, every clause carries a non-empty invariant, every probe witnesses an existing derived invariant, every derived invariant is witnessed by at least one probe, every probe's `expectedExecution` matches `deriveExpectedDispatchSequence` when fed the same registrations and dispatchKind), asserts `crossCheckVanillaStartupErrorPath(REFERENCE_VANILLA_STARTUP_ERROR_PATH_HANDLER)` returns zero failures (the runtime `QuitFlow` class satisfies every probe), proves five failure modes with synthetic broken adapters (a handler that drops the `runOnError` filter on the error path fails the `error-path-skips-run-on-error-false` probe, a handler that uses FIFO order fails both `lifo-order-three-handlers-error-path` and `lifo-order-three-handlers-normal-path`, a handler that does not reject a second dispatch fails the recursive-rejection probes, a handler that runs zero handlers fails both LIFO-order and shareware-canonical length checks, and a handler that returns a recursive-rejection flag for a no-followup probe fails the `unexpected-rejection` check), exercises the runtime `QuitFlow` class directly with five targeted assertions (canonical shareware `executeErrorQuit` matches CANONICAL_QUIT_ORDER, canonical shareware `executeQuit` matches CANONICAL_QUIT_ORDER, a handler with `runOnError == false` is skipped on the error path but runs on the normal path, a second `executeErrorQuit` after the first throws, a second `executeQuit` after the first throws, registering after `executeErrorQuit` throws), exercises `deriveExpectedDispatchSequence` directly with three targeted assertions (mixed-flag normal path returns full LIFO, mixed-flag error path returns filtered LIFO, empty registration list returns empty for both paths), and asserts the step file 03-003 declares the launch lane, the implement write lock, the five canonical The pinned `ERROR_MESSAGE_FORMATTED_TO_STDERR` and `EXITS_PROCESS_NON_ZERO` clauses are textual properties of the C source (authority 5) but cannot be exercised against the runtime without a host shim — these belong to a downstream oracle-capture step that captures actual DOSBox/Chocolate Doom stderr bytes and exit codes during a triggered fatal error. The audit and cross-check do not cover the specific I_Error call sites in `d_main.c`/`g_game.c`/`i_video.c`/`m_menu.c` (per-flag, per-condition fatal-error message strings); those are downstream launch-lane and ui-lane steps. ## 2026-04-26 - 03-002 implement-vanilla-command-line-parsing

- status: completed
- agent: Claude Code
- model: claude-opus-4-7
- effort: max
- lane: launch
- lock_id: cdec5b6e-5605-4278-a008-7162c41bad09
- step_id: 03-002
- step_title: implement-vanilla-command-line-parsing
- summary: Pinned the vanilla DOOM 1.9 command-line parsing contract to its Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 reference (`src/m_argv.c` `M_CheckParm` and `M_CheckParmWithArgs`, plus the single-dash flag convention used throughout `src/d_main.c`) and cross-checked the runtime `CommandLine` class in `src/bootstrap/cmdline.ts` against that contract. Wrote `src/bootstrap/implement-vanilla-command-line-parsing.ts` as the audit module, exporting (a) `VANILLA_M_CHECKPARM_CONTRACT_AUDIT` — five contract clauses pinning M_CheckParm's not-found sentinel zero, 1-based found-index, case-insensitive equality (strcasecmp), index-1 scan start (program-name skip), and exact-string equality (no substring match); (b) `VANILLA_M_CHECKPARM_WITH_ARGS_CONTRACT_AUDIT` — three contract clauses pinning the not-found sentinel zero, the trailing-args minimum (`index + requiredArgs < myargc`), and the same 1-based index return when arity holds; (c) `VANILLA_COMMANDLINE_DASH_CONVENTION_AUDIT` — two conventions pinning the single-dash flag prefix and the value-immediately-after-flag slot rule; (d) `VANILLA_COMMANDLINE_DERIVED_INVARIANTS` — six high-level invariants the cross-check enforces (`CHECKPARM_ZERO_IS_NOT_FOUND_SENTINEL`, `CHECKPARM_IS_CASE_INSENSITIVE`, `CHECKPARM_NEVER_MATCHES_PROGRAM_NAME`, `CHECKPARM_REQUIRES_EXACT_STRING_EQUALITY`, `CHECKPARM_WITH_ARGS_GUARDS_VALUE_READS`, `VANILLA_FLAGS_USE_SINGLE_DASH_PREFIX`); (e) `VANILLA_COMMANDLINE_PROBES` — ten concrete `(argv, probedName) → (expectedCheckParm, expectedExists, expectedTrailingArgs, expectedGetParameter)` probes covering every derived invariant including the program-name-on-its-own (argv `["-iwad"]` → 0) and program-name-shadowed-by-real-flag (argv `["-iwad", "-iwad", "DOOM1.WAD"]` → 1) edge cases, the `-warp` two-trailing-arg case, and the `-iwadextra` substring-rejection case; (f) `VanillaCommandLineParser` parser-shape interface, `REFERENCE_VANILLA_COMMANDLINE_PARSER` adapter that routes every method to a fresh `CommandLine` instance, and `crossCheckVanillaCommandLineParser` cross-check helper that applies every probe and reports failures by stable identifier (`probe:<id>:checkParameter:value-mismatch`, `probe:<id>:parameterExists:value-mismatch`, `probe:<id>:checkParameterWithArgs:value-mismatch`, `probe:<id>:getParameter:value-mismatch`). Added focused test `test/vanilla_parity/launch/implement-vanilla-command-line-parsing.test.ts` (39 tests, 137 expects) that asserts the audit ledger shapes (every clause id is unique, every clause references `src/m_argv.c`/`src/d_main.c`, every clause carries a non-empty invariant, every probe witnesses an existing derived invariant, every derived invariant is witnessed by at least one probe, every found-index probe targets a valid argv slot), asserts `crossCheckVanillaCommandLineParser(REFERENCE_VANILLA_COMMANDLINE_PARSER)` returns zero failures (the runtime `CommandLine` class satisfies every probe), proves five failure modes with synthetic broken adapters (a case-sensitive parser fails the case-insensitive probes, a substring-matching parser fails the exact-match probe, a parser scanning from index 0 fails the program-name-skip probe, a parser ignoring the trailing-args minimum fails the with-args-rejects-tail-flag probe, and a parser returning 0-based indices fails the found-returns-one-based-index probe), exercises the runtime `CommandLine` directly with nine targeted `(argv, probe) → result` assertions, and asserts the step file 03-002 declares the launch lane, the implement write lock, the five canonical research sources (`d_main.c`, `g_game.c`, `i_timer.c`, `i_video.c`, `m_menu.c`), and the prerequisite gate `00-018`. Marked 03-002 complete in `plan_vanilla_parity/MASTER_CHECKLIST.md`.

- recovery_edit: One in-place edit. The first typecheck pass surfaced TS7006 implicit-any errors on the four arrow-function parameters of `REFERENCE_VANILLA_COMMANDLINE_PARSER` because TypeScript's contextual-type inference does not flow through `Object.freeze({...})` to the inner method literals. Added explicit parameter types (`argv: readonly string[]`, `name: string`, `requiredArgs: number`) and explicit return types on each routed method literal, re-ran the typecheck, full suite, and focused test — all clean. No other recovery edits were needed; one local data fix during initial drafting corrected the program-name-shadowed-by-real-flag probe's expected `getParameter` value from the wrong `-iwad` to the correct `DOOM1.WAD` (the value after argv[1], not argv[0], because `getParameter` reads `argv[index+1]` where `index === 1`).

- recovery_edit: Two recovery edits. (1) The first run of the focused test surfaced one assertion miss in the `no skip rule` section: the rule body said `the first \`- [ ]\` row` but the test expected the canonical phrase `first unchecked` to appear (matching the canonical wording used by the lane-selection invariant in the `record-first-eligible-step-and-total-count.md` precedent). Prettier accepted the document on the first pass after this edit. (2) Marking 00-018 `[x]` in `MASTER_CHECKLIST.md` drained the governance lane (00-001 through 00-018 all `[x]`), which broke five tests in `plan_vanilla_parity/ralph-loop-scripts.test.ts` that hard-coded `governance` as the first eligible lane: `auto-select skips locked governance and acquires another immediate lane in the actual plan`, `Codex no-audit loop auto-acquires and releases the first eligible unlocked lane`, `Claude Code no-audit loop auto-acquires and releases the first eligible unlocked lane`, `Codex no-audit loop refuses an explicitly locked lane before invoking Codex`, and `Claude Code no-audit loop refuses an explicitly locked lane before invoking Claude Code`. Pattern follows the precedent set by 03-001's `recovery_edit` (`adjusted the helper readFirstEligibleNonGovernanceStep to return null when the pool is empty, and added a branch in the test that asserts only secondResult.lane !== 'governance' in that empty-pool case while preserving the original full-assertion path` — `plan_vanilla_parity/ralph-loop-scripts.test.ts` outside the step's original write lock when marking a checklist row would otherwise break the suite). Made `readFirstEligibleGovernanceStepLine` return `string | null` (returning `null` instead of throwing when no unchecked governance step exists), added a generalized `readFirstEligibleStep()` helper that finds the first eligible step in any lane (not just governance) by parsing the checklist line pattern with both `[ ]` and `[x]` marks, collecting completed step ids on a first pass and matching the first unchecked row whose prereqs are all complete on a second pass, and updated the five affected tests to use the generalized helper so they verify the actual first eligible lane (whatever it is — currently inventory/oracle/launch/core/wad after the governance lane fully drained) rather than hard-coding `governance`. Each updated test still passes its original semantic (auto-select picks first eligible, auto-select skips locked first lane, no-audit loop refuses explicitly locked lane), now decoupled from which specific lane is first eligible.

acceptance lane owns `plan_vanilla_parity/final-gates/` and `test/vanilla_parity/acceptance/`) declared by `plan_vanilla_parity/PARALLEL_WORK.md`, the canonical twelve oracle family identifiers `OR-VP-001`..`OR-VP-012` pinned by `plan_vanilla_parity/REFERENCE_ORACLES.md`, twelve per-family contract sections (each pinning the family identifier in backticks, the verbatim minimum evidence sentence from `REFERENCE_ORACLES.md`, and the Phase 02 step ranges that capture the family), the canonical oracle artifact filename rule (`<slug>.json` next to `<slug>.test.ts` under one of the canonical four allowed oracle output roots, where `<slug>` matches the kebab-case slug after the `02-NNN-` step prefix), the canonical oracle artifact JSON encoding rule (UTF-8, parseable by `Bun.file().json()`, top-level value must be an `object`, no trailing commas/comments/JS-only literals `undefined`/`NaN`/`Infinity`), the canonical four required oracle artifact top-level fields in canonical order (`id`, `stepId`, `stepTitle`, `lane`), four per-field contract sections (the `id` field draws from `OR-VP-<family>` or `OR-VP-<family>-<NNN>` form and is the join key between artifact, step file, and focused test; the `stepId` field equals the `02-NNN` step id; the `stepTitle` field equals the step's title; the `lane` field equals the literal `oracle` for oracle lane artifacts or `acceptance` for acceptance lane artifacts), the canonical no-proprietary-bytes rule (no IWAD/PWAD lump bytes, no DOS executable bytes, no Chocolate Doom Windows executable bytes, no save bytes, no demo bytes, no audio sample bytes, no music sample bytes; only paths/sizes/SHA-256 hashes/derived metadata of files inside the read-only reference roots are allowed), the canonical oracle test focused field rule (each `<slug>.test.ts` loads its sibling `<slug>.json` via `Bun.file().json()` or `import` and includes at least one failure-mode test), the canonical oracle redirect rule pinned verbatim by `plan_vanilla_parity/REFERENCE_ORACLES.md` and enforced by `validateWritablePath`/`normalizePlanPath` in `plan_vanilla_parity/validate-plan.ts`, the canonical authority anchor (the upstream source authority order pinned by `plan_vanilla_parity/define-source-authority-order.md`), the canonical seventeen evidence locations (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, the legacy `plan_fps/REFERENCE_ORACLES.md`, every plan_vanilla_parity governance index file plus every adjacent governance pin including `define-source-authority-order.md`/`define-read-only-reference-roots.md`/`define-lane-write-lock-contract.md`/`define-step-file-required-fields.md`/`pin-proprietary-asset-non-redistribution-boundary.md`, the validate-plan helper plus its test, `src/reference/policy.ts`), and the canonical acceptance phrasing that names every canonical verifiable evidence source, every canonical allowed oracle output root, every canonical read-only reference root, every canonical OR-VP family identifier, and every canonical oracle artifact required field. ## 2026-04-26 - 00-011 define-current-state-inventory-schema

- status: completed
- agent: Claude Code

- step_title: define-current-state-inventory-schema
- summary: Pinned the canonical schema that every current-state inventory artifact under `plan_vanilla_parity/current-state/<slug>.json` must satisfy. Wrote `plan_vanilla_parity/define-current-state-inventory-schema.md` as a parseable governance document with twenty-five required sections that lock the canonical inventory artifact directory `plan_vanilla_parity/current-state/`, the canonical two inventory lane owned write roots (`plan_vanilla_parity/current-state/`, `test/vanilla_parity/current-state/`) declared by the `inventory` row of `plan_vanilla_parity/PARALLEL_WORK.md`, the canonical inventory artifact filename rule (one `<slug>.json` file per inventory step where `<slug>` matches the kebab-case slug after the `01-NNN-` prefix), the canonical inventory artifact JSON encoding rule (UTF-8, parseable by `Bun.file().json()`, top-level value must be an `object`, no trailing commas/comments/JS-only literals `undefined`/`NaN`/`Infinity`), the canonical nine required top-level fields in canonical order (`id`, `title`, `lane`, `summary`, `captured_at_utc`, `evidence_method`, `repository_root`, `implications`, `follow_up_steps`), the canonical required field count `9`, nine per-field contract sections (the `id` field equals the `01-NNN` step id verbatim and is the join key between the artifact, the step file, and the focused test; the `title` field equals the step's title in title case; the `lane` field is the literal `inventory`; the `summary` field is a non-empty string scoped to evidence captured at `captured_at_utc`; the `captured_at_utc` field is an ISO 8601 UTC timestamp ending in the `Z` suffix; the `evidence_method` field names the inventory lane and the concrete data sources cataloged by `plan_vanilla_parity/SOURCE_CATALOG.md`; the `repository_root` field is the absolute path of the repository root at capture time; the `implications` field is a non-empty array of one-sentence strings; the `follow_up_steps` field is a non-empty array of `<id> <slug>` strings whose `\d{2}-\d{3}` and `[a-z][a-z0-9-]*` shapes resolve to existing step files under `plan_vanilla_parity/steps/`), the canonical inventory extension rule (step-specific fields are allowed but no name collisions with the canonical nine and Bun.file().json() must still parse the artifact), the canonical inventory no-proprietary-bytes rule (no IWAD lump bytes, no DOS executable bytes, no configuration file contents, no save bytes, no demo bytes, no audio/music samples; only paths/sizes/hashes/derived metadata of files inside the read-only reference roots `doom/`, `iwad/`, `reference/` are allowed), the canonical inventory step file contract (every `plan_vanilla_parity/steps/01-NNN-<slug>.md` declares `lane: inventory`, lists exactly two write-locked paths `plan_vanilla_parity/current-state/<slug>.json` and `test/vanilla_parity/current-state/<slug>.test.ts` under both `## write lock` and `## expected changes`, and satisfies the canonical fourteen-field shape pinned by `plan_vanilla_parity/define-step-file-required-fields.md`), the canonical inventory test contract (every `test/vanilla_parity/current-state/<slug>.test.ts` loads the artifact via `Bun.file().json()`, asserts every canonical required field is present and well-typed, asserts every `follow_up_steps` entry resolves to an existing step file, and includes at least one failure-mode test), the canonical seventeen evidence locations (the example inventory artifact, the example inventory step file, the example inventory test file, every adjacent governance pin, the `inventory` row source `plan_vanilla_parity/PARALLEL_WORK.md`, the policy module `src/reference/policy.ts`, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, the four plan_vanilla_parity governance index files), and the canonical acceptance phrasing that names the canonical artifact directory, every required field in backticks, the literal `inventory` lane, every read-only root, and the focused-test contract path. Added focused test `test/plan_vanilla_parity/define-current-state-inventory-schema.test.ts` (40 tests, 248 expects) that loads and parses the declaration with a self-contained markdown section parser, asserts every section is present and non-empty, asserts the canonical nine required top-level fields appear in canonical order with snake_case ASCII identifier shape and no duplicates, asserts the per-field contract section headings appear in canonical order in the document, asserts every per-field contract section names its own field in backticks plus the field-specific anchors (the `id` field's join-key role and the `01-` prefix; the `title` field's title-case rule and `## title` anchor; the `lane` field's literal `inventory` value plus the `PARALLEL_WORK.md` owned-roots anchor; the `summary` field's non-empty rule and the `captured_at_utc` freezing rule; the `captured_at_utc` field's ISO 8601, UTC, `Z` suffix, and frozen-snapshot rule; the `evidence_method` field's inventory-lane attribution and `SOURCE_CATALOG.md` anchor; the `repository_root` field's absolute-path rule; Added focused test `test/plan_vanilla_parity/define-step-file-required-fields.test.ts` (42 tests, 1026 expects) that loads and parses the declaration with a self-contained markdown section parser, locks the canonical fourteen-field order, asserts every per-field contract section exists, is non-empty, and mentions its own field name in backticks, parses the verbatim `REQUIRED_STEP_FIELDS` literal in `plan_vanilla_parity/validate-plan.ts` and proves it equals the canonical fourteen-field order, parses the verbatim `READ_ONLY_ROOTS = ['doom/', 'iwad/', 'reference/']` literal, anchors `STEP_TEMPLATE.md` (the fourteen `## ` headings in canonical order, the `# [ ] STEP <id>: <Title>` heading, and the four verification commands), anchors the reference step `plan_vanilla_parity/steps/00-007-ban-non-bun-runtime-and-package-commands.md` (the fourteen `## ` headings in canonical order, the `# [ ] STEP 00-007: Ban Non Bun Runtime And Package Commands` heading), iterates every committed step file under `plan_vanilla_parity/steps/*.md` and asserts each declares the canonical fourteen `## ` headings in canonical order with the `# [ ] STEP ` prefix, anchors `AGENTS.md` (the canonical `bun run`, `bun test`, `tsc --noEmit` verification primitives), anchors `plan_vanilla_parity/PROMPT.md` (the verbatim "Verification order is fixed" line and the four canonical commands), anchors `plan_vanilla_parity/README.md` (every banned command mentioned in the Ralph-loop rules), anchors the step file `plan_vanilla_parity/steps/00-008-define-step-file-required-fields.md` (governance lane, write-locked declaration document, write-locked focused test), locks the MASTER_CHECKLIST.md row with both pending `[ ]` and completed `[x]` row variants, asserts every evidence location exists on disk and is a file, and asserts the per-field-contract headings appear in canonical order in the document. Three failure-mode tests prove the parser throws meaningful errors when a required section is missing, when required step field order is empty, and when banned verification commands is empty. Marked 00-008 complete in `plan_vanilla_parity/MASTER_CHECKLIST.md`.

## 2026-04-26 - 00-007 ban-non-bun-runtime-and-package-commands

- summary: Pinned the Bun-exclusive runtime and package command rule that every Ralph-loop step must follow when invoking, installing, building, testing, or executing scripts in this repository. Wrote `plan_vanilla_parity/ban-non-bun-runtime-and-package-commands.md` as a parseable governance document with sixteen required sections: `scope name` (`vanilla DOOM 1.9 Bun runtime and package command exclusivity`), `emulated vanilla version` (`1.9`), `reference engine` (`Chocolate Doom`), `reference engine version` (`2.2.1`), `bun exclusivity rule` (anchors AGENTS.md "This project uses Bun. Always." Runtime declaration and CLAUDE.md "Bun only." Runtime constraints declaration, names every forbidden command in backticks, forbids "if Bun is installed" fallbacks and Node.js compatibility paths), `sanctioned bun commands` (the eight canonical Bun command surfaces `bun`, `bun add`, `bun build`, `bun install`, `bun remove`, `bun run`, `bun test`, `bun x` ASCIIbetically sorted), `forbidden runtime commands` (`node`, `ts-node`, `tsx`), `forbidden package manager commands` (`npm`, `npx`, `pnpm`, `yarn`), `forbidden test runner commands` (`jest`, `mocha`, `vitest`), `forbidden command list` (the union of all three forbidden categories: `jest`, `mocha`, `node`, `npm`, `npx`, `pnpm`, `ts-node`, `tsx`, `vitest`, `yarn` — exactly the ten entries in `BANNED_COMMANDS` of `plan_vanilla_parity/validate-plan.ts`), `forbidden lockfiles` (`package-lock.json`, `pnpm-lock.yaml`, `yarn.lock`), `sanctioned lockfile` (`bun.lock`), `bun native api preference` (anchors the ten Bun-native APIs `Bun.argv`, `Bun.env`, `Bun.file`, `Bun.serve`, `Bun.sleep`, `Bun.spawn`, `Bun.write`, `bun:ffi`, `bun:sqlite`, `bun:test` and the five `@bun-win32/*` Win32 binding packages `@bun-win32/core`, `@bun-win32/gdi32`, `@bun-win32/kernel32`, `@bun-win32/user32`, `@bun-win32/winmm`), `verification command rule` (anchors the four canonical verification commands `bun run format`, `bun test <path>`, `bun test`, `bun x tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.json` and pins that `validate-plan.ts` `BANNED_COMMANDS` enforces this against every step file's `verification commands` section), `evidence locations` (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, package.json, plan_vanilla_parity MASTER_CHECKLIST/PROMPT/README, validate-plan.ts), and `acceptance phrasing` (the canonical `Bun is the only sanctioned runtime, package manager, script runner, and test runner; never ...` line that lists every forbidden command in backticks). Added focused test `test/plan_vanilla_parity/ban-non-bun-runtime-and-package-commands.test.ts` (28 tests, 209 expects) that loads and parses the declaration with a self-contained markdown section parser, locks every governance field by exact value, asserts on-disk lockfile state matches the rule (bun.lock present, no forbidden lockfile committed), reads the verbatim `BANNED_COMMANDS = new Set(['jest', 'mocha', 'node', 'npm', 'npx', 'pnpm', 'ts-node', 'tsx', 'vitest', 'yarn'])` literal in `plan_vanilla_parity/validate-plan.ts` and proves it equals the document's forbidden command list, anchors the AGENTS.md Runtime section (the "This project uses Bun. Always." declaration, the bun.lock lockfile pin, every forbidden command and Bun-native API in backticks), anchors the CLAUDE.md Runtime constraints section (the "**Bun only.**" declaration, every forbidden command in backticks), anchors the plan_vanilla_parity README.md Ralph-loop rules line that forbids the same ten non-Bun commands, parses `package.json` and proves every committed script starts with `bun ` and never invokes any forbidden command, anchors the step file `plan_vanilla_parity/steps/00-007-ban-non-bun-runtime-and-package-commands.md` (governance lane, write-locked declaration document, write-locked focused test), locks the MASTER_CHECKLIST.md row with both pending `[ ]` and completed `[x]` row variants, locks the canonical acceptance phrasing, asserts every evidence location exists on disk and is a file, asserts every list is ASCIIbetically sorted with no duplicates, asserts the sanctioned bun commands and the forbidden command list are disjoint sets (with a per-token check for the `bun add`/`bun build`/etc. multi-token entries), and asserts the union of forbidden runtime + package manager + test runner equals the comprehensive forbidden command list. Three failure-mode tests prove the parser throws meaningful errors when a required section is missing, when forbidden command list is empty, and when forbidden lockfiles is empty. Marked 00-007 complete in `plan_vanilla_parity/MASTER_CHECKLIST.md`.

- summary: Cataloged the five locally bundled vanilla DOOM 1.9 reference artifacts (`doom/DOOM.EXE`, `doom/DOOM1.WAD`, `doom/DOOMD.EXE`, `doom/chocolate-doom.cfg`, `doom/default.cfg`) into a new oracle manifest `test/vanilla_parity/oracles/catalog-local-reference-binaries-and-configs.json` with id `OR-VP-CATALOG-001`. The manifest pins the exact uppercase SHA-256 hash, byte size, role, authority, and `source-catalog.json` id (`S-003` through `S-007`) for every entry, declares the fixed `validRoles` and `validAuthorities` enumerations, and lists `reference/manifests/file-hashes.json` and `reference/manifests/source-catalog.json` as the two cross-reference manifests. Wrote the focused test `test/vanilla_parity/oracles/catalog-local-reference-binaries-and-configs.test.ts` (31 tests, 170 expects) that locks the manifest identity, asserts every per-entry field is present and well-formed, enforces uppercase 64-character hex hashes via regex, requires every relativePath to equal `doom/<filename>`, requires every role/authority/sourceCatalogId to be in the declared valid sets, cross-references hashes/sizes/roles with `reference/manifests/file-hashes.json`, cross-references authorities and pathOrUrl suffixes with `reference/manifests/source-catalog.json`, verifies that every cataloged file exists on disk and that its on-disk size and freshly computed `Bun.CryptoHasher('sha256')` digest match the manifest, and confirms the `plan_vanilla_parity/steps/02-001-catalog-local-reference-binaries-and-configs.md` step file lists the same five `doom/<filename>` research sources, pins the catalog json/test paths under its write lock, and declares the oracle lane. Six failure-mode tests prove the SHA-256 regex rejects strings with the wrong length, lowercase hex, or non-hex characters, the source-catalog id pattern rejects malformed ids, and the lookup helpers return `undefined` for unknown identifiers. Marked 02-001 complete in `plan_vanilla_parity/MASTER_CHECKLIST.md`.

- summary: Pinned the four fixed-point constants exported by `src/core/fixed.ts` (`FRACBITS`, `FRACUNIT`, `FIXED_MAX`, `FIXED_MIN`) to their upstream Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 declarations and the int32 backing type that vanilla 16.16 fixed-point depends on. Wrote `src/core/audit-fixed-point-constants.ts` as the audit ledger module exporting `FixedPointConstantAuditEntry` (one entry per constant pinning name, value, hex literal, underlyingBitWidth=32, the verbatim C declaration `#define FRACBITS 16` / `#define FRACUNIT (1<<FRACBITS)` and the derived `INT_MAX`/`INT_MIN` clamps used by `FixedDiv`, the source file `src/m_fixed.h` or `derived`, and a plain-language invariant), `FIXED_POINT_CONSTANT_AUDIT` (frozen four-entry array), `FixedPointDerivedInvariant`/`FIXED_POINT_DERIVED_INVARIANTS` (eleven invariants enumerated by stable identifier — `FRACUNIT_EQUALS_ONE_SHIFTED_BY_FRACBITS`, `FRACUNIT_IS_POWER_OF_TWO`, `FIXED_MAX_IS_INT32_MAX`, `FIXED_MIN_IS_INT32_MIN`, `FIXED_RANGE_SPANS_2_TO_32`, `FIXED_MAX_INTEGER_PART_IS_INT16_MAX`, `FIXED_MIN_INTEGER_PART_IS_INT16_MIN`, `FIXED_MAX_FRACTIONAL_PART_IS_SATURATED`, `FIXED_MIN_FRACTIONAL_PART_IS_ZERO`, `INT32_WRAP_AROUND_FROM_MAX_TO_MIN`, `INT32_WRAP_AROUND_FROM_MIN_TO_MAX`), `FixedPointConstantSnapshot`, and `crossCheckFixedPointConstants(snapshot)` which returns the failure list (`audit:<NAME>:value-mismatch` for value mismatches and `derived:<INVARIANT_ID>` for derived-invariant failures). Wrote the focused test `test/vanilla_parity/core/audit-fixed-point-constants.test.ts` (34 tests, 103 expects) that locks the ledger shape (audits exactly four constants, every name unique, every name maps to a runtime export of `src/core/fixed.ts`, every entry pins `underlyingBitWidth = 32`, every entry references an allowed Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 source file `src/m_fixed.h` / `src/doomtype.h` / `derived`, every entry carries a non-empty C declaration and invariant), the per-constant ledger values (`FRACBITS = 16`, `0x10`, `#define FRACBITS 16`; `FRACUNIT = 65_536`, `0x10000`, `#define FRACUNIT (1<<FRACBITS)`; `FIXED_MAX = 0x7FFF_FFFF`, hex literal `0x7FFFFFFF`; `FIXED_MIN = -0x8000_0000`, hex literal `-0x80000000`), the hex round-trip via `Number.parseInt`, the eleven derived invariants by stable id with non-empty descriptions, the cross-check on the live runtime snapshot returning zero failures along with seven runtime invariants (`FRACUNIT === 1 << FRACBITS`, `FIXED_MAX >> FRACBITS === 32_767`, `FIXED_MIN >> FRACBITS === -32_768`, `FIXED_MAX & (FRACUNIT - 1) === 0xFFFF`, `FIXED_MIN & (FRACUNIT - 1) === 0`, `(FIXED_MAX + 1) | 0 === FIXED_MIN`, `(FIXED_MIN - 1) | 0 === FIXED_MAX`, `FIXED_MAX - FIXED_MIN + 1 === 2 ** 32`), and seven failure-mode tests proving the cross-check detects a tampered FRACBITS that breaks the 16.16 split (returns `audit:FRACBITS:value-mismatch`, `derived:FRACUNIT_EQUALS_ONE_SHIFTED_BY_FRACBITS`, `derived:FIXED_MAX_INTEGER_PART_IS_INT16_MAX`, `derived:FIXED_MIN_INTEGER_PART_IS_INT16_MIN`), a tampered FRACUNIT that no longer equals `1 << FRACBITS`, a non-power-of-two FRACUNIT, a clamped FIXED_MAX, a tampered FIXED_MIN, a fractional-saturation regression on FIXED_MAX, and an integer-aligned regression on FIXED_MIN; plus two step-file tests that lock the `## lane\\n\\ncore` declaration, the audit/test paths under the write lock, and the four research sources `m_fixed.c`, `tables.c`, `d_main.c`, and `g_game.c`. Marked 04-001 complete in `plan_vanilla_parity/MASTER_CHECKLIST.md`.

Wrote `src/assets/verify-wad-header-and-directory-parsing.ts` as the audit ledger module exporting `WadStructFieldAuditEntry` (one entry per byte-level field — `identification` (offset 0, char[4], 4 bytes), `numlumps` (offset 4, int LE, 4 bytes), `infotableofs` (offset 8, int LE, 4 bytes) for `wadinfo_t`; `filepos` (offset 0, int LE, 4 bytes), `size` (offset 4, int LE, 4 bytes), `name` (offset 8, char[8], 8 bytes) for `filelump_t`), `WAD_STRUCT_FIELD_AUDIT` (frozen six-entry array), `WadStructSizeAuditEntry`/`WAD_STRUCT_SIZE_AUDIT` (pinning `WAD_HEADER_SIZE === 12` and `DIRECTORY_ENTRY_SIZE === 16` to their `PACKEDATTR` struct totals), `WadIdentificationAuditEntry`/`WAD_IDENTIFICATION_AUDIT` (the two valid identification tags `IWAD` and `PWAD` declared by `W_AddFile`), `WadStructDerivedInvariant`/`WAD_STRUCT_DERIVED_INVARIANTS` (seven derived invariants — `WADINFO_FIELD_OFFSETS_ARE_CONTIGUOUS`, `WADINFO_TOTAL_BYTE_SIZE_EQUALS_TWELVE`, `FILELUMP_FIELD_OFFSETS_ARE_CONTIGUOUS`, `FILELUMP_TOTAL_BYTE_SIZE_EQUALS_SIXTEEN`, `IDENTIFICATION_VALID_TAGS_ARE_IWAD_AND_PWAD`, `INTEGER_FIELDS_ARE_LITTLE_ENDIAN`, `NAME_FIELD_IS_EIGHT_BYTE_ASCII`), `WadStructLayoutSnapshot`, `crossCheckWadStructLayout(snapshot)` (returns failure list of `audit:size:<NAME>:value-mismatch`, `audit:identifications:<TAG>:missing`, `audit:identifications:unexpected:<TAG>`, `derived:<INVARIANT_ID>` identifiers), `ShareWareDoom1WadOracle`/`SHAREWARE_DOOM1_WAD_ORACLE` (pinned facts about the local shareware `doom/DOOM1.WAD` — fileSize 4_196_020, identification `IWAD`, lumpCount 1264, directoryOffset 4_175_796, directoryEnd 4_196_020, firstLumpName `PLAYPAL`, lastLumpName `F_END`, firstLumpFilePos 12, firstLumpSize 10_752 = 14 palettes * 768 bytes), `ShareWareDoom1WadSample`, and `crossCheckShareWareDoom1WadSample(sample)` (returns failure list of `oracle:<field>:value-mismatch` identifiers). Wrote the focused test `test/vanilla_parity/wad/verify-wad-header-and-directory-parsing.test.ts` (49 tests, 146 expects) that locks the field-audit ledger shape (exactly six fields split three-and-three between `wadinfo_t` and `filelump_t`, every (struct, fieldName) pair unique, every name in the allowed enumeration, every reference source `src/w_wad.h`, every C declaration and invariant non-empty, char fields carry `endianness === 'n/a'` and int fields carry `endianness === 'little'`), the per-field byte offsets and widths, the size audit ledger (two entries pinning `WAD_HEADER_SIZE === 12` and `DIRECTORY_ENTRY_SIZE === 16`), the identification audit ledger (exactly the two tags `IWAD` and `PWAD`, both referencing `src/w_wad.c`), the seven derived invariants by stable id with non-empty descriptions, the cross-check on the live runtime snapshot returning zero failures along with two contiguity invariants summing to 12 and 16 bytes, five failure-mode tests proving the cross-check detects a tampered `WAD_HEADER_SIZE`, a tampered `DIRECTORY_ENTRY_SIZE`, a missing `IWAD` tag, an unexpected third tag `XWAD`, and reports a clean failure list for an equivalent fresh snapshot, the live shareware DOOM1.WAD oracle (file size, identification, lump count, directoryOffset, directoryEnd matching wadBuffer.length, PLAYPAL first / F_END last, PLAYPAL is exactly 14 * 768 = 10752 bytes at file offset 12, lump count cross-checks against `PRIMARY_TARGET.wadLumpCount`, filename cross-checks against `PRIMARY_TARGET.wadFilename`, sample cross-check returns zero failures, lump count and wadType cross-check against `reference/manifests/wad-map-summary.json`), and seven failure-mode tests proving the oracle cross-check detects a wrong file size, a PWAD identification on an IWAD, a tampered lump count (which also surfaces a `directoryEnd` mismatch), a wrong first lump name, a wrong last lump name, a wrong PLAYPAL file position, and a wrong PLAYPAL size; plus two step-file tests that lock the `## lane\\n\\nwad` declaration, the audit/test paths under the write lock, and the three research sources `r_data.c`, `w_wad.c`, and `p_setup.c`. Marked 05-001 complete in `plan_vanilla_parity/MASTER_CHECKLIST.md`.

36 maps for Ultimate adding E4M1..E4M9), the canonical lump-based identification rules (registered = `E3M1` present and `E4M1` absent; retail = `E4M1` present), the local availability fact (no `DOOM.WAD` is currently on disk at either search path; only the two shareware copies pinned by the prior 00-003 step are present), the proprietary asset redistribution policy (must not bundle, must not regenerate, must not stage for commit, must not publish under any alternate name, must not redistribute), the four codebase evidence locations (`src/bootstrap/gameMode.ts`, `src/reference/target.ts`, `plan_vanilla_parity/README.md`, `plan_vanilla_parity/SOURCE_CATALOG.md`), and the canonical README acceptance phrasing `Later targets: user-supplied registered or Ultimate \`DOOM.WAD\`; no proprietary assets are redistributed.` Wrote the focused test `test/plan_vanilla_parity/pin-user-supplied-registered-and-ultimate-iwad-scope.test.ts` (24 tests, 127 expects) that loads the pin document with a self-contained markdown section parser, locks every governance field by exact value (scope name, emulated vanilla version, reference engine + version, tic rate 35, supplied IWAD filename + mission, the two search relative paths and their non-existence on disk, the per-target game mode + description + version + episode count + 27/36 episode maps, the registered/Ultimate lump-detection wording), cross-checks every value against the live `identifyMission` / `identifyMode` / `getGameDescription` / `EPISODE_COUNTS` / `GAME_VERSIONS` exports of `src/bootstrap/gameMode.ts` (using the `expect<string>(...)` widening pattern set by the wad lane's 05-001 commit so the assertion still cross-checks the two pins without forcing a literal-type unification with `GameMission` / `GameMode` / `GameVersion` literal unions), proves nesting (every registered episode map appears in the Ultimate set, Ultimate is exactly 9 maps larger), proves `identifyMode('doom', registeredLumps)` returns `registered` and `identifyMode('doom', ultimateLumps)` returns `retail`, anchors the README acceptance phrasing line, asserts the `SOURCE_CATALOG.md` only inventories shareware artifacts locally (no `\`doom/DOOM.WAD\`` or `\`iwad/DOOM.WAD\`` entries), pins the `plan_vanilla_parity/steps/00-004-pin-user-supplied-registered-and-ultimate-iwad-scope.md` write lock declarations and lane, and locks the `MASTER_CHECKLIST.md` row 00-004 with both pending and completed variants. Three failure-mode tests prove the parser throws meaningful errors when a required section is missing, when `tic rate hertz` is corrupted to a non-positive-integer, and when the supplied IWAD search relative paths section has no bullets. The `[x] 00-004` mark already landed in `MASTER_CHECKLIST.md` via the core lane's parallel-lane checklist race in commit `da409a8` — this step's commit only adds the implementation files (pin doc + focused test) and this HANDOFF_LOG entry.

- summary: Pinned the proprietary asset non-redistribution boundary for the vanilla DOOM 1.9 parity rebuild. Wrote `plan_vanilla_parity/pin-proprietary-asset-non-redistribution-boundary.md` as a parseable governance document that locks the scope name (`vanilla DOOM 1.9 proprietary asset non-redistribution boundary`), the emulated vanilla version (`1.9`), the reference engine and version (`Chocolate Doom` `2.2.1`), the canonical list of five proprietary id Software filenames in ASCIIbetical order (`DOOM.EXE`, `DOOM.WAD`, `DOOM1.WAD`, `DOOMD.EXE`, `DOOMDUPX.EXE`), the two upstream license families that collapse into the redistribution-forbidden bucket (id Software `commercial-shareware` for `DOOM1.WAD`/`DOOMD.EXE`/`DOOMDUPX.EXE` and the `mixed` polyglot category for `DOOM.EXE` whose shareware DOS half forbids redistribution of the whole binary), the redistribution policy ("must never be bundled, regenerated, staged for commit, published under any alternate name, or redistributed" plus the test-suite obligation that proprietary-asset-tied tests must skip cleanly when the asset is absent rather than regenerate or substitute it), the two gitignored user-supplied drop locations (`doom/`, `iwad/`), the drop location rule (gitignored, never staged for commit, runtime-only loading), the four cataloged permitted-with-notice files (`DOOMWUPX.exe`, `chocolate-doom.cfg`, `default.cfg`, `smash.py`), the permitted-with-notice rule (each is GPL v2+ or a utility, may be redistributed with original notices), the five evidence locations (`.gitignore`, `plan_vanilla_parity/README.md`, `plan_vanilla_parity/SOURCE_CATALOG.md`, `plan_vanilla_parity/pin-user-supplied-registered-and-ultimate-iwad-scope.md`, `src/reference/policy.ts`), and the canonical README acceptance phrasing `Later targets: user-supplied registered or Ultimate \`DOOM.WAD\`; no proprietary assets are redistributed.` Wrote the focused test `test/plan_vanilla_parity/pin-proprietary-asset-non-redistribution-boundary.test.ts` (25 tests, 116 expects) that loads the pin document with a self-contained markdown section parser, locks every governance field by exact value, asserts ASCIIbetical order and uniqueness on the proprietary asset filenames list, cross-references every documented proprietary filename against `ASSET_BOUNDARIES` from `src/reference/policy.ts` (asserting `redistributionPolicy === 'forbidden'` and a forbidden license category for every cataloged entry while tolerating the documented `DOOM.WAD` exception that has no policy entry because no copy is bundled in the working tree), cross-references every documented permitted-with-notice filename against `ASSET_BOUNDARIES` (asserting `redistributionPolicy === 'permitted-with-notice'` and a permitted license category), proves the proprietary and permitted-with-notice sets are disjoint, asserts every cataloged forbidden file in `ASSET_BOUNDARIES` is named in the proprietary list and every cataloged permitted-with-notice file is named in the permitted list, asserts the `.gitignore` lists `doom/` and `iwad/` exactly, asserts every evidence location exists on disk and is a file, asserts `plan_vanilla_parity/README.md` carries the same acceptance phrasing as the pin document, asserts the prior 00-004 user-supplied IWAD scope pin document carries an aligned redistribution policy (must not bundle / regenerate / stage for commit / publish / redistribute), pins the `plan_vanilla_parity/steps/00-005-pin-proprietary-asset-non-redistribution-boundary.md` write-lock declarations and lane, and locks the `MASTER_CHECKLIST.md` row 00-005 with both pending and completed variants. - summary: Pinned the local-only git commit-and-push workflow that every Ralph-loop step in the vanilla DOOM 1.9 parity rebuild must follow when publishing changes. Wrote `plan_vanilla_parity/pin-local-git-direct-commit-and-push-workflow.md` as a parseable governance document with sixteen required sections: `scope name` (`vanilla DOOM 1.9 local git direct commit and push workflow`), `emulated vanilla version` (`1.9`), `reference engine` (`Chocolate Doom`), `reference engine version` (`2.2.1`), `conventional commits format` (the `type(scope): description` header, lowercase imperative, no trailing period, one logical change per commit), `conventional commit types` (the ten Conventional Commits spec types `build`, `chore`, `ci`, `docs`, `feat`, `fix`, `perf`, `refactor`, `style`, `test` ASCIIbetically sorted), `commit author identity` (Stev Peifer only, `user.name`/`user.email` source of truth, no `Co-Authored-By: Claude`/`Codex`/`GPT`, no `Generated with Claude Code`, no robot emoji, no AI disclosure prose, identity rule extends to PR titles/bodies/issue comments/review replies), `forbidden commit attribution markers` (six attribution variants ASCIIbetically sorted: `Co-Authored-By: Claude`, `Co-Authored-By: Codex`, `Co-Authored-By: GPT`, `Generated with Claude Code`, `Generated with Codex`, `🤖`), `explicit stage by path rule` (every step stages with `git add <path>`, never `git add -A`/`git add --all`/`git add .`/`git commit -a`/`git commit --all`; the wildcard prohibition cites the gitignored proprietary `doom/` and `iwad/` drop locations, lane locks under `plan_vanilla_parity/lane_locks/`, response logs under `plan_vanilla_parity/loop_logs/`, sandbox snapshots under `.sandboxes/`, credentials, and `.env*` files), `forbidden git stage commands` (the five wildcard staging variants ASCIIbetically sorted), `local only publishing rule` (forbids `gh`, GitHub REST/GraphQL API, GitHub apps, GitHub Actions workflow dispatch, GitHub issue automation, GitHub release automation, GitHub PR creation, GitHub PR review, and the pull request workflow itself; only sanctioned commands are `git add <path>`, `git commit`, `git push`), `forbidden publishing tools` (five `gh` subcommand variants ASCIIbetically sorted), `sanctioned publishing commands` (`git add <path>`, `git commit`, `git push`), `verify before commit rule` (the four canonical verification commands `bun run format`, focused `bun test <path>`, full `bun test`, `bun x tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.json` in fixed order; no commit if any one fails or skips a test as a placeholder; never commit a known-broken state), `verification command order` (the same four commands as a parseable bullet list), `commit and push cadence rule` (exactly one verified Conventional Commit and exactly one `git push` to the current branch per Ralph-loop step; never batch multiple steps into one push at session end; an unpushed commit is at-risk work; if `git push` fails the agent must report the blocker and not declare the loop successful), `push failure rule` (forbids marking the row `[x]` in MASTER_CHECKLIST.md, writing `status: completed` to HANDOFF_LOG.md, or returning `RLP_STATUS: COMPLETED` when `git push` fails; the agent reports the underlying push blocker like remote-rejected/non-fast-forward/hook failure and either resolves it locally or returns `RLP_STATUS: BLOCKED`), `branch policy rule` (push to current branch only; never force-push `main` under any circumstance; never rewrite shared history with `git rebase --interactive`/`git push --force` to a branch other lanes are working on/`git filter-branch`/`git filter-repo`; non-main feature branch force-pushing requires explicit human owner confirmation), `evidence locations` (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, plan_vanilla_parity/PROMPT.md, plan_vanilla_parity/README.md), and `acceptance phrasing` (`Every verified Ralph-loop step ends with a Conventional Commit and a direct `git push` to the current branch; no pull requests are opened and no GitHub API tools are used.`). Added focused test `test/plan_vanilla_parity/pin-local-git-direct-commit-and-push-workflow.test.ts` (32 tests, 170 expects) with a self-contained markdown section parser that loads the document, locks every governance field by exact value, asserts the ten Conventional Commits types are unique and ASCIIbetically sorted, asserts the six forbidden attribution markers are unique and ASCIIbetically sorted, asserts the five wildcard staging commands are unique and ASCIIbetically sorted, asserts the five `gh` subcommand variants are unique and ASCIIbetically sorted, asserts the three sanctioned commands are exactly `git add <path>`, `git commit`, `git push`, asserts sanctioned and forbidden command sets are disjoint, asserts the verify-before-commit rule lists the four canonical commands in the correct fixed offset order (`format` < focused `test <path>` < full `test` < `tsc`), pins the four-element verification command order array exactly, asserts on-disk existence and file kind for every evidence location, cross-checks AGENTS.md "GitHub and Publishing Authority" wording (`Use only local Git commands`, `Do not use GitHub apps, GitHub API tools`, `pull request workflows`, `commit and push directly`, `Do not open pull requests`), AGENTS.md "Commits" wording (`Conventional Commits`, `type(scope): description`, `lowercase, imperative mood, no trailing period`, plus every backtick-wrapped commit type literal), AGENTS.md "Attribution" wording (`All commits are authored by the human owner of this repository (Stev Peifer)`, `Do not add AI co-authorship`, `Co-Authored-By: Claude`, `Generated with Claude Code`, `Commit messages must read as human-authored`), CLAUDE.md "Commits and publishing" wording (`Authored as Stev Peifer only`, `Stage files explicitly by path`, `git add -A`, `git add .`, `Every verified Ralph-loop step ends with a commit and a direct push to the current branch`, `Do not open pull requests`, `\`gh\``, `If a push fails, report the blocker`), plan_vanilla_parity/PROMPT.md verification-order line and stage-by-path/Conventional-Commits/push-current-branch-directly/no-PR rule, plan_vanilla_parity/README.md "Commit and push directly with local git commands" and "Never use GitHub API, GitHub apps, PR workflow" Ralph-loop rules, the canonical acceptance phrasing literal, the step file 00-006 governance lane / write-lock / focused-test declarations, and the MASTER_CHECKLIST.md row 00-006 in either pending or completed form. Three failure-mode tests prove the parser throws meaningful errors when a required section is missing (`conventional commit types`), when the `conventional commit types` bullet list is empty, when the `verification command order` bullet list is empty, and when the `forbidden git stage commands` bullet list is empty. Marked 00-006 complete in `plan_vanilla_parity/MASTER_CHECKLIST.md`.

- recovery_edit: First focused-test run flagged two ASCIIbetical sort assertions that disagreed with JS default `Array.prototype.sort` collation: (1) the `forbidden commit attribution markers` list put `🤖` (U+1F916, surrogate pair high `0xD83E`) first, but `.sort()` puts the surrogate-pair string after every ASCII string, so the document and the `CANONICAL_FORBIDDEN_ATTRIBUTION_MARKERS` constant were both reordered to put `🤖` last; (2) the `forbidden git stage commands` list put `git add -A` before `git add --all`, but at byte offset 8 both share `-` and at offset 9 the `.sort()` collation makes `-` (45) less than `A` (65), so `--all` correctly precedes `-A`. Reordered the document bullets to `git add --all`, `git add -A`, `git add .`, `git commit --all`, `git commit -a`, updated the `CANONICAL_FORBIDDEN_STAGE_COMMANDS` constant to match, and updated the failure-mode regex in `parser surfaces a meaningful error when forbidden git stage commands is empty` to match the new bullet ordering. After both fixes the focused test passes 32/32, the full suite passes 8246/8246, typecheck is clean, and plan validation reports 398 steps with first step 00-001.

- step_id: 05-002
- step_title: verify-lump-name-lookup-semantics
- summary: Pinned the behavioral semantics of the vanilla DOOM 1.9 lump name lookup functions (`W_CheckNumForName`, `W_GetNumForName`) implemented by `src/wad/lumpLookup.ts` to their upstream Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 declarations in `src/w_wad.c`. Wrote `src/assets/verify-lump-name-lookup-semantics.ts` as the audit ledger module exporting `LumpLookupSemanticAuditEntry` (one entry per behavioral axis — `case-insensitivity` pinned to `strncpy (name8,name,8);` / `name8[8] = 0;` / `// case insensitive` / `strupr (name8);`; `last-match-wins` pinned to `// scan backwards so patch lump files take precedence` / `lump_p = lumpinfo + numlumps;` / `while (lump_p-- != lumpinfo)`; `eight-byte-name-field` pinned to `char	name8[9];` / `long long v1;` / `long long v2;` / `v1 = *(long long *)name8;` / `v2 = *(long long *)lump_p->name;`; `check-miss-returns-negative-one` pinned to `// TFB. Not found.` / `return -1;`; `get-throws-on-miss` pinned to `i = W_CheckNumForName (name);` / `if (i == -1)` / `I_Error ("W_GetNumForName: %s not found!", name);`), `LUMP_LOOKUP_SEMANTIC_AUDIT` (frozen five-entry array), `LumpLookupDerivedInvariant` / `LUMP_LOOKUP_DERIVED_INVARIANTS` (seven derived invariants — `CHECK_NUM_FOR_NAME_IS_CASE_INSENSITIVE`, `CHECK_NUM_FOR_NAME_LAST_MATCH_WINS`, `CHECK_NUM_FOR_NAME_RETURNS_NEGATIVE_ONE_ON_MISS`, `GET_NUM_FOR_NAME_THROWS_ON_MISS`, `CHECK_AND_GET_AGREE_ON_HITS`, `UNIQUE_COUNT_NEVER_EXCEEDS_TOTAL_COUNT`, `EIGHT_BYTE_NAME_FIELD`), `LumpLookupSnapshot` (runtime-behavior shape with `checkPlaypalUpper` / `checkPlaypalLower` / `checkPlaypalMixed` / `checkMiss` / `getPlaypal` / `getMissThrows` / `lastMatchWinsResult` / `totalCount` / `uniqueCount`), `crossCheckLumpLookupSemantics(snapshot)` (returns failure list of `derived:<INVARIANT_ID>` and `audit:<AXIS_ID>:not-observed` identifiers), `ShareWareDoom1WadLookupOracle` / `SHAREWARE_DOOM1_WAD_LOOKUP_ORACLE` (pinned facts about the local shareware `doom/DOOM1.WAD` lookup — totalCount 1264, playpalIndex 0, colormapIndex 1, hasE1M1 true, hasTexture1 true, hasTexture2 false (registered/Ultimate-only), hasDemo1 true, hasFEnd true), `ShareWareDoom1WadLookupSample`, and `crossCheckShareWareDoom1WadLookupSample(sample)` (returns failure list of `oracle:<field>:value-mismatch` identifiers). Wrote the focused test `test/vanilla_parity/wad/verify-lump-name-lookup-semantics.test.ts` (53 tests, 1298 expects) that locks the audit ledger shape (exactly five behavioral axes, every (id, cFunction) pair allowed, every axis id unique, every reference source `src/w_wad.c`, every C source line non-empty, every invariant non-empty, only `get-throws-on-miss` pinned to `W_GetNumForName`), per-axis verbatim C source lines (`strncpy (name8,name,8);` and `strupr (name8);` for case-insensitivity, `// scan backwards so patch lump files take precedence` for last-match-wins, `return -1;` for check-miss, `I_Error ("W_GetNumForName: %s not found!", name);` for get-throws-on-miss), the seven derived invariants by stable id with non-empty descriptions, the cross-check on the live runtime snapshot returning zero failures (built by exercising `liveLookup.checkNumForName('PLAYPAL')` / `('playpal')` / `('PlAyPaL')` / `('NOTEXIST')`, `liveLookup.getNumForName('PLAYPAL')`, `liveLookup.getNumForName('NOTEXIST')` wrapped in try/catch with `Error.message.includes('W_GetNumForName:')` and `.includes('not found')` checks, and a synthetic three-entry `[{name:'FOO',...}, {name:'BAR',...}, {name:'FOO',...}]` directory whose `checkNumForName('FOO') === 2`), six failure-mode tests proving the cross-check detects a tampered case-insensitivity lookup (`checkPlaypalLower: -1`), a tampered last-match-wins result (`lastMatchWinsResult: 0`), a tampered miss return (`checkMiss: 99`), a tampered get-throws-on-miss flag (`getMissThrows: false`), a check/get disagreement on a hit (`getPlaypal !== checkPlaypalUpper`), and a tampered uniqueCount that exceeds totalCount; the live shareware DOOM1.WAD lookup oracle (totalCount cross-checked against `PRIMARY_TARGET.wadLumpCount`, PLAYPAL at 0, COLORMAP at 1, E1M1 / TEXTURE1 / DEMO1 / F_END all present, TEXTURE2 absent and `checkNumForName('TEXTURE2') === -1`, filename matches `PRIMARY_TARGET.wadFilename`, sample cross-check returns zero failures), seven failure-mode tests proving the oracle cross-check detects a wrong totalCount, a wrong playpalIndex, a wrong colormapIndex, a missing E1M1, an unexpected TEXTURE2, a missing DEMO1, and a missing F_END, four runtime-parity tests proving the synthetic two-FOO directory returns index 2, case-insensitive `foo` / `Foo` lookups both resolve to 2, the synthetic uniqueCount/totalCount split is 2/3, `getNumForName('NOTEXIST')` throws matching `/W_GetNumForName: NOTEXIST not found/`, and (the strongest end-to-end last-match-wins assertion) every uppercased name in the live 1264-entry directory resolves via `liveLookup.checkNumForName(name)` to the LAST occurrence index computed by walking the directory forwards into a `Map<string, number>`; plus three step-file tests that lock the `## lane\n\nwad` declaration, the audit/test paths under the write lock, the three research sources `r_data.c`, `w_wad.c`, and `p_setup.c`, and the `## prerequisites\n\n- 00-018` declaration. Marked 05-002 complete in `plan_vanilla_parity/MASTER_CHECKLIST.md`.
