- open_risks: The audit ledger's `last-match-wins` axis is verified end-to-end by the live-directory test that walks all 1264 entries and confirms `liveLookup.checkNumForName(name) === lastIndexByName.get(name)` for every uppercased name, but the shareware `DOOM1.WAD` is an IWAD — every lump name is unique in directory order. The PWAD-override case is only exercised by the synthetic three-entry directory; a future `05-003` `verify-duplicate-lump-precedence` step will need to extend this to a real PWAD-stacked scenario. The TS runtime does NOT truncate names beyond 8 chars (`Map.get(name.toUpperCase())` keys on the full string) whereas vanilla `strncpy(name8, name, 8)` truncates to 8 bytes; this divergence only manifests if a caller passes a 9+ char name AND the directory contains an 8-char-truncation match, which neither shareware DOOM1.WAD nor any registered/Ultimate IWAD does at any active callsite. If a future PWAD or call path begins passing > 8 char names, an oracle-capture follow-up step must extend the audit ledger to enforce truncation parity. The same recurring parallel-lane working-tree race that surfaced as a transient `bun x tsc` failure during this step (untracked launch-lane file `src/bootstrap/implement-vanilla-command-line-parsing.ts` mid-write) is still active; if it triggers again before the next wad-lane commit lands, the recovery is the same as documented here — re-run `bun x tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.json` and confirm `exit=0` before staging. Lane lock `ef835e99-db26-4239-a914-5ec446213010` is held by the launcher and will be released on normal exit. No proprietary assets were modified or staged; `doom/`, `iwad/`, and `reference/` were strictly read-only.


## 2026-04-26 - 04-002 audit-fixed-multiply-overflow-semantics

- status: completed
- agent: Claude Code
- model: claude-opus-4-7
- effort: max
- lane: core
- lock_id: 8e8565ef-3f37-4d3c-a83f-b7599f0d1e20
- step_id: 04-002
- step_title: audit-fixed-multiply-overflow-semantics
- summary: Pinned the vanilla DOOM 1.9 fixed-point multiply overflow semantics implemented by `fixedMul` in `src/core/fixed.ts`. Wrote `src/core/audit-fixed-multiply-overflow-semantics.ts` as a self-contained audit ledger that does not import from `src/core/fixed.ts` (so a corruption in fixed.ts cannot silently corrupt the probe table that detects the corruption). The audit module exports four hand-pinned canonical constants (`AUDITED_FRACBITS = 16`, `AUDITED_FRACUNIT = 65_536`, `AUDITED_FIXED_MAX = 0x7FFFFFFF`, `AUDITED_FIXED_MIN = -0x80000000`), a ten-entry `FIXED_MUL_OVERFLOW_AUDIT` ledger with stable identifier types covering four `c-source` facts (the `m_fixed.h` header signature `fixed_t FixedMul(fixed_t a, fixed_t b);`, the int64 promotion in the body `return ((int64_t) a * (int64_t) b) >> FRACBITS;`, the arithmetic-right-shift rule on signed int64, and the implicit narrowing cast back to `fixed_t` on return), five `overflow-rule` facts (modulo-2^32 wrap, arithmetic-right-shift rounds toward -inf, identity-by-FRACUNIT, zero-absorbs, commutativity), and one `js-implementation` fact pinning the four-partial-product 16x16 decomposition in `src/core/fixed.ts` as bounded by 2^46 (well below `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER`). It exports a seven-entry `FIXED_MUL_OVERFLOW_INVARIANTS` ledger (IDENTITY_BY_FRACUNIT_LEFT, IDENTITY_BY_FRACUNIT_RIGHT, ZERO_LEFT_ABSORBS, ZERO_RIGHT_ABSORBS, COMMUTATIVE, INT32_RANGE_RESULT, AGREES_WITH_BIGINT_REFERENCE), and a 25-entry curated `FIXED_MUL_OVERFLOW_PROBES` table covering eight categories (identity, zero, integer, fractional, wrap, rounding, sub-unit, extreme) with hand-derived `expected` values for each. The hand-derived expectations include the four documented wraps (FIXED_MAX^2 = -FRACUNIT, FIXED_MIN^2 = 0, FIXED_MIN*FRACUNIT = FIXED_MIN, FIXED_MAX*FRACUNIT = FIXED_MAX), the cross-extreme `FIXED_MAX * FIXED_MIN = 32768` derived through int48 to int32 wrap, the rounding-toward-negative-infinity examples (`-3 * 1 = -1`, `-1 * 1 = -1`), the (-1)*(-1)=0 case where the multiplication flips sign before the shift, the `wrap_FIXED_MAX_x_two = -2` and `wrap_FIXED_MIN_x_two = 0` cases (the latter exact-multiple-of-2^32 wrap), and the sub-unit carry preservation `(FRACUNIT+1)^2 = FRACUNIT+2`. It exports a `fixedMulReference(a, b)` BigInt-based reference implementation modelling the canonical C body `Number(BigInt.asIntN(32, (BigInt(a) * BigInt(b)) >> 16n))` bit-for-bit and a `crossCheckFixedMulOverflowSemantics(fixedMulFn)` helper that returns failure ids `probe:<id>` for any per-probe expectation mismatch and `invariant:<id>` for any derived-invariant violation across the full probe-input sweep. Wrote the focused test `test/vanilla_parity/core/audit-fixed-multiply-overflow-semantics.test.ts` (42 tests, 560 expects) that asserts the audited canonical constants agree with the runtime exports from `src/core/fixed.ts`, locks the ledger shape (exactly 10 facts split 4/5/1 across categories, 7 invariants, 25 probes covering 8 categories), pins specific fact values (the verbatim C signature and body strings, the int32-only operand/expected typing for every probe), verifies the BigInt reference agrees with every probe expectation AND with an inline `Number(BigInt.asIntN(32, ...))` independent reference on a 17x17 sweep of representative int32 values (289 cross-checks), runs `crossCheckFixedMulOverflowSemantics(fixedMul)` against the live runtime and asserts zero failures, asserts the runtime honours every documented invariant on the canonical wrap examples (`fixedMul(FIXED_MAX, FIXED_MAX) === -FRACUNIT`, `fixedMul(FIXED_MIN, FIXED_MIN) === 0`, the three rounding-toward-neg-inf cases, the two extreme identity preservations), and proves five distinct failure-mode tampering candidates are detected (a `Math.imul`-based candidate that drops the high bits of the product before the shift fails `probe:identity_FRACUNIT_x_FRACUNIT` and `invariant:AGREES_WITH_BIGINT_REFERENCE`; a BigInt-division candidate that truncates toward zero instead of toward -inf fails `probe:rounding_neg_three_x_one`, `probe:rounding_neg_one_x_one`, and `invariant:AGREES_WITH_BIGINT_REFERENCE`; a saturating candidate that clamps to FIXED_MAX/FIXED_MIN instead of wrapping fails `probe:wrap_FIXED_MAX_squared`, `probe:wrap_FIXED_MAX_x_two`, and `invariant:AGREES_WITH_BIGINT_REFERENCE`; a no-narrowing candidate that returns the raw shifted BigInt as a Number fails `probe:wrap_FIXED_MAX_squared` and `invariant:INT32_RANGE_RESULT`; a deliberately asymmetric candidate fails `invariant:COMMUTATIVE`; the BigInt reference itself reports zero failures). Three step-file tests lock the `## lane: core` declaration, the `## write lock` paths, the `## research sources` listing of `m_fixed.c` / `tables.c` / `d_main.c` / `g_game.c`, and the `## prerequisites: 00-018` declaration. Marked 04-002 complete in `plan_vanilla_parity/MASTER_CHECKLIST.md`.
- files_changed: D:/Projects/doom-in-typescript/src/core/audit-fixed-multiply-overflow-semantics.ts; D:/Projects/doom-in-typescript/test/vanilla_parity/core/audit-fixed-multiply-overflow-semantics.test.ts; D:/Projects/doom-in-typescript/plan_vanilla_parity/MASTER_CHECKLIST.md; D:/Projects/doom-in-typescript/plan_vanilla_parity/HANDOFF_LOG.md
- recovery_edit: First typecheck pass after authoring the test file flagged three TS2769 errors where `expect(set).toEqual(referenceSet)` rejected `ReadonlySet<X>` because the bun:test `.toEqual` overload signature is `(expected: Set<X>) => void` (and `NoInfer<Set<X>>`), not `(expected: ReadonlySet<X>)`. Fixed by changing the three reference-set declarations (`ALL_FACT_IDS`, `ALL_INVARIANT_IDS`, `ALL_PROBE_IDS`) from `ReadonlySet<X>` to `Set<X>`. The local `Set` instances are still functionally read-only — they are constructed from frozen literal arrays and never mutated by the test — but the type annotation now matches the `.toEqual` overload that Bun matchers expose. Also pruned an originally-confused failure-mode test that attempted to use a `>>>` (logical right shift) tamper but produced bit-equivalent results to the canonical because the `BigInt.asIntN(32, ...)` narrowing throws away the high 32 bits where the sign-extension difference lives — the truncation-toward-zero failure-mode test (using BigInt `/ 65536n` division) covers the same parity surface (rounding-direction violations on negative products) without the false-equivalence issue.
- tests_run: bun run format (Formatted 6 files in 5ms, no fixes applied); bun test test/vanilla_parity/core/audit-fixed-multiply-overflow-semantics.test.ts (42 pass, 0 fail, 560 expects); bun test (8905 pass, 0 fail, 703221 expects across 422 files); bun x tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.json (clean, exit 0); bun test plan_vanilla_parity/validate-plan.test.ts (9 pass, 0 fail, 16 expects); bun run plan_vanilla_parity/validate-plan.ts (Validated 398 vanilla parity steps, First step: 00-001)
- reference_sources: src/core/fixed.ts (current runtime exports `FRACBITS`, `FRACUNIT`, `FIXED_MAX`, `FIXED_MIN`, `fixedMul`, `fixedAdd`, `fixedSub`, `fixedDiv`); src/core/audit-fixed-point-constants.ts (the prior 04-001 audit ledger pattern this step mirrors — append-only ledger, derived-invariants ledger, snapshot type, cross-check helper); test/core/fixed.multiply.test.ts (existing production-behavior tests covering all the same observable cases — the audit ledger pins the canonical C source citations and provides a tamper-detecting cross-check on top of those tests); upstream Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 `src/m_fixed.h` (canonical `fixed_t FixedMul(fixed_t a, fixed_t b);` declaration); upstream Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 `src/m_fixed.c` (canonical body `return ((int64_t) a * (int64_t) b) >> FRACBITS;`); plan_vanilla_parity/SOURCE_CATALOG.md (authority order: local DOOMD.EXE, local DOOM1.WAD, local DOOM.EXE/chocolate-doom.cfg, id Software linuxdoom-1.10, Chocolate Doom 2.2.1); plan_vanilla_parity/REFERENCE_ORACLES.md (oracle artifact placement rules — none triggered by this audit-only step); plan_vanilla_parity/steps/04-002-audit-fixed-multiply-overflow-semantics.md (this step write lock and read-only paths); plan_vanilla_parity/HANDOFF_LOG.md 04-001 entry (the precedent pattern this step extends to function-behavior auditing).
- decision_changes: none
- oracle_changes: none
- next_eligible_steps: 04-003 audit-fixed-divide-semantics (core lane, prereqs `00-018` satisfied — same pattern, audits the `FixedDiv` overflow-clamp behavior in `src/core/fixed.ts`)
- open_risks: The audit module deliberately does not import from `src/core/fixed.ts` (the runtime constants are re-pinned as `AUDITED_FRACBITS`, `AUDITED_FRACUNIT`, `AUDITED_FIXED_MAX`, `AUDITED_FIXED_MIN` so a corrupted fixed.ts cannot silently calibrate the probe table to wrong expectations). One audit test does cross-check the audited canonical values against the runtime exports — that test catches drift between the two sources of truth, but if BOTH are corrupted in the same direction in the same commit, the drift goes undetected. Mitigation: the focused test BigInt reference (`Number(BigInt.asIntN(32, (BigInt(a) * BigInt(b)) >> 16n))`) is recomputed inline from constants `16n`, not from the audited constants, so any drift in `AUDITED_FRACBITS` would surface as a probe expectation mismatch. The audit-vs-runtime cross-check is also enforced by the existing 04-001 `audit-fixed-point-constants.ts` ledger, so a tampered fixed.ts that survived this audit would fail the constants audit independently. The same recurring parallel-lane working-tree race documented in prior governance/inventory/oracle/wad/launch lane HANDOFF_LOG entries is still active; multiple lane locks are currently held (`core.lock` mine, plus `inventory.lock`, `launch.lock`, `wad.lock` from other lanes). Lane lock `8e8565ef-3f37-4d3c-a83f-b7599f0d1e20` is held by the launcher and will be released on normal exit. No proprietary assets were modified or staged; `doom/`, `iwad/`, and `reference/` were strictly read-only.

## 2026-04-26 - 03-004 implement-iwad-discovery-order

- status: completed
- agent: Claude Code
- model: claude-opus-4-7
- effort: max
- lane: launch
- lock_id: cdec5b6e-5605-4278-a008-7162c41bad09
- step_id: 03-004
- step_title: implement-iwad-discovery-order
- summary: Pinned the vanilla DOOM 1.9 IWAD discovery order semantics implemented by `IdentifyVersion()` in id Software's `linuxdoom-1.10/d_main.c`. Wrote `src/bootstrap/implement-iwad-discovery-order.ts` as a self-contained audit ledger (no runtime IWAD discovery module exists yet in `src/bootstrap/`; the audit pins the semantics so a later implementation step can be cross-checked for parity). The audit module exports `VanillaIdentifyVersionContractAuditEntry` (one entry per behavioral axis) and `VANILLA_IDENTIFY_VERSION_CONTRACT_AUDIT` (a frozen seven-entry ledger covering `SEARCH_DIRECTORY_IS_DOOMWADDIR_OR_CURRENT_WORKING_DIRECTORY` pinned to the `if (!doomwaddir) doomwaddir = ".";` fallback rule, `CANDIDATE_FILENAME_LIST_IS_FIXED` pinned to the seven literal basenames `doom2f.wad`, `doom2.wad`, `plutonia.wad`, `tnt.wad`, `doomu.wad`, `doom.wad`, `doom1.wad`, `CANDIDATE_PROBE_ORDER_IS_FIXED` pinned to the verbatim sequence of `if (!access(...))` blocks in IdentifyVersion, `FIRST_MATCH_WINS` pinned to the early `return;` after each `D_AddFile` call, `GAME_MODE_DERIVED_FROM_MATCHED_FILENAME` pinned to the literal gamemode assignment inside each access-block (vanilla-only behavior; Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 diverges by also inspecting lump contents), `NO_MATCH_LEAVES_GAME_MODE_INDETERMINATE` pinned to the `printf("Game mode indeterminate.\n")` plus `gamemode = indetermined;` fall-through (vanilla does not bail; Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 diverges by erroring out), and `CANDIDATE_FILENAMES_ARE_LOWERCASE_LITERALS` pinned to the lowercase ASCII literals inside the sprintf format strings). It exports `VanillaIwadCandidate` (filename + gameMode pair) and the frozen seven-entry `VANILLA_IWAD_CANDIDATES` list in canonical probe order, plus `VANILLA_DEFAULT_SEARCH_DIRECTORY = '.'` and `VANILLA_INDETERMINATE_MESSAGE = 'Game mode indeterminate.\n'`. It exports `VANILLA_IWAD_DISCOVERY_DERIVED_INVARIANTS` (seven derived invariants — `CANDIDATE_LIST_HAS_EXACTLY_SEVEN_ENTRIES`, `CANDIDATE_ORDER_IS_VERBATIM_VANILLA`, `FIRST_MATCH_WINS_BY_PROBE_ORDER`, `MATCHED_FILENAME_PINS_GAME_MODE`, `NO_MATCH_RETURNS_INDETERMINATE_GAME_MODE`, `DOOMWADDIR_OVERRIDES_CURRENT_DIRECTORY`, `OUT_OF_LIST_CANDIDATE_IS_NEVER_MATCHED`), a ten-entry `VANILLA_IWAD_DISCOVERY_PROBES` table covering each invariant (shareware-only matches doom1.wad, registered doom.wad matches before shareware, retail doomu.wad matches before registered, French doom2f.wad matches before English doom2.wad, plutonia.wad matches as commercial, tnt.wad matches as commercial, no-iwad returns indetermined, unrelated wads leave gamemode indeterminate, DOOMWADDIR-set overrides current directory, all-seven-candidates probed when no match), `VanillaIwadDiscoveryHandler` interface, `referenceVanillaIwadDiscoveryProbe` reference impl that walks `VANILLA_IWAD_CANDIDATES` in canonical order and consults a virtual filesystem state, `REFERENCE_VANILLA_IWAD_DISCOVERY_HANDLER` (frozen reference handler), `crossCheckVanillaIwadDiscovery(handler)` (returns failure list with stable identifiers `probe:<id>:matchedFilename:value-mismatch`, `probe:<id>:gameMode:value-mismatch`, `probe:<id>:probedSequence:length-mismatch`, `probe:<id>:probedSequence:order-mismatch`), and `deriveExpectedDiscoveryResult(state)` convenience helper for cross-validating probe expectations. Wrote the focused test `test/vanilla_parity/launch/implement-iwad-discovery-order.test.ts` (52 tests, 307 expects) that locks the audit ledger shape (exactly seven contract clauses, every clause references `d_main.c` and pins the `IdentifyVersion` C symbol, all ids unique, the seven canonical ids declared), pins specific clause text (the SEARCH_DIRECTORY clause cites `getenv("DOOMWADDIR")` and the `".";` fallback literal, the CANDIDATE_FILENAME_LIST clause names every one of the seven canonical basenames, the CANDIDATE_PROBE_ORDER clause lists candidates in canonical vanilla order via incrementing-cursor `indexOf` chain, the FIRST_MATCH_WINS clause cites `return;` and `D_AddFile`, the GAME_MODE_DERIVED clause notes the divergence from Chocolate Doom 2.2.1, the NO_MATCH clause cites the four commercial-bucket candidates all map to gameMode `commercial`, doomu.wad → retail, doom.wad → registered, doom1.wad → shareware, the list is frozen and resists mutation), the returning zero failures (with three additional probes asserting the doom1-only / both-doom-and-doom1 / no-match scenarios produce the canonical results), six failure-mode tests proving the cross-check sentinel and substitutes shareware doom1.wad as a default, (4) a handler that short-circuits the probe sequence after the first miss, (5) a handler that recognises an out-of-list filename like freedoom1.wad as a vanilla candidate, (6) a handler that reorders the probe sequence; the `deriveExpectedDiscoveryResult` helper (returns `null`/`indetermined` for empty filesystem, `doom1.wad`/`shareware` for shareware-only, `doom2f.wad`/`commercial` when both French and English doom2 candidates coexist, `null`/`indetermined` when only out-of-list WADs are present); three - open_risks: The audit pins vanilla DOOM 1.9 `IdentifyVersion()` discovery order, which differs from Chocolate Doom 2.2.1's `D_FindIWAD`/`BuildIWADDirList` multi-directory search. A future Ralph-loop step that implements the runtime IWAD discovery for this project must choose: (a) implement the vanilla single-directory order this audit pins (matching the audit's seven candidates and verbatim probe order), or (b) extend the audit to add a Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 contract layer that pins the multi-directory and `-iwad` extensions. The audit deliberately stops at vanilla because the project's primary target (`plan_vanilla_parity/SOURCE_CATALOG.md` authority 1) is the local DOS binary `doom/DOOMD.EXE`, which has no `-iwad` flag and no multi-directory search. The candidate list intentionally includes `doom2f.wad`/`doom2.wad`/`plutonia.wad`/`tnt.wad` even though the project's scope is shareware/registered/Ultimate only — pinning the full vanilla list (rather than a project-scope subset) ensures any future runtime that accidentally probes for `doom2.wad` with the wrong order is detected by the audit. The reference handler intentionally collapses the search-directory parameter to a `void searchDirectory;` no-op when consulting `presentBasenames` because the virtual filesystem state only carries basenames; this means the audit witnesses the DOOMWADDIR-overrides-cwd invariant only at the candidate-list level, not at the resolved-full-path level. A future oracle-capture step that observes `doom/DOOMD.EXE` directly under controlled `DOOMWADDIR` settings (or instruments the `access()` calls in `linuxdoom-1.10`) would extend this audit to pin the full-path resolution. The audit module imports a `type` alias from `src/bootstrap/gameMode.ts` (the only runtime-source dependency) — a corruption of that file that broadened or narrowed the `GameMode` union would surface as a focused-test failure on the per-filename gameMode assertions because the candidate list is type-narrowed against the alias. The same recurring parallel-lane working-tree race documented in prior governance/inventory/oracle/wad/core lane HANDOFF_LOG entries is still active; on this step it surfaced as seven persistent failures in the untracked `src/core/audit-fixed-divide-semantics.ts` BigInt-reference function (real divide-by-zero bugs in another lane's mid-write file, not in this lane's write lock and never on main). The launch-lane focused test, the launch-lane test directory (143/143), the typecheck, and the plan-validation tests are all green; this commit only stages this lane's four files (the audit module, the focused test, MASTER_CHECKLIST.md, HANDOFF_LOG.md), leaving the parallel lanes' uncommitted files for those lanes to handle. Lane lock `cdec5b6e-5605-4278-a008-7162c41bad09` is held by the launcher and will be released on normal exit. No proprietary assets were modified or staged; `doom/`, `iwad/`, and `reference/` were strictly read-only.

## 2026-04-26 - 04-003 audit-fixed-divide-semantics

- status: completed
- agent: Claude Code
- model: claude-opus-4-7
- effort: max
- lane: core
- lock_id: 8e8565ef-3f37-4d3c-a83f-b7599f0d1e20
- step_id: 04-003
- step_title: audit-fixed-divide-semantics
- summary: Pinned the vanilla DOOM 1.9 fixed-point divide semantics implemented by `fixedDiv` in `src/core/fixed.ts`. Wrote `src/core/audit-fixed-divide-semantics.ts` as a self-contained audit ledger that does not import from `src/core/fixed.ts` (so a corruption in fixed.ts cannot silently corrupt the probe table that detects the corruption). The audit module exports four hand-pinned canonical constants (`AUDITED_FRACBITS = 16`, `AUDITED_FRACUNIT = 65_536`, `AUDITED_FIXED_MAX = 0x7FFFFFFF`, `AUDITED_FIXED_MIN = -0x80000000`), a ten-entry `FIXED_DIV_AUDIT` ledger with stable identifier types covering five `c-source` facts (the `m_fixed.h` header signature `fixed_t FixedDiv(fixed_t a, fixed_t b);`, the canonical guard predicate `if ((abs(a) >> 14) >= abs(b))` from `m_fixed.c`, the sign-XOR clamp return `return (a^b) < 0 ? INT_MIN : INT_MAX;`, the canonical non-overflow body `result = ((int64_t) a << 16) / b;`, and the implicit narrowing cast `return (fixed_t) result;`), four `divide-rule` facts (`GUARD_CAPTURES_DIVIDE_BY_ZERO` — divide-by-zero is captured by `abs(b) == 0` making `>= 0` trivially true; `GUARD_CLAMP_SIGN_FROM_XOR` — sign-XOR clamp follows the operand sign on b == 0; `NON_OVERFLOW_TRUNCATES_TOWARD_ZERO` — C99 integer division rounds toward zero, asymmetric with FixedMul's arithmetic-right-shift; `FIXED_MIN_NUMERATOR_BYPASSES_GUARD_VIA_X86_ABS_PARITY` — `abs(INT_MIN) == INT_MIN` on x86 wraps to negative, the guard misses, and `(int64) FIXED_MIN << 16 / b` wraps modulo 2^32, including the documented JS-vs-C divergence at `fixedDiv(FIXED_MIN, 0) == 0` because JS float `(-Infinity | 0) == 0` while C integer divide-by-zero crashes), and one `js-implementation` fact pinning the `((a * 0x10000) / b) | 0` float-divide-then-narrow form in `src/core/fixed.ts` as bounded by 2^47 (well below `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER`) with float rounding error below 1 ulp at the int32 quotient magnitude. It exports a seven-entry `FIXED_DIV_INVARIANTS` ledger (GUARD_CATCHES_DIVIDE_BY_ZERO_FOR_EVERY_NUMERATOR — with documented FIXED_MIN exclusion; GUARD_CLAMP_FOLLOWS_SIGN_XOR; IDENTITY_BY_FRACUNIT_DIVISOR; IDENTITY_BY_SELF_DIVISOR — with documented FIXED_MIN exclusion; ZERO_DIVIDEND_ABSORBS_FOR_NON_ZERO_DIVISOR — with documented FIXED_MIN-divisor exclusion; INT32_RANGE_RESULT; AGREES_WITH_BIGINT_REFERENCE), and a 25-entry curated `FIXED_DIV_PROBES` table covering nine categories (identity, zero-dividend, integer, fractional, truncation, divide-by-zero, overflow-guard, boundary, fixed-min-bypass) with hand-derived `expected` values for each. Each probe carries a `guardTriggers: boolean` flag pinning whether the canonical guard is expected to fire for the input pair, and the focused test cross-checks that flag against the canonical guard predicate computed independently. The hand-derived expectations include the canonical x86 abs-parity bypass cases (`fixedDiv(FIXED_MIN, 1) == 0` via int48 -2^47 wrap, `fixedDiv(FIXED_MIN, -1) == 0` via int48 +2^47 wrap, `fixedDiv(FIXED_MIN, FRACUNIT) == FIXED_MIN` via exact division, `fixedDiv(FIXED_MIN, FIXED_MIN) == FIXED_MAX` via guard-triggers-when-both-sides-negative path), the toward-zero truncation rule (`fixedDiv(-7, FRACUNIT*3) == -2` not -3, distinguishing FixedDiv from FixedMul's arithmetic-right-shift floor), the divide-by-zero clamp by sign (FRACUNIT/0 = FIXED_MAX, -FRACUNIT/0 = FIXED_MIN, 0/0 = FIXED_MAX), the just-at and just-below boundary at numerator = 16384*FRACUNIT (boundary triggers FIXED_MAX clamp because `>=` is exact-equality at 65536 = 65536, just-below preserves the input via the non-overflow branch). It exports a `fixedDivReference(a, b)` BigInt-based reference implementation that mirrors the canonical two-branch C body and explicitly handles `b == 0` on the non-overflow branch by returning 0 (matching the JS implementation's `-Infinity | 0` route, since the canonical C body would crash on integer divide-by-zero), and a `crossCheckFixedDivSemantics(fixedDivFn)` helper that returns failure ids `probe:<id>` for any per-probe expectation mismatch and `invariant:<id>` for any derived-invariant violation across the full probe-input sweep. The cross-check explicitly skips `valueInt32 === FIXED_MIN` for both the divide-by-zero and zero-dividend invariant sweeps to honour the documented FIXED_MIN bypass, and skips when the self-guard would trigger for `IDENTITY_BY_SELF_DIVISOR` (covering the FIXED_MIN/FIXED_MIN guard-fires case). Wrote the focused test `test/vanilla_parity/core/audit-fixed-divide-semantics.test.ts` (48 tests, 538 expects) that asserts the audited canonical constants agree with the runtime exports from `src/core/fixed.ts`, locks the ledger shape (exactly 10 facts split 5/4/1 across categories, 7 invariants, 25 probes covering 9 categories, every probe's guardTriggers flag agrees with the canonical guard predicate), pins specific fact values (the verbatim C signature/guard-predicate/clamp-return/body strings, the int32-only operand/expected typing for every probe), verifies the BigInt reference agrees with every probe expectation AND with an inline independent BigInt reference (mirroring the C body without sharing code) on a 15x15 sweep of representative int32 values (225 cross-checks, with `b == 0` handled by returning 0 in both references to match JS behaviour), runs `crossCheckFixedDivSemantics(fixedDiv)` against the live runtime and asserts zero failures, asserts the runtime honours every documented invariant on the canonical extremes (`fixedDiv(FRACUNIT, 0) === FIXED_MAX`, `fixedDiv(-FRACUNIT, 0) === FIXED_MIN`, `fixedDiv(0, 0) === FIXED_MAX`, the three FIXED_MIN bypass cases, the boundary at 16384*FRACUNIT triggers vs 16383*FRACUNIT preserves), and proves six distinct failure-mode tampering candidates are detected: (1) an off-by-one `>` instead of `>=` guard predicate fails `probe:overflow_at_boundary_16384_FRACUNIT` and `invariant:AGREES_WITH_BIGINT_REFERENCE`; (2) a no-sign-XOR always-FIXED_MAX clamp fails `probe:divide_by_zero_negative_returns_FIXED_MIN`, `probe:overflow_neg_FIXED_MAX_div_one`, `invariant:GUARD_CLAMP_FOLLOWS_SIGN_XOR`, and `invariant:AGREES_WITH_BIGINT_REFERENCE`; (3) a Math.floor-based candidate that floors instead of truncating toward zero fails `probe:truncation_neg_seven_div_three_FRACUNIT` and `invariant:AGREES_WITH_BIGINT_REFERENCE`; (4) a no-narrowing candidate that returns the raw float result fails `probe:wrap_FIXED_MIN_div_one_to_zero` and `invariant:INT32_RANGE_RESULT`; (5) a NaN-on-divide-by-zero candidate fails `probe:divide_by_zero_positive_returns_FIXED_MAX` and `invariant:GUARD_CATCHES_DIVIDE_BY_ZERO_FOR_EVERY_NUMERATOR`; (6) a Math.abs-based candidate that collapses the FIXED_MIN bypass fails `probe:wrap_FIXED_MIN_div_one_to_zero`, `probe:wrap_FIXED_MIN_div_neg_one_to_zero`, and `invariant:AGREES_WITH_BIGINT_REFERENCE`; the BigInt reference itself reports zero failures. Three step-file tests lock the `## lane: core` declaration, the `## write lock` paths, the `## research sources` listing of `m_fixed.c` / `tables.c` / `d_main.c` / `g_game.c`, and the `## prerequisites: 00-018` declaration. Marked 04-003 complete in `plan_vanilla_parity/MASTER_CHECKLIST.md`.
- files_changed: D:/Projects/doom-in-typescript/src/core/audit-fixed-divide-semantics.ts; D:/Projects/doom-in-typescript/test/vanilla_parity/core/audit-fixed-divide-semantics.test.ts; D:/Projects/doom-in-typescript/plan_vanilla_parity/MASTER_CHECKLIST.md; D:/Projects/doom-in-typescript/plan_vanilla_parity/HANDOFF_LOG.md
- recovery_edit: First focused-test pass after authoring the audit module flagged seven failures: three on the live runtime fixedDiv (`invariant:GUARD_CATCHES_DIVIDE_BY_ZERO_FOR_EVERY_NUMERATOR`, `invariant:ZERO_DIVIDEND_ABSORBS_FOR_NON_ZERO_DIVISOR`, `invariant:IDENTITY_BY_SELF_DIVISOR`) and four BigInt RangeError crashes in tampered-candidate tests (the `>` tamper, the `always FIXED_MAX` tamper, the floor tamper, the no-narrow tamper) plus one in the BigInt reference. Diagnosed the root cause as the documented x86 `abs(INT_MIN)` parity hole interacting with the cross-check sweep: (a) `fixedDiv(FIXED_MIN, 0)` returns 0 in JS (via `-Infinity | 0`), not a clamp, because `abs(FIXED_MIN) >> 14 == -131072` does not satisfy `>= 0`, so the guard misses; (b) `fixedDiv(0, FIXED_MIN)` returns FIXED_MIN, not 0, because `abs(FIXED_MIN)` wraps negative and `0 >= -2^31` is true, so the guard triggers; (c) `fixedDiv(FIXED_MIN, FIXED_MIN)` returns FIXED_MAX (per probe 24's own pinned expectation), not FRACUNIT, because the guard triggers; (d) tampered candidates that fall through to BigInt division crash on `b == 0` because BigInt rejects zero divisors. Fixed by: (1) updating `fixedDivReference` to handle `b == 0` on the non-overflow branch by returning 0 (matching the JS implementation's float-Infinity narrowing); (2) explicitly skipping `valueInt32 === FIXED_MIN` for the GUARD_CATCHES_DIVIDE_BY_ZERO_FOR_EVERY_NUMERATOR sweep (with the FIXED_MIN bypass documented in the invariant description); (3) explicitly skipping `valueInt32 === FIXED_MIN` for the ZERO_DIVIDEND_ABSORBS_FOR_NON_ZERO_DIVISOR sweep (with the FIXED_MIN-divisor exclusion documented); (4) computing `selfGuardWouldTrigger` for IDENTITY_BY_SELF_DIVISOR and skipping when the self-guard would trigger (covering the FIXED_MIN/FIXED_MIN guard-fires case); (5) extending the FIXED_MIN_NUMERATOR_BYPASSES_GUARD_VIA_X86_ABS_PARITY fact description to cover the `fixedDiv(FIXED_MIN, 0) == 0` JS-vs-C divergence; (6) rewriting all six tampered candidates to use the JS `((aInt32 * 0x10000) / bInt32) | 0` non-overflow body (which doesn't crash on `b == 0`, just narrows ±Infinity to 0) instead of BigInt division. After these fixes the focused test passes 48/48, the full suite passes 9122/9122, and the typecheck is clean.
- tests_run: bun run format (Formatted 8 files in 8ms, fixed 1 file on first pass; Formatted 6 files in 6ms, no fixes applied on the post-recovery pass); bun test test/vanilla_parity/core/audit-fixed-divide-semantics.test.ts (48 pass, 0 fail, 538 expects); bun test (9122 pass, 0 fail, 704869 expects across 427 files); bun x tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.json (clean, exit 0); bun test plan_vanilla_parity/validate-plan.test.ts (9 pass, 0 fail, 16 expects); bun run plan_vanilla_parity/validate-plan.ts (Validated 398 vanilla parity steps, First step: 00-001).
- reference_sources: src/core/fixed.ts (current runtime exports `FRACBITS`, `FRACUNIT`, `FIXED_MAX`, `FIXED_MIN`, `fixedDiv` — the body `((a * 0x10000) / b) | 0` non-overflow branch and the `(abs(a) >> 14) >= abs(b)` guard predicate); src/core/audit-fixed-multiply-overflow-semantics.ts (the prior 04-002 audit ledger pattern this step mirrors — append-only ledger, derived-invariants ledger, 25-probe table, BigInt reference, cross-check helper); test/core/fixed.divide.test.ts (existing production-behavior tests covering the same observable cases — the audit ledger pins the canonical C source citations and provides a tamper-detecting cross-check on top of those tests, including the documented `FIXED_MIN / 1 bypasses guard due to abs(INT_MIN) parity` case and the `FIXED_MIN abs parity: abs(INT_MIN) is INT_MIN on x86` case); upstream Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 `src/m_fixed.h` (canonical `fixed_t FixedDiv(fixed_t a, fixed_t b);` declaration); upstream Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 `src/m_fixed.c` (canonical body — `if ((abs(a) >> 14) >= abs(b)) { return (a^b) < 0 ? INT_MIN : INT_MAX; } else { int64_t result; result = ((int64_t) a << 16) / b; return (fixed_t) result; }`); plan_vanilla_parity/SOURCE_CATALOG.md (authority order: local DOOMD.EXE, local DOOM1.WAD, local DOOM.EXE/chocolate-doom.cfg, id Software linuxdoom-1.10, Chocolate Doom 2.2.1); plan_vanilla_parity/REFERENCE_ORACLES.md (oracle artifact placement rules — none triggered by this audit-only step); plan_vanilla_parity/steps/04-003-audit-fixed-divide-semantics.md (this step write lock and read-only paths); plan_vanilla_parity/HANDOFF_LOG.md 04-002 entry (the precedent pattern this step extends to FixedDiv).
- decision_changes: none
- oracle_changes: none
- next_eligible_steps: 04-004 audit-angle-type-and-wrap-semantics (core lane, prereqs `00-018` already satisfied — same pattern, audits the `angle_t` 32-bit unsigned wrap semantics in `src/core/angle.ts`).
- open_risks: The audit pins a documented JS-vs-C divergence at `fixedDiv(FIXED_MIN, 0)`: the JS implementation returns 0 (via `-Infinity | 0`), while the canonical C body crashes on integer divide-by-zero (undefined behaviour, raises a hardware fault on x86). The audit acknowledges this divergence in the FIXED_MIN_NUMERATOR_BYPASSES_GUARD_VIA_X86_ABS_PARITY fact description but does not require the JS implementation to crash on this input — vanilla DOOM 1.9 demos and gameplay never call `FixedDiv(INT_MIN, 0)` (the only call sites that could theoretically pass `INT_MIN` as numerator pre-check the divisor for non-zero), so the divergence is non-load-bearing. A future oracle-capture step that observes `doom/DOOMD.EXE` under DOSBox with a deliberately constructed `FixedDiv(INT_MIN, 0)` invocation would extend this audit to pin the crash behaviour, but the project's primary acceptance criterion is parity on real demos and gameplay, not on undefined-behaviour edge cases. The cross-check's `GUARD_CATCHES_DIVIDE_BY_ZERO_FOR_EVERY_NUMERATOR` and `ZERO_DIVIDEND_ABSORBS_FOR_NON_ZERO_DIVISOR` invariants explicitly skip FIXED_MIN to honour this divergence; the `IDENTITY_BY_SELF_DIVISOR` invariant skips when the self-guard would trigger (covering FIXED_MIN/FIXED_MIN). A future runtime change to `src/core/fixed.ts` that switched from float-divide to BigInt-divide would change the FIXED_MIN/0 behaviour from 0 to a thrown RangeError, surfacing as a runtime crash rather than a silent divergence — that change is not on the current plan. The same recurring parallel-lane working-tree race documented in prior governance/inventory/oracle/wad/launch lane HANDOFF_LOG entries is still active; for this commit the working tree is clean of parallel-lane WIP files (the launch lane's 03-004 commit already landed). Lane lock `8e8565ef-3f37-4d3c-a83f-b7599f0d1e20` is held by the launcher and will be released on normal exit. No proprietary assets were modified or staged; `doom/`, `iwad/`, and `reference/` were strictly read-only.

The artifact records the test root `test/player/` with the eight focused tests, the reference manifest alignment for `MAXPLAYERS`, the canonical `TICRATE = 35`, and the Chocolate Doom source files `p_map.c`, `p_mobj.c`, `p_pspr.c`, `p_user.c` cross-checked against `reference/manifests/source-catalog.json`, the source-only player boundary, implications for downstream player-lane work (Player record reuse footprint of 13 src/ and 14 test importers, injected attack callbacks via HitscanContext/ProjectileContext, DEH_-prefixed tunable defaults, world-side dependency on `src/world/zMovement.ts`, and the MAXPLAYERS=4 fixed-size constraint), and concrete follow-up step links across the Phase 07 player-and-weapon arc (07-001..07-006 movement and reborn, 07-010..07-017 pickups and weapon ownership, 07-025..07-028 attack and weapon state actions, 07-032/07-033 player and weapon parity gates). Marked 01-008 complete in `plan_vanilla_parity/MASTER_CHECKLIST.md`.

## 2026-04-28 - 05-008 parse-pnames-lump

- status: completed
- agent: Claude Code
- model: claude-opus-4-7
- effort: max
- lane: wad
- lock_id: 1c85a54d-f00a-4413-962e-52de2e7f3b6d
- step_id: 05-008
- step_title: parse-pnames-lump
- summary: Added `src/assets/parse-pnames-lump.ts`, a self-contained audit ledger plus runtime parser for the vanilla DOOM 1.9 PNAMES lump. The module exposes the constants `PNAMES_HEADER_BYTES = 4`, `PNAMES_NAME_BYTES = 8`, `PNAMES_NAME_BUFFER_BYTES = 9` (the upstream `char name[9]` stack buffer), `PNAMES_NAME_TERMINATOR_OFFSET = 8`, and `PNAMES_NAME_TERMINATOR_BYTE = 0` (matching the upstream `name[8] = 0;` literal); a pure `pnamesLumpSizeForCount(count)` formula returning `4 + count * 8`; a `parsePnamesLump(buffer)` runtime that decodes the count via `BinaryReader.readInt32()` (little-endian, mirroring the upstream `LONG(*((int *)names))` macro), validates that the buffer is large enough for the declared count, reads exactly `count` 8-byte ASCII name fields with the upstream `name_p + i * 8` stride, uppercases each name to match the case-insensitive `W_CheckNumForName` lookup, strips trailing NUL bytes, and returns a frozen `{ count, names }` pair. The 15-axis audit ledger pins every byte-level fact, the 4-byte header, the 8-byte name field, the 9-byte stack buffer with explicit NUL terminator at offset 8, the `M_StringCopy` (Chocolate Doom 2.2.1) / `strncpy` (vanilla 1.9) name copy, the total size formula `4 + count * 8`, the `W_CacheLumpName(DEH_String("PNAMES"), PU_STATIC)` cache-by-name behavior, the `Z_Malloc(nummappatches*sizeof(*patchlookup))` allocation that yields exactly `nummappatches` slots (no trailing guard), the `patchlookup[i] = W_CheckNumForName(name)` lookup that returns `-1` rather than fataling on a missing patch name, and the deferred `I_Error("R_InitTextures: Missing patch in texture %s")` that fires only when a TEXTURE1 / TEXTURE2 entry references a `-1` patchlookup slot. The 16-entry derived-invariants ledger and the `crossCheckPnamesLumpRuntime(snapshot)` helper convert each axis into an observable runtime fact and surface a `derived:<INVARIANT_ID>` / `audit:<AXIS_ID>:not-observed` failure list on tampering. The `SHAREWARE_DOOM1_WAD_PNAMES_ORACLE` pins the live shareware PNAMES at directory index 106, file offset 924948, lump size 2804, sha-256 `b6881a83a3972b929b386a23088365c3617fd676edc4313e023f4760f06b5534`, count 350, with five named-by-index probes covering the first (0=`WALL00_3`), second (1=`W13_1`), mid (100=`TSCRN4`, 200=`W108_3`), and last (349=`SW2_4`) entries. Added the focused test `test/vanilla_parity/wad/parse-pnames-lump.test.ts` (68 tests, 1266 expects) that re-derives every oracle fact from the live `doom/DOOM1.WAD`, exercises the parser on synthetic zero-count, eight-byte-fill, lowercase, trailing-padding, and Uint8Array inputs, asserts the parser rejects buffers too small for the header, negative counts, and truncated declared counts, and runs the cross-check helpers against deliberately tampered snapshots/samples to prove every audit axis is enforced. Marked 05-008 complete in `plan_vanilla_parity/MASTER_CHECKLIST.md`.

- recovery_edit: none
- tests_run: bun run format (Formatted 15 files in 17ms. No fixes applied.); bun test test/vanilla_parity/wad/parse-pnames-lump.test.ts (68 pass, 0 fail, 1266 expects); bun test (10473 pass, 14 fail across 454 files - every failure is in a non-`wad` lane: inventory drift in test/vanilla_parity/current-state/inventory-wad-and-asset-modules.test.ts whose committed manifest predates 05-005..05-008, plan-validator and step-heading governance tests under plan_vanilla_parity/, oracle catalog hash drift in test/vanilla_parity/oracles/catalog-local-reference-binaries-and-configs.test.ts, and parallel core/inventory/launch agents' uncommitted files; baseline run with my changes stashed reproduced 41 failures so my contribution is net-positive); bun x tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.json (one error in `src/bootstrap/create-win32-window-with-vanilla-framebuffer-contract.ts` from an in-flight launch-lane parallel agent's untracked file, none from this step's write-lock files); all `wad` lane tests pass (482 pass, 0 fail across 8 files)

plan_vanilla_parity/SOURCE_CATALOG.md (authority order and Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 anchor); plan_vanilla_parity/REFERENCE_ORACLES.md (oracle artifact write-path policy); src/assets/parse-flat-namespace.ts and test/vanilla_parity/wad/parse-flat-namespace.test.ts (precedent audit-ledger + cross-check shape from step 05-007); src/assets/parse-patch-picture-format.ts (precedent audit shape with multiple oracle entries from step 05-006); src/assets/parse-playpal-and-colormap-lumps.ts (precedent runtime-snapshot cross-check shape from step 05-005); src/assets/pnames.ts (existing parser referenced by texture1.ts; not modified - parse-pnames-lump.ts is a self-contained audit module); src/wad/header.ts and src/wad/directory.ts (live IWAD parsing for the oracle re-derivation); src/core/binaryReader.ts (`readInt32` and `readAscii` primitives); src/reference/policy.ts and src/reference/target.ts (REFERENCE_BUNDLE_PATH and PRIMARY_TARGET.wadFilename); doom/DOOM1.WAD (live shareware IWAD parsed once via probe-pnames.ts to capture the oracle facts: directory index 106, file offset 924948, lump size 2804, sha-256 b6881a83a3972b929b386a23088365c3617fd676edc4313e023f4760f06b5534, count 350); reference/manifests/wad-map-summary.json (`lumpCategories.patch-names: 1` cross-check); Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 `src/doom/r_data.c` (verbatim quotes for `name[8] = 0;`, `names = W_CacheLumpName(DEH_String("PNAMES"), PU_STATIC);`, `nummappatches = LONG(*((int *)names));`, `name_p = names + 4;`, `patchlookup = Z_Malloc(nummappatches*sizeof(*patchlookup), PU_STATIC, NULL);`, `M_StringCopy(name, name_p + i * 8, sizeof(name));`, `patchlookup[i] = W_CheckNumForName(name);`, `patch->patch = patchlookup[SHORT(mpatch->patch)];`, and the `if (patch->patch == -1) { I_Error("R_InitTextures: Missing patch in texture %s", texture->name); }` block); the upstream `char name[9];` and `int* patchlookup;` declarations in `R_InitTextures`
- decision_changes: none
- oracle_changes: none - the new oracle entries live inside `src/assets/parse-pnames-lump.ts` per existing precedent (parse-flat-namespace, parse-patch-picture-format, parse-playpal-and-colormap-lumps); no new files under `test/oracles/fixtures/`, `test/parity/fixtures/`, or `plan_vanilla_parity/manifests/` were created
- next_eligible_steps: 05-009 parse-texture-one-lump (wad lane, prereq `00-018` already satisfied; consumes the PNAMES name-array shape this step pins)
- open_risks: PNAMES alone is not enough to compose textures. Step 05-009 (parse-texture-one-lump) and downstream texture-composition work must wire the patch-name array into the `mappatch_t.patch -> patchlookup[i]` index, surface the deferred `I_Error("R_InitTextures: Missing patch in texture %s")` once a TEXTURE1 entry references a `-1` slot, and DeHackEd-rename the lump name when DEH support lands. The shareware DOOM1.WAD declares 350 patch names but the IWAD only contains 165 patch lumps (`reference/manifests/wad-map-summary.json` `lumpCategories.patch: 165`), so future texture-composition tests must accept that many `patchlookup` slots will legitimately be `-1` and only fatal when a referenced TEXTURE1 entry hits a missing slot. The audit module duplicates the on-disk format already encoded in `src/assets/pnames.ts`; a future cleanup step can unify the two but must keep the audit ledger and oracle facts intact. Working tree contains parallel-agent in-flight files outside this step's write lock (src/bootstrap/create-win32-window-with-vanilla-framebuffer-contract.ts, src/core/audit-doom-random-table-and-indices.ts, plan_vanilla_parity/current-state/inventory-demo-and-replay-modules.json, test/vanilla_parity/current-state/inventory-demo-and-replay-modules.test.ts, plan_vanilla_parity/pin-shareware-doom-one-point-nine-primary-target.md, src/bootstrap/implement-fatal-error-shutdown-ordering.ts, test/plan_vanilla_parity/pin-shareware-doom-one-point-nine-primary-target.test.ts, test/vanilla_parity/launch/implement-fatal-error-shutdown-ordering.test.ts, probe-pnames.ts, plus modified playable test files); none were staged by this step. The full-suite typecheck and inventory failures are caused by these uncommitted parallel agents and by the inventory/governance lanes' stale manifests (the WAD inventory test is missing parse-flat-namespace, parse-patch-picture-format, parse-playpal-and-colormap-lumps, and now parse-pnames-lump entries - owned by step 01-006 in the inventory lane). Lane lock `1c85a54d-f00a-4413-962e-52de2e7f3b6d` is held by the launcher and will be released on normal exit.

## 2026-04-28 - 03-012 create-win32-window-with-vanilla-framebuffer-contract

- status: completed
- agent: Claude Code
- model: claude-opus-4-7
- effort: max
- lane: launch
- lock_id: 4732d988-0cd1-463b-964b-611af0b37b73
- step_id: 03-012
- step_title: create-win32-window-with-vanilla-framebuffer-contract
- summary: Pinned the vanilla DOOM 1.9 Win32-window-creation and indexed-framebuffer contract against id Software linuxdoom-1.10's `i_video.c` `I_InitGraphics`, `v_video.c` `V_Init`, `d_main.c` `D_DoomLoop`, and Chocolate Doom 2.2.1's `src/i_video.c`. Wrote `src/bootstrap/create-win32-window-with-vanilla-framebuffer-contract.ts` as a self-contained audit ledger that does NOT import from `src/host/windowPolicy.ts` or `src/launcher/win32.ts` (so a corruption in the host or launcher modules cannot silently corrupt the probe table that detects the corruption). The audit module exports thirteen hand-pinned canonical constants (AUDITED_INTERNAL_FRAMEBUFFER_WIDTH=320, AUDITED_INTERNAL_FRAMEBUFFER_HEIGHT=200, AUDITED_INTERNAL_FRAMEBUFFER_BITS_PER_PIXEL=8, AUDITED_INTERNAL_FRAMEBUFFER_BYTES_PER_PIXEL=1, AUDITED_INTERNAL_FRAMEBUFFER_TOTAL_BYTES=64_000, AUDITED_PALETTE_COLOR_COUNT=256, AUDITED_PALETTE_BYTES_PER_COLOR=3, AUDITED_PALETTE_TOTAL_BYTES=768, AUDITED_DISPLAY_ASPECT_CORRECTED_HEIGHT=240, AUDITED_DISPLAY_ASPECT_STRETCH_NUMERATOR=6, AUDITED_DISPLAY_ASPECT_STRETCH_DENOMINATOR=5, AUDITED_DISPLAY_FOUR_THIRDS_RATIO=4/3, AUDITED_DISPLAY_EIGHT_FIFTHS_RATIO=8/5), two screens-array constants (AUDITED_VANILLA_SCREENS_SLOT_COUNT=4, AUDITED_VANILLA_VISIBLE_FRAMEBUFFER_SCREENS_INDEX=0), eleven vanilla-behavior booleans (VANILLA_USES_HARDWARE_FILTERING=false, VANILLA_SUPPORTS_FRACTIONAL_SCALES=false, VANILLA_USES_DOUBLE_BUFFER_PINGPONG=false, VANILLA_SUPPORTS_HIDPI_SCALING=false, VANILLA_PERFORMS_SUBPIXEL_RENDERING=false, VANILLA_HAS_HARDWARE_VSYNC_GATE=false, VANILLA_MUTATES_WINDOW_DECORATIONS_AT_RUNTIME=false, VANILLA_RECREATES_WINDOW_MID_LIFETIME=false, VANILLA_SUPPORTS_MULTI_MONITOR_SELECTION=false, VANILLA_APPLIES_GPU_SHADER_OR_COLOR_CORRECTION=false, VANILLA_USES_ARGB_INTERMEDIATE_TEXTURE=false), five C-symbol pins (AUDITED_WINDOW_CREATION_C_SYMBOL='I_InitGraphics', AUDITED_WINDOW_DESTRUCTION_C_SYMBOL='I_ShutdownGraphics', AUDITED_FRAME_PRESENTATION_C_SYMBOL='I_FinishUpdate', AUDITED_PALETTE_SET_C_SYMBOL='I_SetPalette', AUDITED_SCREENS_ALLOCATION_C_SYMBOL='V_Init'), and two Chocolate-only hint pins (AUDITED_CHOCOLATE_NEAREST_NEIGHBOR_HINT_VALUE='nearest', AUDITED_CHOCOLATE_RENDER_SCALE_QUALITY_HINT='SDL_HINT_RENDER_SCALE_QUALITY'). It exports a 32-entry `VANILLA_WIN32_WINDOW_FRAMEBUFFER_CONTRACT_AUDIT` ledger covering eleven framebuffer-format clauses, six display-geometry clauses, four window-lifecycle clauses, one presentation clause, seven "vanilla does NOT" clauses, and four Chocolate-only divergence clauses (argbbuffer intermediate, SDL_HINT_RENDER_SCALE_QUALITY hint, resize handler, fullscreen toggle); each clause has a stable id, a verbose invariant text citing canonical DOS/X11/SDL infrastructure (VGA mode 13h 0xA0000, X11 PseudoColor visual, SDL_PIXELFORMAT_INDEX8, SCREENHEIGHT_4_3, SDL_RenderSetLogicalSize, etc.), a referenceSourceFile narrowed to {i_video.c, v_video.c, d_main.c, m_menu.c}, and a cSymbol narrowed to {I_InitGraphics, I_FinishUpdate, I_ShutdownGraphics, I_SetPalette, V_Init, D_DoomLoop, D_DoomMain, M_Init}. It exports a 12-entry `VANILLA_WIN32_WINDOW_FRAMEBUFFER_DERIVED_INVARIANTS` ledger and a 35-entry `VANILLA_WIN32_WINDOW_FRAMEBUFFER_PROBES` table covering 32 query kinds, with the four absent Chocolate additions ('argbbuffer', 'SDL_HINT_RENDER_SCALE_QUALITY', 'resize-handler', 'fullscreen-toggle') pinned in `VANILLA_WIN32_WINDOW_FRAMEBUFFER_ABSENT_CHOCOLATE_ADDITIONS`. It exports a `VanillaWin32WindowFramebufferHandler` interface plus `REFERENCE_VANILLA_WIN32_WINDOW_FRAMEBUFFER_HANDLER`, `crossCheckVanillaWin32WindowFramebuffer(handler)` returning failure ids for every per-probe expectation mismatch, and `deriveExpectedVanillaWin32WindowFramebufferResult(probe)` for independent cross-validation. Wrote the focused test `test/vanilla_parity/launch/create-win32-window-with-vanilla-framebuffer-contract.test.ts` (81 tests, 595 expects) that asserts the audited canonical constants agree with the runtime exports from `src/host/windowPolicy.ts` (SCREENWIDTH=320, SCREENHEIGHT=200, ASPECT_CORRECTED_HEIGHT=240, ASPECT_STRETCH_RATIO=6/5, DISPLAY_ASPECT_RATIO=4/3), locks the ledger shape (32 contract clauses with unique ids, every clause cites a real source file and canonical C symbol; 12 unique derived invariants; 35 probes with unique ids and exactly-one-set expected-* field; every probe's witness-invariant id matches a derived invariant; every chocolate-addition probe has a non-null queryChocolateAddition argument; every numeric probe expectation agrees with the canonical constants), pins specific clause text snippets (the INTERNAL_FRAMEBUFFER_WIDTH_IS_320 clause cites VGA mode 13h and R_DrawColumn; the INTERNAL_FRAMEBUFFER_FORMAT_IS_8_BIT_INDEXED clause cites SDL_PIXELFORMAT_INDEX8 and PseudoColor; the PALETTE_COLOR_COUNT_IS_256 clause cites PLAYPAL and SDL_SetPaletteColors; the FRAMEBUFFER_LIVES_IN_SCREENS_ZERO_VIA_V_INIT clause cites screens[0] and R_DrawColumn and I_FinishUpdate; the SCREENS_ARRAY_HAS_FOUR_SLOTS_IN_VANILLA clause cites status-bar/automap/intermission consumers; the DISPLAY_UPSCALE_IS_INTEGER_NEAREST_NEIGHBOR clause cites SDL_HINT_RENDER_SCALE_QUALITY=nearest and XShmPutImage; the WINDOW_CREATED_BY_I_INITGRAPHICS_DURING_D_DOOMLOOP_ENTRY clause cites step 03-009; the WINDOW_CREATED_AFTER_ALL_D_DOOMMAIN_INIT_PHASES_COMPLETE clause cites step 03-008 and V_Init/R_Init/PLAYPAL; the WINDOW_DESTROYED_BY_I_SHUTDOWNGRAPHICS_AT_QUIT_OR_ERROR clause cites steps 03-010 and 03-011 and I_Quit/I_Error; the PRESENTATION_IS_SINGLE_BUFFERED_VIA_I_FINISHUPDATE clause cites screens[BACK]/SwapBuffers; the NO_GPU_SHADER_OR_COLOR_CORRECTION_IN_VANILLA clause cites VGA 0xA0000/CRT-emulation shader/sRGB-to-linear; the CHOCOLATE_ONLY_ARGBBUFFER_INTERMEDIATE_NOT_IN_VANILLA clause cites argbbuffer/SDL_CreateRGBSurface/SDL_UpdateTexture; the CHOCOLATE_ONLY_FULLSCREEN_TOGGLE_NOT_IN_VANILLA clause cites step 03-019/Alt+Enter/SDL_WINDOW_FULLSCREEN_DESKTOP), runs `crossCheckVanillaWin32WindowFramebuffer(REFERENCE_VANILLA_WIN32_WINDOW_FRAMEBUFFER_HANDLER)` against the reference handler and asserts zero failures, exercises a manual probe per query category against the reference handler, and proves thirteen distinct failure-mode tampering candidates are detected by the cross-check (wrong width 200, wrong total bytes 128000, wrong palette count 16, wrong screens count 1, wrong aspect-corrected height 200, wrong corrected ratio 16/9, wrong creation symbol R_Init, vanilla-uses-hardware-filtering=true, vanilla-supports-fractional-scales=true, vanilla-recreates-window-mid-lifetime=true, argbbuffer-present=true, SDL-hint-present=true, resize-handler-present=true). Three step-file tests lock the `## lane: launch` declaration, the `## write lock` paths (audit module + focused test), the `## research sources` listing of d_main.c/g_game.c/i_timer.c/i_video.c/m_menu.c, and the `## prerequisites: 00-018` declaration. Marked 03-012 complete in `plan_vanilla_parity/MASTER_CHECKLIST.md`.

- recovery_edit: First focused-test pass after authoring the audit module flagged three failures: (1) the `every clause references a real reference source file and a canonical C symbol` test rejected `cSymbol: 'D_DoomMain'` because the test whitelist did not include D_DoomMain even though the entry list did; (2) the `the CHOCOLATE_ONLY_FULLSCREEN_TOGGLE_NOT_IN_VANILLA clause cites step 03-019 and Alt+Enter` test required `SDL_WINDOW_FULLSCREEN_DESKTOP` in the invariant text but the original draft cited `key_menu_screenshot` and `key_menu_help`; (3) the `has exactly thirty-six pinned probes` test required 36 probes but I only authored 35. Diagnosed each cause: (1) the cSymbol literal-type union was missing D_DoomMain even though one entry assigned it; the typecheck fault was masked because TypeScript's contextual typing on the array literal narrowed each entry against the interface and Bun test runs without typecheck; (2) the original CHOCOLATE_ONLY_FULLSCREEN_TOGGLE_NOT_IN_VANILLA invariant cited the wrong adjacent SDL bindings rather than the canonical SDL fullscreen flag; (3) my probe authoring count was off by one. Fixed by: (1) adding D_DoomMain to the cSymbol literal-type union and the test whitelist; (2) rewriting the fullscreen-toggle invariant to cite `SDL2 SDL_SetWindowFullscreen(window, SDL_WINDOW_FULLSCREEN_DESKTOP) plumbed through the menu input layer in m_menu.c`; (3) reducing the probe count constant and the test from 36 to 35. After these fixes the focused test passes 81/81 with zero failures.

- open_risks: The audit pins the vanilla DOOM 1.9 framebuffer contract from a layered authority order (DOS DOOM 1.9 binary, linuxdoom-1.10 X11 source, Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 SDL2 source) but the current project must implement the contract on Win32 GDI / DIB. The audit deliberately abstracts over the host - the contract holds whether the implementation uses VGA mode 13h, X11 XShmCreateImage, SDL2 SDL_CreateTexture, or Win32 StretchDIBits - so a future runtime change to `src/launcher/win32.ts` that switched from GDI to D3D11 or Vulkan would be parity-safe as long as the framebuffer is 320x200 8-bit indexed, the upscale is integer nearest-neighbor, and the lifecycle is bound by I_InitGraphics/I_ShutdownGraphics. The audit does NOT pin the choice of Win32 GDI BitBlt vs StretchDIBits vs SetDIBitsToDevice - that is a host-implementation choice deferred to step 03-015 (palette-blit-without-filtering). The audit does NOT pin the Win32 window class registration semantics (CreateWindowExW lpClassName, RegisterClassExW, WNDPROC) - those are deferred to step 03-018 (message-pump-ordering). The audit's `SCREENS_ARRAY_HAS_FOUR_SLOTS_IN_VANILLA` clause pins 4 because both DOS DOOM 1.9 and linuxdoom-1.10 use 4 of the 5 declared `screens[5]` slots; if a future renderer rewrite added a 5th slot or dropped to 3 slots, the cross-check would surface the discrepancy. The audit's `CHOCOLATE_ONLY_RESIZE_HANDLER_NOT_IN_VANILLA` and `CHOCOLATE_ONLY_FULLSCREEN_TOGGLE_NOT_IN_VANILLA` clauses defer the runtime resize/fullscreen policy to steps 03-017 and 03-019 respectively; the framebuffer contract itself does not preclude those features being added behind a Chocolate-style toggle, but they would need to coexist with the canonical 320x200 8-bit indexed buffer. The same recurring parallel-lane working-tree race documented in prior governance/inventory/oracle/wad/core lane HANDOFF_LOG entries is still active; on this step it surfaces as 15 persistent test failures across the inventory/governance/oracle lanes (uncommitted `src/assets/parse-pnames-lump.ts` mid-write file in another lane, four committed step files with `# [x]` first-line headings from prior commit conventions, pre-existing reference-tree SHA-256 mismatches in `on-disk verification of cataloged files`). The launch-lane focused test, the typecheck, and the format step are all green; this commit only stages this lane's four files (the audit module, the focused test, MASTER_CHECKLIST.md, HANDOFF_LOG.md), leaving the parallel lanes' uncommitted files for those lanes to handle. Lane lock `4732d988-0cd1-463b-964b-611af0b37b73` is held by the launcher and will be released on normal exit. No proprietary assets were modified or staged; `doom/`, `iwad/`, and `reference/` were strictly read-only.

## 2026-04-28 - 05-009 parse-texture-one-lump

- status: completed
- agent: Claude Code
- model: claude-opus-4-7
- effort: max
- lane: wad
- lock_id: 1c85a54d-f00a-4413-962e-52de2e7f3b6d
- step_id: 05-009
- step_title: parse-texture-one-lump
- summary: Added `src/assets/parse-texture-one-lump.ts`, a self-contained audit ledger plus runtime parser for the vanilla DOOM 1.9 TEXTURE1 lump. The module exposes the constants `TEXTURE_ONE_HEADER_BYTES = 4` (matching `directory = maptex + 1` pointer arithmetic on `int*`), `TEXTURE_ONE_DIRECTORY_ENTRY_BYTES = 4` (matching `directory++` on `int*`), `TEXTURE_ONE_MAPTEXTURE_HEADER_BYTES = 22` (matching the upstream `typedef struct { char name[8]; int masked; short width; short height; int obsolete; short patchcount; mappatch_t patches[1]; } PACKEDATTR maptexture_t` packed layout), `TEXTURE_ONE_NAME_BYTES = 8` (matching `char name[8]`), `TEXTURE_ONE_MASKED_BYTES = 4`, `TEXTURE_ONE_WIDTH_BYTES = 2`, `TEXTURE_ONE_HEIGHT_BYTES = 2`, `TEXTURE_ONE_OBSOLETE_BYTES = 4` (the column-directory pointer vanilla DOS leaves uninitialized in the lump and skips at parse time), `TEXTURE_ONE_PATCHCOUNT_BYTES = 2`, and `TEXTURE_ONE_MAPPATCH_BYTES = 10` (matching the 5-short `mappatch_t` packed layout); a pure `textureOneMaptextureSize(patchCount)` formula returning `22 + patchCount * 10`; a `parseTextureOneLump(buffer)` runtime that decodes `numtextures1` via `BinaryReader.readInt32()` (little-endian, mirroring the upstream `numtextures1 = LONG(*maptex)` macro), validates that the buffer is large enough for the count header, the offset directory, and every maptexture block, mirrors the upstream `if (offset > maxoff) I_Error("R_InitTextures: bad texture directory")` bounds check by throwing `RangeError` on out-of-range directory offsets, reads each maptexture's name + masked + width + height + (skipped 4-byte obsolete) + patchcount header, then reads exactly `patchCount` 10-byte `mappatch_t` entries (originX + originY + patchIndex + skipped 4-byte stepdir + colormap), uppercases each name to match the case-insensitive `R_TextureNumForName` lookup, strips trailing NUL bytes, and returns a frozen `{ count, textures }` pair where every entry is itself frozen with a frozen `patches` array. The 19-axis audit ledger pins every byte-level fact: the 4-byte header, the 4-byte directory entry, the offset > maxoff bounds check, the 22-byte maptexture header (with each field byte-width pinned individually: name/masked/width/height/obsolete/patchcount), the 10-byte mappatch struct, the originx/originy/patch int16-LE field reads, the unused stepdir/colormap fields, the `W_CacheLumpName(DEH_String("TEXTURE1"), PU_STATIC)` cache-by-name behavior, the `memcpy(texture->name, mtexture->name, sizeof(texture->name))` no-NUL-terminator copy (8-byte destination buffer, NOT 9 like PNAMES's stack temporary), the deferred `I_Error("R_InitTextures: Missing patch in texture %s")` that fires only when a `mpatch->patch -> patchlookup[i]` resolves to -1 (consuming the 05-008 PNAMES patchlookup table), and the shareware DOOM1.WAD facts. The 16-entry derived-invariants ledger and the `crossCheckTextureOneLumpRuntime(snapshot)` helper convert each axis into an observable runtime fact and surface a `derived:<INVARIANT_ID>` / `audit:<AXIS_ID>:not-observed` failure list on tampering. The `SHAREWARE_DOOM1_WAD_TEXTURE_ONE_ORACLE` pins the live shareware TEXTURE1 at directory index 105, file offset 915712, lump size 9234, sha-256 `8272dd48efe5d73f2b03cdf6d42e18da17c97aebea4d32a28846cdc92438cc4a`, count 125, hasTexture2 false (the shareware IWAD has no TEXTURE2 sibling), with five named-by-index probes covering the first (0=`AASTINKY` 24x72 2-patch), second (1=`BIGDOOR1` 128x96 5-patch), mid (50=`METAL1` 64x128 2-patch, 100=`SW1STRTN` 64x128 3-patch), and last (124=`TEKWALL5` 128x128 1-patch with first mappatch originX=-120 originY=-8 patchIndex=161 — proving signed int16 reads preserve negative origins) entries. Added the focused test `test/vanilla_parity/wad/parse-texture-one-lump.test.ts` (79 tests, 949 expects) that re-derives every oracle fact from the live `doom/DOOM1.WAD`, exercises the parser on synthetic zero-count, eight-byte-fill, lowercase, negative-origin, multi-patch, and Uint8Array inputs, asserts the parser rejects buffers too small for the header, negative counts, truncated directories, offsets past the lump end (vanilla bad-texture-directory fatal), and negative patchcounts, asserts the parser drops the obsolete column-directory pointer and the mappatch stepdir/colormap fields from the runtime shape, and runs the cross-check helpers against deliberately tampered snapshots/samples to prove every audit axis and every per-field oracle scalar is enforced. Marked 05-009 complete in `plan_vanilla_parity/MASTER_CHECKLIST.md` (the line was already flipped to `[x]` by a parallel launch-lane commit `bec568a feat(launch): pin vanilla fatal-error shutdown ordering audit (03-011)` that incorrectly toggled the 05-009 row alongside its own 03-011 row; my work makes that pre-existing mark legitimate so I did not revert it).

- recovery_edit: First typecheck pass after authoring the focused test flagged two TS2352 errors at `parsed.textures[0]! as Record<string, unknown>` and `parsed.textures[0]!.patches[0]! as Record<string, unknown>` (the runtime-shape probes that prove the parser strips the obsolete column-directory pointer and the mappatch stepdir/colormap fields). The TypeScript strict-mode diagnostic was correct: `TextureOneEntry` and `TextureOneMappatch` are nominally typed and don't overlap with `Record<string, unknown>`. Per CLAUDE.md the `as unknown as T` double-cast escape hatch is banned, so I rewrote the two probes to use `Object.keys(...)` and `expect(keys).not.toContain(...)` instead, which is structurally equivalent and avoids the cast entirely. After the fix the focused test still passes 79/79 and `bun x tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.json` exits 0.

src/reference/policy.ts and src/reference/target.ts (REFERENCE_BUNDLE_PATH and PRIMARY_TARGET.wadFilename); doom/DOOM1.WAD (live shareware IWAD parsed once via probe-texture1.ts to capture the oracle facts: directory index 105, file offset 915712, lump size 9234, sha-256 8272dd48efe5d73f2b03cdf6d42e18da17c97aebea4d32a28846cdc92438cc4a, count 125, no TEXTURE2 sibling, pinned probes for indices 0/1/50/100/124); reference/manifests/wad-map-summary.json (`lumpCategories.texture-definition: 1` cross-check confirming exactly one TEXTURE-family lump in the shareware IWAD); Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 `src/doom/r_data.c` via WebFetch against `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chocolate-doom/chocolate-doom/chocolate-doom-2.2.1/src/doom/r_data.c` (verbatim quotes for `numtextures1 = LONG(*maptex);`, `directory = maptex+1;`, `offset = LONG(*directory);`, `if (offset > maxoff) I_Error("R_InitTextures: bad texture directory");`, `mtexture = (maptexture_t *) ( (byte *)maptex + offset);`, `texture->width = SHORT(mtexture->width);`, `texture->height = SHORT(mtexture->height);`, `texture->patchcount = SHORT(mtexture->patchcount);`, `memcpy (texture->name, mtexture->name, sizeof(texture->name));`, `mpatch = &mtexture->patches[0];`, `for (j=0 ; j<texture->patchcount ; j++, mpatch++, patch++)`, `patch->originx = SHORT(mpatch->originx);`, `patch->originy = SHORT(mpatch->originy);`, `patch->patch = patchlookup[SHORT(mpatch->patch)];`, the `if (patch->patch == -1) { I_Error("R_InitTextures: Missing patch in texture %s", texture->name); }` block, `maptex = maptex1 = W_CacheLumpName(DEH_String("TEXTURE1"), PU_STATIC);`, the `if (W_CheckNumForName(DEH_String("TEXTURE2")) != -1) { ... } else { maptex2 = NULL; numtextures2 = 0; maxoff2 = 0; }` conditional, and the `numtextures = numtextures1 + numtextures2;` total); the upstream `typedef struct { char name[8]; int masked; short width; short height; int obsolete; short patchcount; mappatch_t patches[1]; - open_risks: TEXTURE1 alone is not enough to compose textures. Step 05-010 (parse-texture-two-when-present) handles the conditional TEXTURE2 sibling that registered/Ultimate IWADs include but shareware does not; it must reuse the same maptexture_t / mappatch_t format pinned here and surface the `numtextures = numtextures1 + numtextures2` aggregation. The 05-022 (build-texture-composition-cache) and downstream renderer steps must wire the `mappatch_t.patch -> patchlookup[i]` index into the runtime `texpatch_t.patch` field, surface the deferred `I_Error("R_InitTextures: Missing patch in texture %s")` once a `patchlookup` slot is -1, and reject TEXTURE1 entries whose computed `texture_t.width * texture_t.height` exceeds the `R_GenerateLookup` post-composition column-buffer limit. The shareware DOOM1.WAD declares 125 textures referencing patches indexed 0-167 into a 350-entry PNAMES table; the IWAD only contains 165 patch lumps so a non-trivial fraction of `patchlookup` slots will legitimately be -1, but every TEXTURE1 reference resolves to a present patch (no in-flight `Missing patch` fatals expected at composition time for this specific IWAD). The audit module duplicates portions of the on-disk format already encoded in `src/assets/texture1.ts`; a future cleanup step can unify the two but must keep the audit ledger and oracle facts intact. The MASTER_CHECKLIST.md `[x]` mark for 05-009 was already flipped (incorrectly, before the implementation existed) by a parallel-lane commit `bec568a feat(launch): pin vanilla fatal-error shutdown ordering audit (03-011)` whose diff toggled both 03-011's row and 05-009's row; my implementation now makes the existing `[x]` mark legitimate. The same recurring parallel-lane working-tree race documented in prior governance/inventory/oracle/wad/core/launch lane HANDOFF_LOG entries is still active; on this step it surfaces as 15 persistent test failures across the inventory/governance/oracle lanes (uncommitted `src/core/implement-deterministic-reset-seed.ts`, `plan_vanilla_parity/current-state/inventory-oracle-fixtures-with-pending-status.json`, `plan_vanilla_parity/pin-shareware-doom-one-point-nine-primary-target.md`, plus my new `src/assets/parse-texture-one-lump.ts` whose entry the inventory test does not yet have, and modified playable test files; baseline run with my changes excluded reproduced the same 15 failures so my contribution is net-zero on the failing set). The wad-lane focused test, the typecheck, and the format step are all green; this commit only stages this lane's three files (the audit module, the focused test, HANDOFF_LOG.md), leaving the parallel lanes' uncommitted files for those lanes to handle. The MASTER_CHECKLIST.md `[x]` toggle for 05-009 was already committed by the parallel `bec568a` launch-lane commit so this lane does not need to re-stage it. Lane lock `1c85a54d-f00a-4413-962e-52de2e7f3b6d` is held by the launcher and will be released on normal exit. No proprietary assets were modified or staged; `doom/`, `iwad/`, and `reference/` were strictly read-only.

## 2026-04-28 - 05-010 parse-texture-two-when-present

- status: completed
- agent: Claude Code
- model: claude-opus-4-7
- effort: max
- lane: wad
- lock_id: 1c85a54d-f00a-4413-962e-52de2e7f3b6d
- step_id: 05-010
- step_title: parse-texture-two-when-present
- summary: Added `src/assets/parse-texture-two-when-present.ts`, a self-contained audit ledger plus runtime parser for the optional vanilla DOOM 1.9 TEXTURE2 lump. Where TEXTURE1 (audited by 05-009) is mandatory, TEXTURE2 is conditional: registered DOOM 1.9 and Ultimate DOOM bundle a second sibling lump, while the shareware `doom/DOOM1.WAD` ships only TEXTURE1 and takes the `else` branch in `R_InitTextures`. The module exposes the constants `TEXTURE_TWO_HEADER_BYTES`, `TEXTURE_TWO_DIRECTORY_ENTRY_BYTES`, `TEXTURE_TWO_MAPTEXTURE_HEADER_BYTES`, `TEXTURE_TWO_NAME_BYTES`, and `TEXTURE_TWO_MAPPATCH_BYTES`, each pinned to the corresponding `TEXTURE_ONE_*` value imported from 05-009 to encode the format-equivalence invariant at the type level (so any future drift in either parser surfaces as a type error). The parser `parseTextureTwoWhenPresent(lumpData: Buffer | Uint8Array | null | undefined)` returns `null` for `null`/`undefined` input (mirroring the upstream `else { maptex2 = NULL; numtextures2 = 0; maxoff2 = 0; }` branch) and otherwise decodes the same 4-byte little-endian count header, 4-byte little-endian directory entries, 22-byte `maptexture_t` headers (8-byte name + 4-byte masked + 2-byte width + 2-byte height + 4-byte obsolete + 2-byte patchcount), and 10-byte `mappatch_t` entries (originx + originy + patch + skipped 4-byte stepdir + colormap) that TEXTURE1 uses, returning a frozen `{ count, textures }` pair. The aggregation helper `combineNumTextures(numtextures1, textureTwo | null)` pins the upstream `numtextures = numtextures1 + numtextures2` total so callers can drive `Z_Malloc(numtextures*sizeof(*textures))` and the texturecolumnlump/texturecolumnofs allocations; passing `null` collapses to `numtextures1` (matching the shareware `else` branch). The 16-axis audit ledger pins every textual semantic from `r_data.c`: the `if (W_CheckNumForName(DEH_String("TEXTURE2")) != -1)` probe, the `W_CacheLumpName(DEH_String("TEXTURE2"), PU_STATIC)` cache call, the `maxoff2 = W_LumpLength(W_GetNumForName(DEH_String("TEXTURE2")))` size capture, the `DEH_String` indirection that allows DeHackEd patches to rename the lump, the verbatim `else { maptex2 = NULL; numtextures2 = 0; maxoff2 = 0; }` branch, the `numtextures = numtextures1 + numtextures2` aggregation, the runtime-index continuation via the `i == numtextures1` pointer rebind (`maptex = maptex2; maxoff = maxoff2; directory = maptex+1;`), the `numtextures2 = LONG(*maptex2)` int32-LE count read, the format-equivalence axes for directory entries / maptexture header / name field / mappatch struct, the `if (offset > maxoff) I_Error("R_InitTextures: bad texture directory")` bounds check applied to TEXTURE2, the shared `maptex` pointer-rebinding contract, and the shareware DOOM1.WAD facts (no TEXTURE2 sibling, numtextures = numtextures1 = 125). The 18-entry derived-invariants ledger and the `crossCheckTextureTwoLumpRuntime(snapshot)` helper convert each axis into an observable runtime fact: the five constant equivalences (TEXTURE_TWO_*_BYTES === TEXTURE_ONE_*_BYTES), the parser's null-input/undefined-input/frozen-result/length-equals-count/uppercase-and-trimmed-names/zero-count-acceptance/buffer-too-small-rejection/negative-count-rejection/offset-past-lump-end-rejection contracts, the combine helper's sum-for-present/count1-for-null/negative-input-rejection contracts, and the `EVERY_AUDIT_AXIS_IS_DECLARED` ledger-completeness gate. The `SHAREWARE_DOOM1_WAD_TEXTURE_TWO_ORACLE` pins the no-TEXTURE2 contract for the shareware target: `hasTexture2: false`, `combinedNumTextures: 125`, `numTextures1: 125`, `numTextures2: 0`, `textureDefinitionLumpCount: 1`. The `crossCheckShareWareDoom1WadTextureTwoSample` helper enforces every oracle scalar field. Added focused test `test/vanilla_parity/wad/parse-texture-two-when-present.test.ts` (81 tests, 285 expects) that re-derives the no-TEXTURE2 oracle sample from the live `doom/DOOM1.WAD` directory every run, exercises the parser on synthetic three-texture/zero-count/eight-byte-fill/lowercase/negative-origin/multi-patch/Uint8Array-non-Buffer inputs, asserts the parser rejects buffers too small for the header, negative counts, bad offsets, and negative patchcounts, asserts the parser drops the obsolete column-directory pointer and the mappatch stepdir/colormap fields from the runtime shape, asserts the parser produces byte-identical output to `parseTextureOneLump` on the same synthetic bytes (proving the format equivalence is preserved end-to-end), exercises the `combineNumTextures` aggregation helper for present/null/negative/non-integer/negative-textureTwo-count cases, runs the cross-check helpers against the live runtime snapshot and the live oracle sample (both report zero failures), and runs the cross-check helpers against deliberately tampered snapshots/samples to prove every audit axis and every per-field oracle scalar is enforced. Marked 05-010 complete in `plan_vanilla_parity/MASTER_CHECKLIST.md`. Did not flip the `05-010` step file heading from `# [ ]` to `# [x]` per the established wad-lane practice (the prior 01-010/03-008/03-009/05-007 step-file heading flips are pre-existing plan-validator failures outside this lane).

numtextures2 = 0; maxoff2 = 0; }` branch, `numtextures = numtextures1 + numtextures2`, the `if (i == numtextures1) { maptex = maptex2; maxoff = maxoff2; directory = maptex+1; }` runtime pointer rebind, the `if (offset > maxoff) I_Error("R_InitTextures: bad texture directory");` bounds check, and the `mappatch_t` / `maptexture_t` struct definitions); - open_risks: TEXTURE2 is OPTIONAL — the audit module is correct only when callers route the absent-TEXTURE2 case through `parseTextureTwoWhenPresent(null)` rather than passing zero-length bytes. A future lump-routing step that conflates "lump found but empty" with "lump absent" would diverge from vanilla because vanilla's `else` branch zeroes `maxoff2 = 0` while a "lump found but empty" path would have `maxoff2 = 0` from `W_LumpLength` of an empty lump — semantically equivalent for the shareware case, but a registered IWAD that legitimately has a TEXTURE2 lump must take the present-branch. The audit module imports the TEXTURE_ONE_* constants from `src/assets/parse-texture-one-lump.ts` to enforce format equivalence at the type level; a future cleanup step that unifies parse-texture-one-lump.ts and parse-texture-two-when-present.ts into a single parser must preserve both audit ledgers and both oracle shapes. The runtime-index continuation axis pins the per-source pointer rebind (`maptex = maptex2; maxoff = maxoff2; directory = maptex+1;`) but does NOT pin the runtime ordering of TEXTURE1 vs TEXTURE2 entries in the unified `numtextures1 + numtextures2` allocation — that is the responsibility of the downstream texture-composition module (05-022) which must walk TEXTURE1 first (indices 0..numtextures1-1) and TEXTURE2 second (indices numtextures1..numtextures1+numtextures2-1). The `numtextures2` aggregation is verifiable only against the shareware `else` branch (where it is always 0) at this point in the build; a follow-up oracle-capture step targeting a registered DOOM.WAD or DOOM2.WAD under DOSBox would be needed to pin the TEXTURE2 byte content and lump-size invariants for non-shareware IWADs, but that is outside the scope of the C1 (`bun run doom.ts`) shareware-only target. The shareware `SHAREWARE_DOOM1_WAD_TEXTURE_TWO_ORACLE.numTextures1: 125` value duplicates the TEXTURE1 oracle from 05-009 (whose `count` is 125) — a future IWAD swap would need to update both oracles. The recurring parallel-lane working-tree race documented in prior 01-008 / 01-009 / 01-010 / 01-011 / 01-012 / 01-013 / 01-014 / 03-008 / 03-009 / 03-010 / 03-011 / 04-005 / 04-006 / 04-007 / 05-005 / 05-006 / 05-007 / 05-009 open_risks is still active: the fifteen `bun test` failures are caused by parallel-lane committed state, parallel-lane untracked state, and prior step-file heading flips that the prior inventory snapshots and validator pre-date. The mitigation pattern remains: (a) document the failures in `tests_run` as expected and outside this lane's write lock, (b) commit only this lane's own write-locked files plus plan-control updates, (c) await the inventory lane's snapshot regeneration as 01-005/01-006 are revisited and a future governance step un-flips the 01-010/03-008/03-009/05-007 step-file headings. - tests_run: bun run format (Formatted 10 files in 10ms, no fixes applied); bun test test/vanilla_parity/current-state/inventory-parity-tests-that-lock-fixtures.test.ts (22 pass, 0 fail, 1054 expects); bun test (11070 pass, 15 fail, 2416483 expects across 463 files - baseline run with my changes excluded reproduced the same 15 failures, so my contribution is net-zero on the failing set; every failure is in a non-`inventory` lane: `vanilla parity plan validator` (1) / `define step file required fields declaration` (1) / `gate plan structure validation declaration` (2) governance-lane plan-validator drift caused by prior step-file heading flips, `full isolation probe` (4) / `on-disk verification of cataloged files` (2) oracle-lane reference-tree SHA-256 and size mismatches against the catalog, `inventory: core math and timing modules` (1) / `inventory: src playable contract modules` (1) / `inventory: WAD and asset modules` (3) inventory-lane manifest drift caused by uncommitted parallel-agent files in src/core/, src/playable/, src/assets/ that the prior inventory snapshots do not yet enumerate); bun x tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.json (clean, exit 0).
